univ_mit_yasheng_huang.gold: Yasheng Huang is the Epoch Foundation Professor of Global Economics and Management at MIT Sloan. He founded the MIT India Lab and China Lab, researching comparative development and political economy. His notable books include *Capitalism with Chinese Characteristics* and *The Rise and Fall of the EAST*. He previously taught at Harvard Business School.

univ_mit_victor_costan.gold: Victor Costan is a researcher at MIT’s Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL) within the Computation Structures Group. He co-authored the influential "Intel SGX Explained" and developed Sanctum, a system providing strong software isolation through minimal hardware extensions. His research focuses on secure processor architectures, trusted execution environments, and hardware-based security verification.

univ_mit_jeffrey_goldstone.gold: Jeffrey Goldstone is an Emeritus Professor of Physics at MIT, renowned for proving Goldstone’s theorem and discovering Nambu-Goldstone bosons. He established rigorous diagrammatic methods for many-body problems and co-developed adiabatic quantum computation. A Fellow of the Royal Society, his honors include the Dirac Medal and the Dannie Heineman Prize for Mathematical Physics.

univ_mit_alan_edelman.gold: Alan Edelman is a Professor of Applied Mathematics at MIT and a Principal Investigator at CSAIL. He is a co-creator of the Julia programming language and leads the MIT Julia Lab. Recognized for breakthroughs in random matrix theory, he received the IEEE Sidney Fernbach Award and the James H. Wilkinson Prize for Numerical Software.

univ_mit_kenneth_a_oye.gold: Kenneth A. Oye is an MIT professor of Political Science and Data Systems and Society, directing the Program on Emerging Technologies. Formerly director of the Center for International Studies, he authored the seminal *Cooperation under Anarchy*. He holds a Harvard PhD and is a World Peace Foundation trustee and Council on Foreign Relations member.

univ_mit_jeffrey_i_steinfeld.gold: Jeffrey I. Steinfeld is a Professor Emeritus of Chemistry at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology. He earned his PhD from Harvard in 1965. His research transitioned from molecular spectroscopy to atmospheric chemistry and aerosols. He authored *Molecules and Radiation*, co-authored *Chemical Kinetics and Dynamics*, and serves on the MIT Energy Education Task Force.

univ_mit_krishna_rajagopal.gold: Krishna Rajagopal is the William A. M. Burden Professor of Physics at MIT. He researches quark-gluon plasma and the QCD phase diagram. He previously served as Chair of the MIT Faculty and Dean for Digital Learning. A MacVicar Faculty Fellow and APS Fellow, he co-authored "Color superconductivity in dense quark matter."

univ_mit_thomas_l_magnanti.gold: Thomas L. Magnanti is an MIT Institute Professor and former Dean of the School of Engineering. A leader in large-scale optimization and transportation planning, he co-authored the seminal textbook *Network Flows*. He was the founding president of the Singapore University of Technology and Design and is a member of the National Academy of Engineering.

univ_mit_michael_j_piore.gold: Michael J. Piore is the David W. Skinner Professor of Political Economy, Emeritus, at MIT. A 1984 MacArthur Fellow with a PhD from Harvard, he is renowned for developing the dual labor market hypothesis. His influential books include *Birds of Passage*, *Internal Labor Markets and Manpower Analysis*, and *The Second Industrial Divide*.

univ_mit_susan_whitfield_gabrieli.gold: Susan Whitfield-Gabrieli is the Tommy Fuss Endowed Chair in Precision Psychiatry at Mass General Brigham and a Research Affiliate at MIT’s McGovern Institute. She co-developed the CONN toolbox, a leading software for functional connectivity analysis. Her research employs real-time fMRI neurofeedback and multimodal imaging to develop personalized biomarkers for psychiatric and neurodevelopmental disorders.

univ_mit_heidi_nepf.gold: Heidi Nepf is the Donald and Martha Harleman Professor at MIT, specializing in environmental fluid mechanics. She is internationally recognized for modeling how aquatic vegetation influences flow, sediment transport, and nutrient flux. A 2018 AGU Fellow, she received the 2023 IAHR M. Selim Yalin Lifetime Achievement Award and 2019 ASCE Hunter Rouse Award.

univ_mit_rub_n_juanes.gold: Rubén Juanes is a Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering and Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences at MIT, where he directs the Henry L. Pierce Laboratory. He holds a PhD from UC Berkeley, specializing in multiphase flow, geomechanics, and induced seismicity. A Fellow of the APS and AGU, he received the DOE Early Career Award.

univ_mit_maxim_imakaev.gold: Maxim Imakaev is a computational biologist at the Mirny Lab. He co-authored the foundational 2009 *Science* paper introducing Hi-C and developed the iterative correction method for Hi-C data analysis. His research focuses on loop extrusion and 3D genome architecture, and he contributed to the development of genomic tools including Bioframe and Cooltools.

univ_mit_danny_fox.gold: Danny Fox is the Anshen-Chomsky Professor of Language and Thought and Head of the Department of Linguistics and Philosophy at MIT. A former Harvard Junior Fellow, he authored *Economy and Semantic Interpretation*. His research explores the syntax-semantics interface, specifically the copy theory of movement, exhaustification, and the formal properties of scalar implicatures.

univ_mit_j_taylor_perron.gold: J. Taylor Perron is the Cecil and Ida Green Professor at MIT and a 2021 MacArthur Fellow. A geomorphologist, he investigates how climate and geology shape landscapes on Earth, Mars, and Titan. His research includes reviving the Mars ocean hypothesis, analyzing Titan's methane rivers, and receiving the American Geophysical Union’s James B. Macelwane Medal.

univ_mit_adam_c_martin.gold: Adam C. Martin is the Salvador E. Luria Professor of Biology at MIT. He discovered "contractile pulsing," a mechanism where the actin cytoskeleton generates force to drive tissue morphogenesis. His research utilizes Drosophila and mouse models to investigate how molecular mechanisms and mechanical forces sculpt tissues during embryonic development, gastrulation, and neural tube closure.

univ_mit_hazhir_rahmandad.gold: Hazhir Rahmandad is the Schussel Family Professor of Management Science and Professor of System Dynamics at the MIT Sloan School of Management. He applies system dynamics to epidemiological modeling, focusing on COVID-19 underreporting and behavioral responses. His research, published in Health Affairs and The Lancet Planetary Health, examines how culture and responsiveness influence pandemic outcomes.

univ_mit_haruhiko_asada.gold: Haruhiko "Harry" Asada is the Ford Professor of Engineering at MIT and Director of the d'Arbeloff Laboratory. He co-developed the Direct Drive Arm and pioneered Supernumerary Robotic Limbs. His research focuses on robotics, control systems, and bioengineering. He received the 2023 IEEE RAS Pioneer in Robotics and Automation Award and the ASME Rufus Oldenburger Medal.

univ_mit_b_c_burchfiel.gold: B. Clark Burchfiel was the Schlumberger Professor Emeritus of Geology at MIT. A National Academy of Sciences member, he received the 2009 Penrose Medal and served as president of the Geological Society of America. He pioneered research on the tectonic evolution of the Tibetan Plateau and identified Death Valley as a pull-apart basin.

univ_mit_annapurna_poduri.gold: Annapurna Poduri is the Deputy Director of the National Institute of Neurological Disorders and Stroke and Director of the Epilepsy Genetics Program at Boston Children’s Hospital. A Harvard Medical School professor and Broad Institute affiliate, she pioneered somatic mutation discovery in focal epilepsy and utilizes zebrafish models to investigate rare neurogenetic disorders.

univ_mit_polina_anikeeva.gold: Polina Anikeeva is the Head of MIT’s Department of Materials Science and Engineering and the Matoula S. Salapatas Professor. She directs the K. Lisa Yang Brain-Body Center, developing bioelectronic interfaces and magnetic nanomaterials. Her foundational research focused on colloidal quantum dot synthesis for optoelectronics. She received the NIH Pioneer Award and Vilcek Prize.

univ_mit_vikash_p_chauhan.gold: Vikash P. Chauhan is a Research Scientist at MIT’s Koch Institute, co-mentored by Robert Langer and Phillip Sharp. He earned his PhD from Harvard University. His research spans genome engineering and cancer nanomedicine, including serving as lead author of a 2025 Nature study on high-precision prime editors and investigating tumor vessel normalization for nanoparticle delivery.

univ_mit_david_c_page.gold: David C. Page is a Professor of Biology at MIT and was Director of the Whitehead Institute from 2004 to 2020. An HHMI Investigator and MacArthur Fellow, he is best known for mapping and sequencing the human Y chromosome. His research examines sex-biased gene expression and the genetic origins of male infertility and Turner’s syndrome.

univ_mit_james_utama_surjadi.gold: James Utama Surjadi is a Postdoctoral Associate at MIT researching cellular metamaterials and architected structures. He holds a PhD from City University of Hong Kong. Recipient of the 2026 MRS Postdoctoral Award and a Forbes 30 Under 30 Asia honoree, he joins the National University of Singapore as an Assistant Professor in 2026.

univ_mit_dennis_h_klatt.gold: A Senior Research Scientist at MIT, Klatt was a pioneer in computerized speech synthesis. He developed the Klatt synthesizer and KlattTalk, the basis for the commercial DECtalk system. He created the "Perfect Paul" voice, famously used by Stephen Hawking. In 1987, he received the ASA Silver Medal and the John Price Wetherill Medal.

univ_mit_young_s_song.gold: Young S. Song is a researcher at MIT’s Department of Mechanical Engineering. Affiliated with the Environmentally Benign Manufacturing group, he specializes in life cycle energy analysis and recycling of fiber-reinforced composites. He co-authored the 2009 study "Life cycle energy analysis of fiber-reinforced composites," evaluating the energy intensity and end-of-life sustainability of manufacturing processes.

univ_mit_evelina_fedorenko.gold: Evelina Fedorenko is an Associate Professor at MIT and an investigator at the McGovern Institute for Brain Research. She leads the Language and Brain Lab, specializing in the neural architecture of the human language system. A 2025 Troland Research Award recipient, she developed individualized fMRI localizers to demonstrate the functional dissociation between language and other cognitive systems.

univ_mit_bernd_zetsche.gold: Bernd Zetsche is a researcher at the Broad Institute and MIT’s McGovern Institute. He was the lead author of the 2015 *Cell* paper characterizing Cpf1 (Cas12a), a novel CRISPR-Cas system for human genome editing. His work also includes developing multiplex gene editing using Cpf1 and chemical control mechanisms for CRISPR-Cas9 technologies.

univ_mit_venkat_venkatasubramanian.gold: Venkat Venkatasubramanian is the Samuel Ruben-Peter G. Viele Professor of Engineering at Columbia University and a member of the National Academy of Engineering. A leading expert in process fault detection and diagnosis, he authored a seminal three-part review on the subject. He directs the Complex Resilient Intelligent Systems Laboratory and co-directs the Center for Systemic Risks.

univ_mit_chiang_c_mei.gold: Chiang C. Mei is Ford Professor of Engineering Emeritus at MIT. A member of the National Academy of Engineering, he is renowned for research in fluid mechanics and ocean wave dynamics. He authored the seminal *The Applied Dynamics of Ocean Surface Waves* and received the Theodore von Karman Medal and ASCE International Coastal Engineering Award.

univ_mit_nuno_loureiro.gold: Nuno Loureiro (1977–2025) was the Herman Feshbach Professor of Physics and Director of the MIT Plasma Science and Fusion Center. An APS Fellow specializing in magnetic reconnection, he received the 2015 Thomas H. Stix Award and a 2025 Presidential Early Career Award. He earned his PhD from Imperial College London in 2005.

univ_mit_rahul_mazumder.gold: Rahul Mazumder is the NTU Associate Professor of Operations Research and Statistics at MIT Sloan. He earned his PhD from Stanford University under Trevor Hastie. His research integrates mathematical optimization with sparse statistical learning. A recipient of the Leo Breiman Junior Award, he developed the softImpute R package and specializes in large-scale discrete optimization.

univ_mit_rory_p_o_shea.gold: Rory P. O’Shea is a Visiting Professor at the MIT Sloan School of Management and an Associate Professor at University College Dublin. He specializes in technology strategy and academic entrepreneurship, co-editing the book *Building Technology Transfer: An Entrepreneurial Approach* (2014). His research investigates the determinants of university spin-offs and the management of innovation.

univ_mit_michael_t_hemann.gold: Michael T. Hemann is a Professor of Biology at MIT and a member of the Koch Institute. He earned his Ph.D. from Johns Hopkins University in Carol Greider’s lab, demonstrating that the shortest telomeres trigger cellular senescence. His research utilizes high-throughput screening and mouse models to investigate the genetic mechanisms of cancer drug resistance.

univ_mit_peter_slattery.gold: Peter Slattery is a Research Scientist at MIT FutureTech within CSAIL. He leads the AI Risk Repository and AI Risk Index. A behavioral scientist, he authored "Research co-design in health" (2020). His research covers mental health, patient involvement, and AI safety; he co-founded Ready Research and led the SCRUB COVID-19 survey.

univ_mit_robert_e_laflamme.gold: Robert E. Laflamme is an environmental chemist and researcher at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology. Collaborating with Ronald A. Hites, he co-authored seminal studies on polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs), including "The Global Distribution of Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons in Recent Sediments" (1978). His research established the historical record of anthropogenic combustion as a primary source of sedimentary pollutants.

univ_mit_han_altae_tran.gold: Han Altae-Tran earned his PhD in Biological Engineering from MIT in 2023, advised by Feng Zhang. He pioneered "one-shot learning" for low-data drug discovery and co-developed programmable RNA-guided systems, including OMEGA and Fanzor. He also led the discovery of nearly 200 rare CRISPR-Cas systems using deep terascale clustering of genomic data.

univ_mit_roland_j_m_pellenq.gold: Roland J.‐M. Pellenq is a CNRS Director of Research and MIT Senior Research Scientist. He founded the MIT-CNRS joint laboratory MSE2 and developed the first realistic molecular model of cement hydrates. His work utilizes atomistic simulations of nanoporous materials to optimize concrete durability and reduce its carbon footprint, leading international research in Urban Physics.

univ_mit_tom_n_jagatic.gold: Tom N. Jagatic is a researcher at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology. He is best known for co-authoring the influential 2007 paper "Social phishing" in *Communications of the ACM*, which demonstrated how social network data significantly increases phishing success rates. His work also includes research on browser authentication and context-based phishing detection.

univ_mit_geoffrey_s_d_beach.gold: Geoffrey S. D. Beach is a Professor of Materials Science and Engineering at MIT and director of the Laboratory for Nanomagnetism and Spin Dynamics. He is recognized for pioneering research in spintronics, specifically the stabilization of room-temperature magnetic skyrmions and the development of hydrogen-gated thin films for energy-efficient memory and logic technologies.

univ_mit_erin_l_kelly.gold: Erin L. Kelly is the Sloan Distinguished Professor of Work and Organization Studies at MIT and Co-Director of the Institute for Work and Employment Research. She co-authored the award-winning book *Overload* (2020). Her research on workplace equity and wellbeing has received the Max Weber Award and the Ellen Galinsky Generative Researcher Award.

univ_mit_florian_kabinger.gold: Florian Kabinger is a researcher at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology and the Max Planck Institute for Multidisciplinary Sciences. He elucidated the molecular mechanism of the antiviral drug molnupiravir, demonstrating how it induces SARS-CoV-2 mutagenesis. His work utilizes cryo-electron microscopy to characterize viral RNA polymerase inhibition by nucleoside and non-nucleoside inhibitors like HeE1-2Tyr.

univ_mit_serta_karaman.gold: Sertaç Karaman is a Professor of Aeronautics and Astronautics at MIT and Director of the Laboratory for Information and Decision Systems. He co-developed the RRT* and PRM* algorithms, which established asymptotic optimality in sampling-based motion planning. He also co-founded the self-driving startup Optimus Ride and received the IEEE RAS Early Career Award.

univ_mit_roberto_rigob_n.gold: Roberto Rigobón is the Society of Sloan Fellows Professor of Applied Economics at the MIT Sloan School of Management. He co-founded the Billion Prices Project and PriceStats to modernize inflation measurement. He also directs the Aggregate Confusion Project, addressing ESG rating inconsistencies, and researches financial contagion and the propagation of economic shocks.

univ_mit_r_ahmad.gold: R. Ahmad is an academic researcher at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology. He has contributed to the Research Laboratory of Electronics (RLE), specifically within the Nanostructures and Computation Group. His work includes co-authoring research on quantitative characterization of higher-order mode converters in weakly multimoded fibers, published in journals such as Optics Express.

univ_mit_peter_belobaba.gold: Peter Belobaba is a Principal Research Scientist at MIT’s International Center for Air Transportation. He directs the PODS Revenue Management Research Consortium and manages the Global Airline Industry Program. A pioneer in airline seat inventory management, he co-edited *The Global Airline Industry* and received the 2006 INFORMS Revenue Management and Pricing Historical Prize.

univ_mit_terry_p_orlando.gold: A Professor of Electrical Engineering at MIT and principal investigator in the Research Laboratory of Electronics, Orlando co-authored the textbooks *Foundations of Applied Superconductivity* and *Applied Quantum and Statistical Mechanics*. His research focuses on superconducting circuits for quantum computation, particularly magnetic-flux-based qubits. He earned his Ph.D. in physics from Stanford University.

univ_mit_g_b_olson.gold: Gregory B. Olson is the Thermo-Calc Professor of the Practice at MIT and a pioneer of Integrated Computational Materials Engineering (ICME). Known as the "father of materials design," he founded the Steel Research Group and co-founded QuesTek Innovations. A National Academy of Engineering member, he developed the first computationally designed flight-qualified material, Ferrium S53.

univ_mit_amala_mahadevan.gold: Amala Mahadevan is a Senior Scientist at the Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution and faculty in the MIT-WHOI Joint Program. She developed the Process Study Ocean Model (PSOM) to investigate submesoscale ocean dynamics and the carbon cycle. A former Radcliffe Fellow, she also serves as Faculty Dean of Mather House at Harvard University.

univ_mit_jonathan_hwang.gold: Jonathan Hwang is a researcher at MIT’s Department of Materials Science and Engineering. He co-authored the seminal review "Perovskites in catalysis and electrocatalysis" in *Science*. His work focuses on leveraging the electronic structure of perovskite oxides to optimize oxygen electrocatalysis, CO2 reduction, and energy storage for sustainable energy conversion technologies.

univ_mit_mehrdad_jazayeri.gold: Mehrdad Jazayeri is a Professor of Brain and Cognitive Sciences at MIT and an Investigator at the McGovern Institute and HHMI. He directs the JazLab, researching the neural basis of timing, Bayesian integration, and music perception. A 2024 Vannevar Bush Fellow, he has received a Sloan Research Fellowship and a McKnight Scholar Award.

univ_mit_g_ricker.gold: George R. Ricker is a Senior Research Scientist at the MIT Kavli Institute and Director of its Detector Laboratory. He is the Principal Investigator for NASA’s Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite (TESS) and the HETE-2 mission. He also served as Deputy PI for the Chandra X-ray Observatory’s ACIS instrument and developed CCD detectors for space astronomy.

univ_mit_a_m_rao.gold: Abhinav Rao is a researcher who earned his PhD from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology in 2018. Affiliated with the MIT Mechanosynthesis Group, he specialized in the mechanics and manufacturing of carbon nanotube-polymer composites and yarns. His work includes developing in situ interfacial polymerization techniques and investigating load transfer mechanisms in vertically aligned nanotube forests.

univ_mit_r_l_jaffe.gold: Robert L. Jaffe is the Jane and Otto Morningstar Professor of Physics and former director of the Center for Theoretical Physics. He co-invented the MIT Bag Model of quark confinement and formulated the Ellis-Jaffe sum rule for nucleon spin. His research also encompasses exotic hadrons, transversity, and the physics of energy.

univ_mit_richard_p_binzel.gold: Richard P. Binzel is a Professor of Planetary Sciences at MIT who invented the Torino Scale for asteroid impact hazards. He led the Small Main-Belt Asteroid Spectroscopic Survey (SMASS) and served as a co-investigator for NASA’s New Horizons and OSIRIS-REx missions. A Urey Prize recipient, he has asteroid 2873 Binzel named in his honor.

univ_mit_alar_toomre.gold: A 1984 MacArthur Fellow and MIT Professor of Applied Mathematics, Toomre developed the Toomre stability criterion for galactic disks. He pioneered computer simulations of galaxy collisions, establishing the "Toomre sequence," and co-authored influential research on polar wander and the dynamics of the Antennae Galaxies. He received the 1993 Dirk Brouwer Award for his contributions to dynamics.

univ_mit_hamlin_m_jennings.gold: Hamlin M. Jennings (1946–2015) was the inaugural executive director of the MIT Concrete Sustainability Hub. He developed the first quantitative model of the calcium silicate hydrate (C-S-H) nanostructure. Previously, he chaired Northwestern University’s Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering and co-founded the Center for Advanced Cement-based Materials. He held a PhD from Brown University.

univ_mit_catherine_d_ignazio.gold: Catherine D’Ignazio is an Associate Professor of Urban Science and Planning at MIT and Director of the Data + Feminism Lab. She co-authored *Data Feminism* (2020) and wrote *Counting Feminicide* (2024). Her research integrates intersectional feminism with data science to address social justice, data literacy, and the ethical impacts of automated systems.

univ_mit_monica_diez_silva.gold: Monica Diez-Silva is a Research Scientist in MIT’s Department of Materials Science and Engineering. She specializes in the biomechanics of red blood cells, focusing on malaria and sickle cell disease. Her notable research includes refractive index mapping of parasitized cells and identifying the RESA protein’s role in membrane stiffness and impaired microcirculation.

univ_mit_qing_hu.gold: Qing Hu is a Distinguished Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology. He is a pioneer in terahertz (THz) science, specifically for developing high-performance terahertz quantum-cascade lasers (QCLs). His research enables advanced imaging and sensing technologies with significant applications in medical diagnostics and information and cyber security.

univ_mit_cynthia_breazeal.gold: Cynthia Breazeal is a Professor of Media Arts and Sciences at MIT and Dean for Digital Learning. A pioneer in social robotics, she founded the Personal Robots Group at the Media Lab and created Kismet and Leonardo. She co-founded Jibo, Inc., authored *Designing Sociable Robots*, and directs the MIT RAISE initiative.

univ_mit_leonid_levitov.gold: Leonid Levitov is an MIT Physics Professor specializing in condensed matter theory. He pioneered quantum noise theory and counting statistics, predicting "levitons"—noise-free coherent current pulses. A pioneer in quasicrystal theory, he co-proposed superconducting persistent current qubits and has extensively researched graphene’s electronic properties, including viscous electron fluids and atomic collapse.

univ_mit_neville_hogan.gold: Neville Hogan is the Sun Jae Professor of Mechanical Engineering and Brain and Cognitive Sciences at MIT. He pioneered impedance control, a foundational theory for robot manipulation and physical interaction. Regarded as the father of rehabilitation robotics, he directs the Newman Laboratory and received the 2021 IEEE Pioneer in Robotics and Automation Award.

univ_mit_simon_gustavsson.gold: A Principal Research Scientist at MIT’s Engineering Quantum Systems group, Gustavsson specializes in superconducting qubits and time-resolved electron transport. He earned his Ph.D. from ETH Zurich and M.S. from Chalmers University. He founded Labber and Atlantic Quantum, and received the Newcomb Cleveland Prize and Physics World’s 2020 Breakthrough of the Year for his research.

univ_mit_ca_ross.gold: Caroline Anne Ross is the Ford Professor of Materials Science and Engineering at MIT and former interim department head. She specializes in multiferroic oxides and the self-assembly of block copolymers for nanolithography. A Fellow of the APS, IEEE, and MRS, she previously developed hard disk technology at Komag Inc. and holds over 20 patents.

univ_mit_andrew_bosson.gold: Andrew Bosson is a biologist who earned his PhD from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology. Affiliated with the Koch Institute for Integrative Cancer Research, he specialized in microRNA regulatory networks and disease. He co-authored the 2016 *Cell Reports* study on multi-omic microRNA regulation and conducted further research at the NYU School of Medicine.

univ_mit_pavel_etingof.gold: Pavel Etingof is a Professor of Mathematics at MIT specializing in representation theory and mathematical physics. A Fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, he co-founded the MIT PRIMES program. He served as editor-in-chief of the Journal of the AMS and authored foundational texts on tensor categories and quantum groups.

univ_mit_laura_l_kiessling.gold: Laura L. Kiessling is the Novartis Professor of Chemistry at MIT and a member of the Broad Institute. She researches multivalent protein-carbohydrate interactions and cell surface glycans. A MacArthur Fellow and member of the National Academy of Sciences, she is the founding Editor-in-Chief of ACS Chemical Biology and received the 2018 Tetrahedron Prize.

univ_mit_myriam_heiman.gold: Myriam Heiman is MIT’s John and Dorothy Wilson Professor of Neuroscience and Director of the Picower Institute. She co-invented the Translating Ribosome Affinity Purification (TRAP) technique for cell-type-specific mRNA translation profiling. Her research identifies molecular drivers of neuronal vulnerability in neurodegenerative diseases like Huntington’s and Parkinson’s, supported by an NIH Director’s Transformative Research Award.

univ_mit_barbara_ruskin.gold: Barbara Ruskin is a molecular biologist who co-discovered the lariat structure of introns during pre-mRNA splicing. Her foundational research, published in *Cell* and *Science*, identified the 2',5'-phosphodiester bond in splicing intermediates and characterized lariat debranching activity. She conducted postdoctoral research at the Whitehead Institute (MIT) and earned her PhD from Harvard University.

univ_mit_frederick_a_frey.gold: Frederick A. Frey (1938–2021) was a Professor Emeritus of Geochemistry at MIT. He pioneered radiochemical and instrumental neutron activation analysis to model trace element behavior in the Earth's mantle. Recipient of the AGU Bowen Award and GSA Distinguished Geologic Career Award, he served as president of the AGU Volcanology, Geochemistry and Petrology Section.

univ_mit_george_zweig.gold: George Zweig is an American physicist who independently proposed the existence of quarks, which he called "aces," in 1964. A former student of Richard Feynman at Caltech, he formulated the OZI rule and later transitioned into neurobiology. He is a researcher at MIT specializing in particle interactions and a National Academy of Sciences member.

univ_mit_james_m_utterback.gold: James M. Utterback was the David J. McGrath jr Professor of Management and Innovation, Emeritus at MIT Sloan. He co-developed the Abernathy-Utterback model and the "dominant design" concept. He authored the seminal book *Mastering the Dynamics of Innovation* and was a Fellow of the AAAS and the Royal Swedish Academy of Engineering Sciences.

univ_mit_samar_burney.gold: Samar Burney is a researcher who investigated nitric oxide and endothelin effects at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology. A member of Steven R. Tannenbaum’s laboratory, she co-authored "The chemistry of DNA damage from nitric oxide and peroxynitrite." She later served as a Scientific Program Manager for the Juvenile Diabetes Foundation International.

univ_mit_bradford_h_hager.gold: Bradford H. Hager is the Cecil and Ida Green Professor of Earth Sciences at MIT and former director of the Earth Resources Laboratory. A pioneer in mantle dynamics, he first related seismic tomography to Earth’s geoid and plate motions. His honors include the AGU Lehmann Medal and the EGU Augustus Love Medal.

univ_mit_mohammad_a_alkhadra.gold: Mohammad A. Alkhadra co-founded Lithios, a startup commercializing electrochemical lithium extraction. He earned his PhD in chemical engineering from MIT in 2022, where he pioneered shock electrodialysis and shock ion extraction for selective mineral recovery. A 2025 Forbes 30 Under 30 honoree, he also holds bachelor’s and master’s degrees from UC San Diego.

univ_uc_berkeley_nicole_king.gold: Nicole King is a Professor of Molecular and Cell Biology at UC Berkeley and a Howard Hughes Medical Institute Investigator. A MacArthur Fellow and National Academy of Sciences member, she pioneered using choanoflagellates to study animal origins, discovering that bacterial signals trigger their transition from unicellular to multicellular states.

univ_uc_berkeley_jonathan_richard_shewch.gold: A Professor of Computer Science at UC Berkeley, Shewchuk created the Triangle mesh generator, winning the 2003 James Hardy Wilkinson Prize in Numerical Software. He is renowned for his paper "An Introduction to the Conjugate Gradient Method Without the Agonizing Pain" and research in computational geometry, numerical robustness, and scientific computing.

univ_mit_p_w_rosenkranz.gold: Philip W. Rosenkranz is a Principal Research Scientist at the MIT Research Laboratory of Electronics. He developed the Rosenkranz model for atmospheric microwave absorption and utilizes gyrotrons for high-resolution molecular spectroscopy. His research in microwave radiometry supported satellite missions like AMSU and Nimbus-5. He received the 2018 IEEE GRSS Distinguished Achievement Award.

univ_uc_berkeley_rachel_b_brem.gold: Rachel B. Brem is an Associate Professor at UC Berkeley and the Buck Institute for Research on Aging. She developed the RH-seq method to map genetic variation between species. Her research utilizes *Saccharomyces* and *Kluyveromyces* yeasts to investigate the molecular basis of complex phenotypes, including thermotolerance, stress resistance, and evolutionary transitions.

univ_mit_gabriel_r_bitran.gold: Gabriel R. Bitran is a Professor of Management and former Associate Dean at MIT Sloan. He served as Editor-in-Chief of Management Science and President of the Production and Operations Management Society. An MSOM Distinguished Fellow, he specialized in pricing and service operations before a 2015 conviction for a $500 million hedge fund scam.

univ_uc_berkeley_martin_jay.gold: Martin Jay is the Sidney Hellman Ehrman Professor of History Emeritus at UC Berkeley. A leading intellectual historian, he authored the seminal Frankfurt School study *The Dialectical Imagination* (1973) and *Downcast Eyes* (1993), which explores the denigration of vision in French thought. He received the American Historical Association’s Award for Scholarly Distinction in 2018.

univ_uc_berkeley_j_w_taylor.gold: John Waldo Taylor is a Professor of the Graduate School at UC Berkeley. He pioneered molecular species recognition in fungi and researches the evolution of pathogens like *Coccidioides* and fungal communities in *Sorghum* crops. A former president of the International Mycological Association, he was awarded the 2018 DeBary Medal for his contributions to mycology.

univ_uc_berkeley_basem_al_shayeb.gold: A microbiologist and co-founder of Amber Bio, Al-Shayeb co-discovered "huge phages" with genomes exceeding 200 kilobases and "Borgs," giant DNA elements that influence methane oxidation. His research at UC Berkeley led to the development of the hypercompact CRISPR-CasΦ gene-editing system. He was named to the 2021 Forbes 30 Under 30 list.

univ_uc_berkeley_pratik_mukherjee.gold: Pratik Mukherjee, MD, PhD, is a Professor of Radiology and Bioengineering at UCSF and core faculty at UC Berkeley. He directs the Center for Imaging of Neurodegenerative Disease and the Neural Connectivity Laboratory. A specialist in diffusion MRI and connectomics, he is a Principal Investigator for the TRACK-TBI study and the Weill Neurohub.

univ_uc_berkeley_elie_j_diner.gold: Elie J. Diner is an immunologist who conducted research at the University of California, Berkeley. In the Russell Vance lab, he co-discovered that the enzyme cGAS produces the cyclic dinucleotide cGAMP, which activates the STING receptor to trigger type I interferon responses. He earned his Ph.D. from UC Santa Barbara, studying bacterial contact-dependent growth inhibition.

univ_uc_berkeley_k_nishiizumi.gold: Kunihiko Nishiizumi is a Senior Space Fellow at the University of California, Berkeley’s Space Sciences Laboratory. He pioneered cosmogenic nuclide dating, developing absolute calibration standards for beryllium-10 and aluminum-26 accelerator mass spectrometry. His research applies cosmic ray exposure histories to date Meteor Crater and analyze lunar samples, meteorites, and terrestrial landscape evolution.

univ_uc_berkeley_sarah_a_stanley.gold: Sarah A. Stanley is an Associate Professor at UC Berkeley with joint appointments in Molecular and Cell Biology and the School of Public Health. She directs the Alliance for Global Health and Science and manages the university’s BSL3 facilities. A 2025 Distinguished Teaching Award recipient, she researches *Mycobacterium tuberculosis* host-pathogen interactions and immune subversion mechanisms.

univ_uc_berkeley_enrico_moretti.gold: Enrico Moretti is the Michael Peevey and Donald Vial Professor of Economics at UC Berkeley. He authored *The New Geography of Jobs*, which won the William Bowen Prize, and served as Editor-in-Chief of the *Journal of Economic Perspectives*. A recipient of the Sherwin Rosen Prize, he is a Research Associate at the NBER.

univ_uc_berkeley_jeffrey_mahler.gold: Co-founder and CTO of Ambi Robotics, Jeffrey Mahler earned his PhD from UC Berkeley’s AUTOLAB. He is best known for developing the Dexterity Network (Dex-Net), a framework using deep learning and synthetic data for robust robot grasping. His work focuses on simulation-to-reality transfer and autonomous manipulation for logistics and surgical applications.

univ_uc_berkeley_susan_marqusee.gold: Susan Marqusee is a biophysical chemist and the Eveland Warren Chair at UC Berkeley. A National Academy of Sciences member, she pioneered the de novo design of alpha-helical peptides and used single-molecule optical tweezers to observe protein folding. She previously directed QB3-Berkeley and currently leads the National Science Foundation’s Directorate for Biological Sciences.

univ_uc_berkeley_stuart_russell.gold: Stuart Russell is the Smith-Zadeh Professor of Engineering at UC Berkeley and Director of the Center for Human-Compatible AI. He co-authored the standard textbook *Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach*. A Fellow of the Royal Society and OBE recipient, he is also a Professor of Computational Precision Health at UCSF and an ACM Karlstrom Outstanding Educator.

univ_uc_berkeley_ronald_w_wolff.gold: Ronald W. Wolff is Professor Emeritus of Industrial Engineering and Operations Research at UC Berkeley, serving as department chair from 1993 to 1995. He is renowned for proving the PASTA property (Poisson Arrivals See Time Averages) and authoring the seminal 1989 textbook *Stochastic Modeling and the Theory of Queues*.

univ_uc_berkeley_maria_schriver.gold: Maria Schriver is a researcher at the University of California, Berkeley, specializing in graphene and nanostructures. A member of the Zettl Research Group, she lead-authored the highly cited study "Graphene as a long-term metal oxidation barrier: worse than nothing." Her work includes developing metal nanoparticle-graphene superstructures for solar cells and amorphous silicon-carbon nanostructure photovoltaic devices.

univ_uc_berkeley_grace_r_nakayama.gold: Grace R. Nakayama is a molecular biology researcher at the University of California, Berkeley. Collaborating with Peter G. Schultz, she pioneered the development of catalytic antibodies, co-authoring seminal papers in *Science* and *JACS*. Her research focused on the chemical modification of antibody combining sites and the creation of semisynthetic, metal-dependent antibodies for stereospecific catalysis.

univ_uc_berkeley_mahasin_s_mujahid.gold: The Lillian E.I. and Dudley J. Aldous Chair and Professor of Epidemiology at UC Berkeley, Mujahid is the first Black woman to chair the Epidemiology Division. Her research examines neighborhood health effects and cardiovascular inequities. A University of Michigan PhD, she received the 2021 Carol D. Soc Distinguished Graduate Student Mentoring Award.

univ_uc_berkeley_koushik_sen.gold: A Professor of Computer Science at UC Berkeley, Sen pioneered concolic testing and Directed Automated Random Testing (DART), developing the CUTE and jCUTE tools. He received the 2019 ACM SIGSOFT Impact Paper Award, a Sloan Research Fellowship, and the NSF CAREER Award. He holds a Ph.D. from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign.

univ_uc_berkeley_severin_borenstein.gold: Severin Borenstein is the E.T. Grether Professor at UC Berkeley’s Haas School of Business and Faculty Director of the Energy Institute at Haas. A Research Associate at the NBER, he chairs the California ISO Board of Governors. His research focuses on market power and competition in energy and airline industries.

univ_mit_d_surry.gold: David S. Surry is a chemist who conducted postdoctoral research at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology in the Buchwald Group. He is recognized for his work on palladium-catalyzed cross-coupling reactions, particularly the amination of aryl halides. He co-authored a definitive guide on dialkylbiaryl phosphine ligands and earned his PhD from the University of Cambridge.

univ_uc_berkeley_yang_dan.gold: Yang Dan is the Paul Licht Distinguished Professor of Neurobiology at UC Berkeley and an HHMI Investigator. Elected to the National Academy of Sciences in 2018, she researches neural circuits controlling sleep, visual perception, and executive control. Her honors include the 2023 Scolnick Prize and the 2024 Peter Seeburg Integrative Neuroscience Prize.

univ_uc_berkeley_francis_a_macdonald.gold: Francis A. Macdonald is a Professor at UC Berkeley who previously taught at Harvard University. He holds a PhD from Harvard and a BS from Caltech. His research focuses on Snowball Earth, the Great Unconformity, and the co-evolution of tectonics and climate. He received the 2014 Donath Medal and the 2019 Macelwane Medal.

univ_uc_berkeley_c_c_lee.gold: C.C. Lee (Chuen Chien Lee) is an electrical engineer known for his seminal 1990 survey "Fuzzy Logic in Control Systems: Fuzzy Logic Controller," published in IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics. He earned his M.S. from UC Berkeley, conducting research at the Electronics Research Laboratory and NASA Ames Research Center.

univ_uc_berkeley_suzanne_scotchmer.gold: Suzanne Scotchmer was a Professor of Law, Economics, and Public Policy at UC Berkeley. She authored the seminal paper "Standing on the Shoulders of Giants" (1991) and the book *Innovation and Incentives* (2004). A Fellow of the Econometric Society, she pioneered models of cumulative innovation and consulted for the U.S. Department of Justice.

univ_uc_berkeley_anil_aswani.gold: Anil Aswani is a Professor of Industrial Engineering and Operations Research at UC Berkeley. He earned his PhD in EECS from Berkeley and a BS from the University of Michigan. His research integrates AI with systems engineering for optimization and control. A recipient of the NSF CAREER Award, he specializes in human-centered optimization and algorithmic fairness.

univ_uc_berkeley_lia_c_h_fernald.gold: Lia C. H. Fernald is the Brian and Jennifer Maxwell Endowed Chair and Professor of Community Health Sciences. She evaluates the impact of conditional cash transfer programs and nutritional interventions on child development in low-income countries. An affiliate of J-PAL, she has authored over 200 papers, including influential series in *The Lancet*.

univ_uc_berkeley_ning_sun.gold: Ning Sun is a Research Scientist at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory’s Advanced Biofuels and Bioproducts Process Development Unit (ABPDU). She specializes in lignin valorization and biomass pretreatment using ionic liquids. Sun earned her Ph.D. in Chemistry from the University of Alabama and was a postdoctoral fellow at the Joint BioEnergy Institute (JBEI).

univ_uc_berkeley_wendy_r_hansen.gold: Wendy R. Hansen is a biophysics researcher at the University of California, Berkeley. She co-authored the landmark 2002 *PNAS* study identifying van der Waals forces as the primary mechanism for gecko adhesion. Her work also demonstrated the self-cleaning properties of gecko setae and explored the detachment mechanisms of the parasite *Giardia lamblia*.

univ_uc_berkeley_daniel_a_fletcher.gold: Daniel A. Fletcher is the Purnendu Chatterjee Chair in Engineering Biological Systems at UC Berkeley. A Rhodes Scholar with a PhD from Stanford, he invented CellScope, a mobile phone-based microscopy tool for disease diagnostics. Elected to the National Academy of Medicine in 2025, he is a Chan Zuckerberg Biohub Investigator and Blum Center Faculty Director.

univ_uc_berkeley_d_r_stoddart.gold: David Ross Stoddart (1937–2014) was a geographer and preeminent coral reef authority. A professor at Cambridge and UC Berkeley, he founded the International Society for Reef Studies and led the successful campaign to save Aldabra Atoll from military use. His honors include the OBE, the Darwin Medal, and the Royal Geographical Society’s Founder’s Medal.

univ_uc_berkeley_anca_d_dragan.gold: Anca D. Dragan is an Associate Professor at UC Berkeley, directing the InterACT Lab and co-founding the Berkeley AI Research (BAIR) Lab. Currently leading AI Safety and Alignment at Google DeepMind, she received the PECASE and MIT TR35 awards for her pioneering work in human-robot interaction, AI alignment, and coordination for autonomous systems.

univ_uc_berkeley_sergey_e_paramonov.gold: Sergey E. Paramonov is a materials researcher who worked at the University of California, Berkeley and Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. Collaborating with Jean M. J. Fréchet, he developed acid-degradable polyacetal and polyurethane microparticles for drug delivery. At Rice University, he specialized in supramolecular self-assembly of peptide-functionalized carbon nanotubes and nanostructured collagen mimics.

univ_uc_berkeley_richard_m_harland.gold: Richard M. Harland is the C.H. Li Distinguished Professor and Dean of Biological Sciences at UC Berkeley. He is best known for discovering the *noggin* gene, which regulates neural induction and skeletal development. A member of the National Academy of Sciences and Fellow of the Royal Society, he received the 2014 Edwin G. Conklin Medal.

univ_uc_berkeley_william_peebles.gold: William Peebles is a research scientist at OpenAI and the head of Sora, the company's text-to-video generation model. He earned his PhD from the University of California, Berkeley in 2023, where he co-invented the Diffusion Transformer (DiT) architecture. His research focuses on scalable generative models, including applications in computer vision and magnetic resonance imaging reconstruction.

univ_uc_berkeley_paul_kalas.gold: Paul Kalas is an adjunct professor of astronomy at UC Berkeley. He led the team that captured the first visible-light images of exoplanet candidate Fomalhaut b using the Hubble Space Telescope. He also discovered the AU Microscopii debris disk and received the 2009 AAAS Newcomb Cleveland Prize for his work on circumstellar matter.

univ_mit_charles_e_osgood.gold: Charles E. Osgood (1916–1991) was a psychologist and professor at the University of Illinois. He is best known for developing the semantic differential technique to measure connotative meaning and the GRIT strategy for international tension reduction. His research in psycholinguistics, including works like *The Measurement of Meaning*, explored the cognitive dynamics of synesthesia and metaphor.

univ_uc_berkeley_bryan_d_mccloskey.gold: Bryan D. McCloskey is the Department Chair and Schlinger Distinguished Professor of Chemical Engineering at UC Berkeley and a faculty engineer at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. His research focuses on lithium-oxygen batteries and electrolyte transport. He received the 2015 VW/BASF Science Award Electrochemistry and the 2020 ECS Charles W. Tobias Young Investigator Award.

univ_uc_berkeley_ksenia_v_krasileva.gold: Ksenia V. Krasileva is an Associate Professor at UC Berkeley specializing in plant innate immunity and genomics. A 2021 NIH Director's New Innovator Award recipient and Gordon and Betty Moore Inventor Fellow, she uses comparative genomics to study plant immune receptors and genetic variation in crops, earning the 2018 Carlotta Award for wheat research.

univ_uc_berkeley_larry_nucci.gold: Adjunct Professor at UC Berkeley and Professor Emeritus at the University of Illinois Chicago, Larry Nucci pioneered social cognitive domain theory. He directs the Domain Based Moral Education lab, authored *Education in the Moral Domain*, and edited the *Handbook of Moral and Character Education*. He is a past president of the Jean Piaget Society.

univ_uc_berkeley_liam_foster.gold: Liam Foster is a Professor of Social Policy at the University of Sheffield specializing in pensions and active ageing. He edited the *Research Handbook on the Sociology of Ageing*, co-authored *Bryman’s Social Research Methods*, and is the managing editor of *Social Policy and Society*. He has collaborated with UC Berkeley faculty and presented at the university.

univ_uc_berkeley_baoxia_mi.gold: Baoxia Mi is the Wood-Calvert Chair in Engineering and Professor of Civil and Environmental Engineering, directing the Membrane Innovation Laboratory. Her research utilizes nanotechnology and 2D materials for water purification and desalination. A recipient of the NSF CAREER and Hellman Fellows awards, she earned her PhD from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign.

univ_uc_berkeley_ana_paula_arruda.gold: Ana Paula Arruda is an Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley in Nutritional Sciences & Toxicology. She earned her PhD from the Federal University of Rio de Janeiro and conducted postdoctoral research at Harvard. A 2025 Pew Scholar and Chan-Zuckerberg Biohub Investigator, she studies how organelle communication and endoplasmic reticulum architecture regulate metabolism in obesity.

univ_uc_berkeley_heather_a_haveman.gold: Heather A. Haveman is a Professor of Sociology and Business at the University of California, Berkeley. She authored the award-winning book *Magazines and the Making of America* (2016). In 2025, she received the Rosabeth Moss Kanter Distinguished Career Award. Her research utilizes natural-language processing to study organizational evolution, entrepreneurship, and gender in the workplace.

univ_uc_berkeley_joy_y_wang.gold: Joy Y. Wang is a CRISPR researcher who earned her Ph.D. from UC Berkeley in 2022 under Jennifer Doudna. She lead-authored major reviews in *Science* and *Nature Reviews Microbiology* and published research in *Nature* on CRISPR trimmer-integrases. An NSF Graduate Research Fellow, she specializes in the structural biology and evolution of genome-editing enzymes.

univ_uc_berkeley_ross_zervos_levine.gold: Ross Zervos Levine is the Willis H. Booth Chair in Banking and Finance Emeritus at UC Berkeley and a Senior Fellow at the Hoover Institution. His research focuses on financial regulation, banking stability, and economic growth. He authored *Rethinking Bank Regulation* and *Guardians of Finance* and previously served at the World Bank and Federal Reserve.

univ_uc_berkeley_ellen_oliensis.gold: Ellen Oliensis is the Klio Distinguished Professor of Classical Languages and Literature at UC Berkeley. A specialist in Latin poetry and psychoanalytic criticism, she authored *Horace and the Rhetoric of Authority*, *Freud’s Rome*, and *Loving Writing: Ovid’s Amores*. She previously chaired Berkeley’s Department of Classics and delivered the 2020 Housman Lecture.

univ_uc_berkeley_emily_elizabeth_crane.gold: Emily Elizabeth Crane is a genomics researcher at the University of California, Berkeley. She earned her Ph.D. in Molecular and Cell Biology in 2011 under Barbara Meyer, focusing on chromosome structure and dosage compensation. She notably authored the 2015 Nature study on condensin-driven remodeling of X chromosome topology and research on C. elegans enhancers.

univ_uc_berkeley_wendy_u_huynh.gold: Wendy U. Huynh is a researcher at the University of California, Berkeley, known for pioneering hybrid nanorod-polymer solar cells. In the Alivisatos group, she lead-authored the 2002 Science paper "Hybrid Nanorod-Polymer Solar Cells," demonstrating efficient energy conversion using CdSe nanorods and conjugated polymers. Her research significantly advanced the field of colloidal nanocrystal photovoltaics.

univ_uc_berkeley_roya_maboudian.gold: Roya Maboudian is a Distinguished Professor of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering at UC Berkeley and Co-Director of the Berkeley Sensor & Actuator Center. A recipient of the Presidential Early Career Award (PECASE), she is an IEEE and AVS Fellow. Her research focuses on surface science and materials engineering for micro/nanosystems and harsh-environment sensing.

univ_uc_berkeley_fyodor_d_urnov.gold: Fyodor D. Urnov is a Professor of Molecular Therapeutics at UC Berkeley and Director at the Innovative Genomics Institute. He co-coined the term "genome editing" in 2005 and led research identifying the target for the first approved CRISPR therapy for sickle cell disease. He previously held leadership roles at Sangamo Therapeutics and co-founded Tune Therapeutics.

univ_uc_berkeley_alistair_sinclair.gold: Alistair Sinclair is the Kikuo Ogawa and Kaoru Ogawa Professor of Computer Science and Statistics at UC Berkeley. He received the 1996 Gödel Prize and 2006 Fulkerson Prize for his work on Markov chain mixing times and randomized approximation algorithms for the permanent. He was the founding Associate Director of the Simons Institute.

univ_uc_berkeley_jose_m_carmena.gold: Jose M. Carmena is the Chancellor’s Professor of Electrical Engineering and Neuroscience at UC Berkeley and co-director of the Center for Neural Engineering and Prostheses. An IEEE Fellow, he co-developed "neural dust" for wireless neural recording. His honors include the McKnight Technological Innovations in Neuroscience Award and the Alfred P. Sloan Research Fellowship.

univ_uc_berkeley_maria_mooshammer.gold: Maria Mooshammer is a postdoctoral researcher at the University of California, Berkeley. A PhD graduate of the University of Vienna, she specializes in ecological stoichiometry and microbial nitrogen use efficiency. Her research, published in *Nature Communications* and *One Earth*, examines how crop diversity and microbial feedbacks influence soil organic matter dynamics and agricultural resilience.

univ_uc_berkeley_russell_l_de_valois.gold: Russell L. De Valois pioneered single-cell recordings in the macaque lateral geniculate nucleus, resolving the conflict between trichromatic and opponent-process theories of color vision. A National Academy of Sciences member, he co-authored the influential book *Spatial Vision* and received the Edgar D. Tillyer Award and the APA Distinguished Scientific Contribution Award.

univ_uc_berkeley_paulo_j_m_monteiro.gold: Paulo J.M. Monteiro is the Roy W. Carlson Distinguished Professor at UC Berkeley. Elected to the National Academy of Engineering in 2020, he co-authored the textbook *Concrete: Microstructure, Properties, and Materials*. His research focuses on sustainable cementitious materials and concrete nanotechnology, notably uncovering the chemical secrets behind the durability of ancient Roman structures.

univ_uc_berkeley_joshua_s_apte.gold: Joshua S. Apte is an Associate Professor at UC Berkeley, jointly appointed in Civil and Environmental Engineering and Public Health. A 2017 Walter A. Rosenblith New Investigator Award recipient, he pioneered hyperlocal air pollution mapping using Google Street View cars. His research addresses environmental justice disparities and global life expectancy impacts of particulate matter.

univ_uc_berkeley_s_d_bale.gold: Stuart D. Bale is a Professor of Physics at UC Berkeley and former Director of the Space Sciences Laboratory. He is the Principal Investigator for the FIELDS instrument suite on NASA’s Parker Solar Probe. A recipient of the 2025 Arctowski Medal, he earned his PhD from the University of Minnesota in 1994.

univ_uc_berkeley_william_r_boos.gold: William R. Boos is a Professor of Earth and Planetary Science at UC Berkeley and a Faculty Scientist at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. An MIT PhD, he researches the fluid dynamics of tropical monsoons. He received the NSF CAREER Award and AGU James R. Holton Award, and published Nature studies on monsoon orographic forcing.

univ_uc_berkeley_daniel_k_nomura.gold: Daniel K. Nomura is a Professor of Chemical Biology and Molecular Therapeutics at UC Berkeley. He directs the Novartis-Berkeley Translational Chemical Biology Institute and co-directs the Molecular Therapeutics Initiative. His research employs chemoproteomic platforms to target the undruggable proteome and dysregulated lipid metabolism. He co-founded Frontier Medicines and is Editor-in-Chief of Molecular Cancer Therapeutics.

univ_uc_berkeley_ziad_obermeyer.gold: Ziad Obermeyer is the Blue Cross of California Distinguished Associate Professor at UC Berkeley. A Chan Zuckerberg Biohub Investigator, he researches machine learning in medicine, notably identifying racial bias in clinical algorithms in *Science*. He co-founded Nightingale Open Science and Dandelion Health and was named to the 2023 TIME100 AI list.

univ_uc_berkeley_heejung_bang.gold: Heejung Bang is a Professor of Biostatistics at the University of California, Davis, and a Fellow of the American Statistical Association. She is best known for co-authoring the seminal 2005 paper "Doubly Robust Estimation in Missing Data and Causal Inference Models" with James Robins, which introduced the doubly robust estimator to advanced causal inference.

univ_uc_berkeley_g_mathias_kondolf.gold: G. Mathias Kondolf is a fluvial geomorphologist and Professor of Environmental Planning at UC Berkeley, where he leads the River-Lab. He authored *Tools in Fluvial Geomorphology* and is known for his "hungry water" research on dam impacts. He has provided expert testimony to the U.S. Supreme Court and the International Court of Justice.

univ_uc_berkeley_dan_i_slobin.gold: Dan I. Slobin is Professor Emeritus of Psychology and Linguistics at UC Berkeley. A pioneer in psycholinguistics, he developed the "thinking for speaking" hypothesis, exploring how language-specific structures influence cognitive processes during communication. He edited the series The Crosslinguistic Study of Language Acquisition and is a Fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences.

univ_uc_berkeley_jonathan_arons.gold: Jonathan Arons is a Professor Emeritus of Astronomy and Physics at UC Berkeley. He served as Director of the Theoretical Astrophysics Center and Executive Director of the Miller Institute. An APS and Guggenheim Fellow, he specializes in pulsar magnetospheres and relativistic shocks, notably modeling the Crab Nebula’s wisps. He earned his Ph.D. from Harvard.

univ_uc_berkeley_benjamin_blonder.gold: Benjamin Blonder is an associate professor at UC Berkeley and the Haydn P. Reinecker Distinguished Professor in Forest Genetics. He leads the Macrosystems Ecology Laboratory, studying plant responses to climate change. A former National Geographic Young Explorer, he co-founded the University of Arizona Sky School and was named a White House Champion of Change.

univ_uc_berkeley_donald_sarason.gold: Donald Sarason (1933–2017) was a professor of mathematics at the University of California, Berkeley, specializing in operator theory and Hardy spaces. He is known for his 1967 paper "Generalized interpolation in $H^\infty$," which founded commutant-lifting theory. He authored *Complex Function Theory* and supervised 40 doctoral students, including Sun-Yung Alice Chang and Sheldon Axler.

univ_uc_berkeley_judith_p_klinman.gold: Judith P. Klinman is a Professor of the Graduate School at UC Berkeley and the first female professor in its physical sciences. Awarded the National Medal of Science, she discovered protein-derived quinone cofactors and pioneered research on quantum mechanical hydrogen tunneling in enzyme catalysis. She also chaired Berkeley’s Department of Chemistry.

univ_uc_berkeley_theresa_harrison.gold: Theresa (Tessa) Harrison is a researcher at the University of California, Berkeley’s Helen Wills Neuroscience Institute. Working in the Jagust Lab, she specializes in cognitive aging and Alzheimer’s disease, utilizing multimodal neuroimaging and PET biomarkers. She has co-authored research on clinical trial design and disease-modifying treatments, focusing on longitudinal tau accumulation and neuroimaging biomarker harmonization.

univ_uc_berkeley_yoram_rubin.gold: Yoram Rubin is a Chancellor’s Professor Emeritus at the University of California, Berkeley, specializing in stochastic hydrogeology and inverse modeling. He authored the influential book *Applied Stochastic Hydrogeology* and received the 2015 Henry Darcy Medal and the 2004 AGU Hydrologic Science Award. He holds degrees from the Technion and a PhD from Tel-Aviv University.

univ_uc_berkeley_hilary_hoynes.gold: Hilary Hoynes is the Chancellor’s Professor of Economics and Public Policy at UC Berkeley and director of the Berkeley Opportunity Lab. A National Academy of Sciences member, she researches the long-term health and economic impacts of social safety nets, specifically SNAP. She received the 2024 Daniel Holland Medal for lifetime achievement in public economics.

univ_uc_berkeley_david_l_sunding.gold: David L. Sunding is the Thomas J. Graff Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley and former chair of Agricultural and Resource Economics. He founded the Berkeley Water Center and served as Senior Economist for the White House Council of Economic Advisers. Currently Vice Chairman at Berkeley Research Group, he specializes in water markets and agricultural technology.

univ_uc_berkeley_ananth_madhavan.gold: Ananth Madhavan is the Executive Director of the Master of Financial Engineering program at UC Berkeley Haas. He was previously Managing Director and Global Head of Research for ETF and Index Investing at BlackRock. A Cornell PhD, he authored *Exchange-Traded Funds and the New Dynamics of Investing* and formerly taught at USC and Wharton.

univ_uc_berkeley_patrick_gonz_lez.gold: Patrick González is a forest ecologist and Associate Adjunct Professor at UC Berkeley. A lead author for five IPCC reports, he previously served as Principal Climate Change Scientist for the U.S. National Park Service and Assistant Director for Climate and Biodiversity at the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy.

univ_uc_berkeley_ronald_amundson.gold: Ronald Amundson is the Betty and Isaac Barshad Endowed Chair in Soils Science at UC Berkeley. A Fellow of the American Geophysical Union and Soil Science Society of America, he specializes in isotope biogeochemistry. His research investigates soil carbon and nitrogen cycling, the Anthropocene, and the conservation of "endangered soils" in the United States.

univ_uc_berkeley_j_bradford_delong.gold: J. Bradford DeLong is a professor of economics at the University of California, Berkeley, and a National Bureau of Economic Research associate. He served as Deputy Assistant Secretary of the U.S. Treasury (1993–1995) during the Clinton administration. He authored the 2022 book *Slouching Towards Utopia* and co-authored *The End of Influence*.

univ_uc_berkeley_venkatesan_guruswami.gold: Venkatesan Guruswami is a Chancellor’s Professor at UC Berkeley and Senior Scientist at the Simons Institute. Renowned for his work on list decoding, he received the Presburger Award, a Guggenheim Fellowship, and the ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award. He is a Fellow of the ACM, IEEE, and AMS, and Editor-in-Chief of the Journal of the ACM.

univ_uc_berkeley_johannes_w_de_jong.gold: Johannes W. de Jong is an Assistant Project Scientist at the University of California, Berkeley. Researching the mesolimbic dopamine system, he identified neural circuit mechanisms for encoding aversive stimuli in *Neuron*. His work demonstrates how ventral tegmental dopamine D2 receptor expression influences incentive motivation and explores the neural basis of addiction and motivated behavior.

univ_uc_berkeley_yihan_xiao.gold: A researcher in the Ceder Group at the University of California, Berkeley, and Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, Xiao specializes in computational materials science and solid-state batteries. They identified topological principles for the high-throughput discovery of fast Li-ion conductors and authored key research on lithium oxide superionic conductors inspired by garnet and NASICON structures.

univ_uc_berkeley_jonathan_simon.gold: Jonathan Simon is the Lance Robbins Professor of Criminal Justice Law at UC Berkeley. He authored the award-winning books *Governing through Crime* (2007) and *Poor Discipline* (1993), as well as *Mass Incarceration on Trial* (2014). His research examines the role of punishment and risk management in the governance of contemporary societies.

univ_ucsd_sahngki_hong.gold: Sahngki Hong is a researcher at the University of California San Diego’s Department of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering. He is the lead author of studies on wearable thermoelectrics for personalized thermoregulation (*Science Advances*, 2019) and adaptive thermal camouflage (*Advanced Functional Materials*, 2020). His research also includes developing sub-nanowatt microfluidic single-cell calorimetry.

univ_ucsd_maria_del_mar_inda.gold: Maria-del-Mar Inda is a researcher at the University of California San Diego and the Ludwig Institute for Cancer Research. She specializes in glioblastoma heterogeneity, identifying a paracrine cytokine signaling circuit involving IL-6 and leukemia inhibitory factor (LIF). Her research in *Genes & Development* shows how mutant EGFR-induced circuits maintain tumor diversity and promote growth.

univ_ucsd_sharon_rose.gold: Sharon Rose is Professor and Chair of Linguistics at the University of California, San Diego. Specializing in phonological typology and African languages, she conducts fieldwork on Semitic and Kordofanian systems. Her research investigates grammatical tone, prosody, and musical pitch processing. She co-authored "A typology of consonant agreement as correspondence" and is a NIAS Fellow.

univ_ucsd_eli_o_meltzer.gold: A Clinical Professor of Pediatrics at UC San Diego, Meltzer co-directs the Allergy & Asthma Medical Group and Research Center. He developed the Rhinitis Control Assessment Test (RCAT) and has authored over 600 scientific publications. His honors include the AAAAI Distinguished Clinician Award and the AAP Jerome Glaser Distinguished Service Award.

univ_ucsd_manoj_monga.gold: Manoj Monga is the Chair and Professor of Urology at UC San Diego School of Medicine. An authority in endourology, his research includes reproductive health, infertility, and medical device innovation. He previously served as Secretary of the American Urological Association and directed the Stevan B. Streem Center for Endourology at the Cleveland Clinic.

univ_ucsd_sidney_zisook.gold: Sidney Zisook is a Distinguished Professor of Psychiatry at UC San Diego and Director of its Psychiatry Residency Training Program. He co-chaired the VAST-D study on treatment-resistant depression and directs the HEAR Program for physician suicide prevention. A leading authority on bereavement, he received the 2021 American College of Psychiatry Depression Research Award.

univ_ucsd_william_z_potter.gold: William Z. Potter is an academic researcher in Drug-Induced Hepatotoxicity and Protection at University of California San Diego. At the NIH, he co-authored seminal 1973 studies on acetaminophen-induced hepatic necrosis and metabolic activation. He later served as Vice President of Translational Neuroscience at Merck, Head of Early Clinical Development at Eli Lilly, and Senior Advisor at NIMH.

univ_ucsd_katherine_a_delong.gold: Katherine A. DeLong was an Associate Project Scientist at the University of California, San Diego, affiliated with the Kutas Lab and Center for Research in Language. She is distinguished for her research using event-related brain potentials (ERPs) to study language prediction, notably co-authoring a seminal 2005 *Nature Neuroscience* paper on probabilistic word pre-activation during comprehension.

univ_ucsd_mark_w_bondi.gold: Mark W. Bondi is a Distinguished Professor of Psychiatry at UC San Diego and Director of the Neuropsychological Assessment Unit at the VA San Diego Healthcare System. Former President of the Society for Clinical Neuropsychology, he received the 2016 Alzheimer Award for staging preclinical Alzheimer’s disease and co-authored *Mild Cognitive Impairment and Dementia*.

univ_ucsd_gentry_n_patrick.gold: Gentry Patrick is a Professor of Neurobiology at UC San Diego. He investigates molecular pathways involving the ubiquitin-proteasome system and synaptic plasticity, notably discovering the p25 protein's role in Alzheimer's disease. A former Damon Runyon Cancer Research Foundation fellow, he founded the PATHSways to STEM program and serves as Director of Mentorship and Diversity.

univ_ucsd_eoin_fahy.gold: Eoin Fahy is a researcher in the Department of Bioengineering and the San Diego Supercomputer Center at UC San Diego. A principal investigator for the LIPID MAPS project, he co-developed its international lipid classification system and nomenclature. He also contributes to the Metabolomics Workbench repository and develops bioinformatics tools like LipidFinder for mass spectrometry data analysis.

univ_ucsd_shashi_kant_tiwari.gold: A researcher at the University of California San Diego, Tiwari earned his PhD from CSIR-IITR in 2015. He studies neural stem cell responses to HIV, Zika, and neurodegenerative diseases using 3D brain organoids. His research notably demonstrated that nanotized curcumin induces adult neurogenesis and reverses cognitive deficits in Alzheimer’s disease models.

univ_ucsd_y_n_l_espiritu.gold: Yến Lê Espiritu is a Distinguished Professor of Ethnic Studies at UC San Diego. A founding member of the Critical Refugee Studies Collective, she has served as President of the Association for Asian American Studies. Her award-winning books include *Asian American Panethnicity* and *Home Bound: Filipino American Lives across Cultures, Communities, and Countries*.

univ_ucsd_kaare_w_str_m.gold: Kaare W. Strøm is a Distinguished Professor of Political Science at the University of California, San Diego. A Stanford PhD recipient, he authored Minority Government and Majority Rule and co-edited Delegation and Accountability in Parliamentary Democracies. He received the Stein Rokkan Prize and is a member of the Norwegian Academy of Science and Letters.

univ_ucsd_shunichi_shimasaki.gold: Shunichi Shimasaki is an Emeritus Professor at UC San Diego. He is recognized for pioneering research on the bone morphogenetic protein (BMP) system in reproduction, specifically identifying the roles of BMP-15 and GDF-9. He also characterized insulin-like growth factor binding proteins (IGFBPs) and follistatin, and investigates the molecular basis of granulosa cell tumors.

univ_ucsd_galina_khemlina.gold: Galina Khemlina is a researcher at the University of California San Diego’s Department of Geriatrics. She co-authored "The biology of Hepatocellular carcinoma: implications for genomic and immune therapies" (2017), examining genomic alterations and targeted aberrations in liver cancer. An MD from the Second Moscow Medical Institute, she also specializes in hospice and palliative medicine.

univ_uc_berkeley_iris_a_ly.gold: Iris A. Ly is an academic researcher at the University of California, Berkeley. In the 1970s and 1980s, she co-authored immunological studies with Robert I. Mishell, including research on sterile subpopulations of mouse spleen cells and the effects of interferon-γ and interleukin-2 on cytotoxic T lymphocytes. Her work focuses on RNA interference and gene delivery.

univ_uc_berkeley_irina_m_conboy.gold: Irina M. Conboy is a Professor of Bioengineering at UC Berkeley and Chief Science Officer of Generation Lab. She pioneered heterochronic parabiosis to study stem cell rejuvenation in a 2005 Nature study. Her research focuses on systemic factors in aging and developed "neutral blood exchange" (plasma dilution) therapies. She is Editor-in-Chief of Rejuvenation Research.

univ_uc_berkeley_m_w_leung.gold: M. W. Leung is a public health researcher who co-authored the influential 2004 study "Community based participatory research: a promising approach for increasing epidemiology's relevance in the 21st century" with Meredith Minkler and Irene Yen at the University of California, Berkeley. Leung’s work focuses on community-based participatory research (CBPR) and health policy implementation.

univ_ucsd_xianjin_zhou.gold: Xianjin Zhou is an Assistant Professor of Psychiatry at the University of California San Diego. He investigates molecular mechanisms of psychiatric disorders, identifying *SP4* as a susceptibility gene for bipolar disorder. His research focuses on RNA-related mechanisms, including *XIST* overexpression in affective disorders and intergenic splicing of the *DISC1* gene in schizophrenia.

univ_ucsd_kathleen_i_pinson.gold: Kathleen I. Pinson is a researcher at the University of California, San Diego, in the Department of Reproductive Medicine. She was the lead author of a seminal 2000 *Nature* paper that identified LRP6 as a critical Wnt co-receptor. Her research investigates Wnt/β-catenin signaling roles in embryonic development, dorsal thalamic formation, and cancer.

univ_ucsd_vikash_gilja.gold: Vikash Gilja is an Associate Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering at UC San Diego, where he directs the Translational Neural Engineering Lab. He earned his PhD from Stanford and co-founded its Neural Prosthetics Translational Laboratory. Gilja previously worked with Neuralink’s founding team and advises Paradromics, focusing on high-performance brain-machine interfaces and clinical neural prosthetics.

univ_ucsd_kristen_jepsen.gold: Kristen Jepsen is the Director of the IGM Genomics Center at the University of California San Diego. Her research focuses on nuclear receptor co-repressors N-CoR and SMRT, specifically their regulation via proteasome-dependent degradation and inhibitors. She also serves as Co-Director of the Genomics & Epigenetics Core for the UCSD Diabetes Research Center.

univ_ucsd_s_r_renn.gold: Scot R. Renn is a physicist at the University of California, San Diego. He is best known for co-predicting the twist-grain-boundary (TGB) phase in liquid crystals with Tom C. Lubensky in 1988, identifying it as the liquid crystalline analog of the Abrikosov vortex lattice in superconductors. His research also includes Luttinger liquids and high-temperature superconductors.

univ_ucsd_jin_zhang.gold: Jin Zhang is a Professor and Vice Chair of Pharmacology at UC San Diego. A former trainee of Nobel laureate Roger Tsien, she specializes in genetically encoded fluorescent biosensors and super-resolution microscopy to study spatiotemporal cell signaling. Her honors include the NIH Director’s Pioneer Award, NCI Outstanding Investigator Award, and the Christian B. Anfinsen Award.

univ_ucsd_nathan_j_zvaifler.gold: Nathan J. Zvaifler (1927–2015) was Professor of Medicine Emeritus and Chief of Rheumatology at UC San Diego from 1970 to 1990. He pioneered research on synoviocytes and mesenchymal precursor cells in rheumatoid arthritis. Zvaifler served as Acting Chairman of Medicine, was the 1990 Heberden Orator, and is the namesake of a UCSD rheumatology lectureship.

univ_ucsd_ben_a_croker.gold: Ben A. Croker is a Professor of Pediatrics at the University of California San Diego, specializing in innate immunity and inflammation. He is recognized for identifying SOCS3 as a critical negative regulator of IL-6 and G-CSF signaling. His research explores Jak-Stat pathways, neutrophil biology, and the roles of pyroptosis and necroptosis in emergency hematopoiesis.

univ_ucsd_robert_e_clark.gold: Robert E. Clark is a Professor Emeritus of Psychiatry at UC San Diego and a Research Scientist at the VA San Diego Healthcare System. Specializing in the neurological foundations of memory, he developed a rat model of human amnesia. His research extensively examines the medial temporal lobe and hippocampal systems, often collaborating with Larry Squire.

univ_ucsd_john_w_ayers.gold: John W. Ayers is an associate professor and Vice Chief of Innovation at the University of California San Diego. A computational epidemiologist, he led a 2023 *JAMA Internal Medicine* study comparing AI chatbot and physician responses. He is an affiliate scientist at the Qualcomm Institute and received the Burroughs Wellcome Fund innovator award.

univ_uc_berkeley_herbert_m_evans.gold: Herbert M. Evans is an academic researcher at the University of California, Berkeley. He is associated with the study of advanced mathematical theories within the university's academic environment. No further specific research achievements, publications, or distinct mathematical contributions are verified for an individual of this name beyond the provided disambiguation.

univ_ucsd_emily_a_meier.gold: Emily A. Meier, PhD, is the director of Psycho-Oncology Supportive Care Services at UC San Diego Health and an assistant clinical professor of psychiatry. Affiliated with Moores Cancer Center, she specializes in dignity therapy and meaning-centered psychotherapy. Her research focuses on end-of-life issues, including rituals and the conceptualization of "successful dying."

univ_ucsd_renkun_chen.gold: Renkun Chen is a Professor of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering who earned his Ph.D. from UC Berkeley in 2008. His research investigates micro- and nanoscale thermal transport, specifically thin-film boiling and personal thermal management. An R&D 100 Award recipient, he develops wearable thermoelectric devices and high-flux evaporation cooling technologies for high-power electronics.

univ_ucsd_r_c_dynes.gold: Robert C. Dynes is a physicist who served as the 18th president of the University of California and 6th chancellor of the San Diego campus. Known for the Dynes formula in superconductivity, he previously led research at Bell Laboratories and received the 1990 Fritz London Memorial Prize. He is a National Academy of Sciences member.

univ_ucsd_matthew_d_shin.gold: Matthew D. Shin is a researcher at the University of California San Diego’s Department of NanoEngineering. He specializes in nanotechnology-based COVID-19 vaccine development, utilizing plant virus-based nanoparticles and slow-release hydrogels. He co-authored the Nature Nanotechnology review "COVID-19 vaccine development and a potential nanomaterial path forward" and developed trivalent subunit vaccine candidates for SARS-CoV-2.

univ_ucsd_vincent_p_crawford.gold: Vincent P. Crawford is a Distinguished Professor Emeritus and Research Professor at UC San Diego and Drummond Professor of Political Economy Emeritus at Oxford. He pioneered behavioral game theory and strategic communication, including the Crawford-Sobel model of "cheap talk." A Fellow of the Econometric Society and British Academy, he specializes in coordination and level-k models.

univ_ucsd_larry_r_squire.gold: Larry R. Squire is a Distinguished Professor at UC San Diego and Research Career Scientist at VA San Diego. A National Academy of Sciences member and former Society for Neuroscience president, he authored *Memory and Brain* and co-authored *Memory: From Mind to Molecules* with Eric Kandel, specializing in the medial temporal lobe and declarative memory.

univ_ucsd_paula_desplats.gold: Paula Desplats is an Associate Professor of Neurosciences at UC San Diego and Co-Director of the Center for Circadian Biology and Medicine. Her research focuses on neuroepigenetics and circadian rhythms in Parkinson’s and Alzheimer’s diseases. She leads Michael J. Fox Foundation-funded studies identifying blood-based DNA methylation biomarkers for Parkinson’s diagnosis and investigates time-restricted eating treatments.

univ_ucsd_ajay_bharti.gold: Clinical Professor of Medicine at UC San Diego, Bharti is an infectious disease specialist and lead author of the seminal review "Leptospirosis: a zoonotic disease of global importance" in *The Lancet Infectious Diseases*. His research also examines HIV and malaria co-infections and neurocognitive functioning, with funding from the National Institutes of Health.

univ_ucsd_chi_yuan_chang.gold: Chi-Yuan Chang is a researcher at UC San Diego’s Swartz Center for Computational Neuroscience. He earned his PhD in Bioengineering from UCSD in 2022. His research focuses on EEG signal processing and Brain-Computer Interfaces, specifically developing Artifact Subspace Reconstruction (ASR) for artifact removal and advancing methods for seizure forecasting and online mental stress monitoring.

univ_ucsd_hyun_woo_kim.gold: Hyun Woo Kim is a researcher at UC San Diego’s Scripps Institution of Oceanography and Skaggs School of Pharmacy. Affiliated with William H. Gerwick’s lab, he developed **NPClassifier**, a deep learning tool for natural product classification, and **PECAN**, a neural network predicting anticancer activity. His research advances computational metabolomics and natural product drug discovery.
