univ_ucsd_nancy_cartwright.gold: A MacArthur Fellow and past president of the Philosophy of Science Association, Cartwright is a professor at Durham University. She received the 2026 BBVA Frontiers of Knowledge Award. Her seminal books include *How the Laws of Physics Lie* and *The Dappled World*, which examine causality, evidence-based policy, and the philosophy of science.

univ_ucsd_gordon_b_dahl.gold: Gordon B. Dahl is a Professor of Economics at UC San Diego. A Princeton PhD and recipient of the Albert Rees Prize, he is a Research Associate at the NBER. His research on family income, child achievement, and family violence has appeared in the *American Economic Review* and *Quarterly Journal of Economics*.

univ_ucsd_f_g_bailey.gold: F. G. Bailey (1924–2020) was a founding faculty member of the Department of Anthropology at the University of California, San Diego. A Fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, he authored *Caste and the Economic Frontier* (1957) and *Stratagems and Spoils* (1969), analyzing social change, power, and economic transitions in rural India.

univ_ucsd_alexandra_c_newton.gold: Alexandra C. Newton is a Distinguished Professor of Pharmacology at University of California San Diego. A leading authority on Protein Kinase C (PKC) and PHLPP phosphatase, she reframed PKC as a tumor suppressor in cancer. She served as President of the International Union of Biochemistry and Molecular Biology and received the ASPET Julius Axelrod Award.

univ_ucsd_jay_s_desgrosellier.gold: Jay S. Desgrosellier is an Associate Professor of Pathology at the University of California, San Diego, and a member of the Moores Cancer Center. He earned his Ph.D. from Vanderbilt University. His research investigates integrin signaling and breast cancer stem cells, notably identifying an adhesion-independent role for integrin αvβ3 in promoting tumor metastasis.

univ_ucsd_m_omar_din.gold: M. Omar Din is a bioengineer and researcher at the University of California, San Diego. He developed the synchronized lysis circuit (SLC), a synthetic gene circuit for periodic bacterial drug delivery to tumors, published in *Nature*. A co-founder of GenCirq, Inc., his research focuses on synthetic biology and engineered bacterial therapeutics for cancer treatment.

univ_ucsd_carrie_a_joyce.gold: Carrie A. Joyce is a researcher at the University of California, San Diego, specializing in signal processing and cognitive science. She co-authored the 2003 study using blind source separation (BSS) to remove ocular artifacts from EEG data. Her research also explores neurocomputational models of visual expertise and the fusiform face area.

univ_ucsd_priyadarshini_pantham.gold: Priyadarshini Pantham is an Assistant Professor at UC San Diego, leading the Pantham Lab. She investigates placental molecular mechanisms in gestational diabetes and preeclampsia. Pantham co-leads the Science and Technology Hub at UCSD’s Center for OBGYN Research Innovation and holds a PhD in Obstetrics and Gynecology from the University of Auckland.

univ_ucsd_hadi_esmaeilzadeh.gold: Hadi Esmaeilzadeh is the Halıcıoğlu Chair in Computer Architecture and founding director of the Alternative Computing Technologies (ACT) Lab. He serves as associate director of the Center for Machine Integrated Computing and Security. A 2018 ISCA Hall of Fame inductee, he received the IEEE TCCA Young Computer Architect Award and an HPCA Distinguished Paper Award.

univ_ucsd_ted_j_case.gold: Ted J. Case (1947–2015) was a Professor of Biology at UC San Diego and a member of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences. An expert in evolutionary ecology, he authored *An Illustrated Guide to Theoretical Ecology* and co-edited *Community Ecology* with Jared Diamond. His research extensively covered island biogeography, species invasions, and reptile population dynamics.

univ_ucsd_deborah_yelon.gold: Deborah Yelon is a Professor of Cell and Developmental Biology at UCSD. She utilizes zebrafish models to investigate the molecular mechanisms of cardiac organogenesis and chamber formation. A former department chair, she has received the Burroughs Wellcome Fund Career Award and the American Heart Association Established Investigator Award for her research on heart development.

univ_ucsd_dean_c_delis.gold: Dean C. Delis is a Professor Emeritus of Psychiatry at the University of California San Diego. A board-certified neuropsychologist, he is the lead author of the California Verbal Learning Test (CVLT) and the Delis-Kaplan Executive Function System (D-KEFS). He previously served as Director of the Psychological Assessment Unit at the San Diego VA Medical Center.

univ_ucsd_martin_f_kagnoff.gold: Martin F. Kagnoff was a Professor Emeritus at UC San Diego, directing the Laboratory of Mucosal Immunology and the Wm. K. Warren Medical Research Center for Celiac Disease. A Harvard Medical School graduate, he served as Editor-in-Chief of the Journal of Clinical Investigation and published extensively on celiac disease pathogenesis and intestinal immunity.

univ_ucsd_nathan_c_gianneschi.gold: Nathan C. Gianneschi is the Jacob & Rosaline Cohn Professor at Northwestern University and former Teddy Traylor Scholar at UC San Diego. He specializes in supramolecular chemistry, enzyme-responsive nanomaterials, and liquid-phase TEM. A PECASE recipient, he received the NIH Director’s New Innovator and Transformative Research Awards for his work in bionanotechnology and stimuli-responsive materials.

univ_ucsd_greg_w_rouse.gold: Greg W. Rouse is a Distinguished Professor and Curator of the Benthic Invertebrate Collection at Scripps Institution of Oceanography. He co-discovered the ruby seadragon and specializes in the phylogeny of annelids, notably bone-eating *Osedax* worms. Author of the book *Annelida*, he has named over 200 species and received the Antarctic Service Medal.

univ_ucsd_stephen_hedrick.gold: Stephen M. Hedrick is a Distinguished Professor Emeritus at UC San Diego and a National Academy of Sciences member. He is renowned for co-cloning the T-cell receptor genes in 1984. His research investigates immune homeostasis, lymphocyte dynamics, and the function of FOXO transcription factors in regulating T-cell survival and autoimmunity.

univ_ucsd_hua_jin.gold: Hua Jin is a researcher at the University of California San Diego, affiliated with the Division of Biological Sciences and Moores Cancer Center. Specializing in cell adhesion and spreading, Jin investigated how Abl tyrosine kinase regulates Rap1-GTP levels and CrkII phosphorylation to modulate integrin affinity. Jin also contributed to research on T-cadherin secretion in stem cells.

univ_ucsd_samara_l_reck_peterson.gold: Samara L. Reck-Peterson is a Howard Hughes Medical Institute Investigator and Professor at UC San Diego. Renowned for her research on the molecular motor dynein and intracellular transport, she was named an ASCB Fellow in 2023. She was appointed inaugural Chair of Biochemistry and Biophysics at Weill Cornell Medicine, effective August 2025.

univ_ucsd_andrew_b_sharabi.gold: Andrew B. Sharabi, MD, PhD, is a Professor and Jacobs Chancellor's Endowed Chair at UC San Diego. He directs the Center for Precision Radiation Medicine and leads an NIH-funded lab investigating radiation-immunotherapy synergy. A board-certified radiation oncologist, he chairs ASTRO’s Immunotherapy Track and completed his MD/PhD at Baylor and residency at Johns Hopkins.

univ_ucsd_larissa_m_podust.gold: Larissa M. Podust is an Emeritus Professor at the UC San Diego Skaggs School of Pharmacy and Pharmaceutical Sciences. She specializes in the structural biology of cytochrome P450 enzymes, notably CYP51, to target neglected tropical diseases like primary amoebic meningoencephalitis. Her work employs X-ray crystallography and enzyme engineering for drug discovery.

univ_ucsd_soni_ancoli_israel.gold: Sonia Ancoli-Israel is a Professor Emeritus of Psychiatry at UC San Diego and former Director of the Gillin Sleep and Chronomedicine Research Center. A Past-President of the Sleep Research Society, she received the SRS Distinguished Scientist Award and AASM William C. Dement Academic Achievement Award for her research on sleep apnea, aging, and cancer.

univ_ucsd_shu_hong_zhu.gold: Shu-Hong Zhu is a Professor of Family Medicine and Public Health at UC San Diego. He directs the Center for Research and Intervention in Tobacco Control and has led the California Smokers' Helpline since 1992. He pioneered telephone-based quitlines, publishing a seminal 2002 New England Journal of Medicine study on their real-world effectiveness.

univ_ucsd_matthew_b_kennel.gold: Matthew B. Kennel is a researcher at the University of California San Diego’s Institute for Nonlinear Science. He co-developed the "false nearest neighbors" algorithm (1992), a standard method for determining embedding dimensions in phase-space reconstruction. His research focuses on nonlinear dynamics, pattern formation, and the estimation of Lyapunov exponents from experimental time-series data.

univ_ucsd_koyel_mitra.gold: Koyel Mitra is a bioinformatics researcher at the University of California, San Diego, specializing in systems biology and gene regulatory networks. She co-authored the influential review "Integrative approaches for finding modular structure in biological networks" in *Nature Reviews Genetics*. Her research includes developing Active Interaction Mapping (AIM) to model the hierarchical organization of autophagy.

univ_ucsd_adeleh_yarmohammadi.gold: Adeleh Yarmohammadi is an ophthalmologist and researcher who completed her residency and glaucoma fellowship at the UC San Diego Shiley Eye Institute. At the Hamilton Glaucoma Center, she investigated blood flow in glaucoma pathogenesis using OCT-Angiography. A former Chief Resident, she has published extensively on ocular imaging, retinal nerve fiber loss, and vascular biomarkers.

univ_ucsd_ak_n_ta_karao_lu.gold: Akın Taşçıkaraoğlu is an Associate Professor at Muğla Sıtkı Koçman University and a Visiting Professor at UC San Diego (2024–2025). He earned his Ph.D. from Yildiz Technical University and was a postdoctoral scholar at UC Berkeley. A 2021 TÜBA-GEBİP award recipient, he co-edited the book *Pathways to a Smarter Power System*.

univ_ucsd_jiun_shyan_chen.gold: Jiun-Shyan Chen is the William Prager Chair Professor and Distinguished Professor at UC San Diego, specializing in computational mechanics and meshfree methods. He founded the Center for Extreme Events Research and received the 2025 John von Neumann Medal and 2024 Raymond D. Mindlin Medal. Chen is a past president of USACM and EMI.

univ_ucsd_julie_onton.gold: An Associate Project Scientist at the Swartz Center for Neural Computation, Onton earned her Ph.D. in neuroscience from Tufts University. Specializing in Independent Component Analysis (ICA) of EEG data, she researches emotional imagery, meditation, and sleep. She identified a novel deep sleep stage, "Lo Deep," notably in military veterans with PTSD.

univ_ucsd_george_sakoulas.gold: An infectious diseases clinician at Sharp Rees-Stealy Medical Group, Sakoulas is a Professor at the UCSD School of Medicine. His research investigates the pharmacodynamics of antistaphylococcal antibiotics and their synergy with host defense peptides like cathelicidins. He co-authored a 2020 *Cell* study identifying proteomic biomarkers for *Staphylococcus aureus* bacteremia mortality.

univ_ucsd_emily_a_troyer.gold: Emily A. Troyer, MD, is an Associate Clinical Professor at the UC San Diego Department of Psychiatry and Director of the Community Psychiatry Research Center. Specializing in psychoneuroimmunology, she lead-authored a 2020 *Brain, Behavior, and Immunity* study examining the potential long-term neuropsychiatric sequelae of COVID-19 and its underlying immunologic mechanisms.

univ_ucsd_sherry_a_kempin.gold: Sherry A. Kempin is a plant molecular biologist at the University of California, San Diego, previously affiliated with Caltech. She is known for identifying the molecular basis of the cauliflower phenotype in *Arabidopsis*, demonstrating that the *CAULIFLOWER* and *APETALA1* genes redundantly control floral meristem identity. Her research also characterizes the *INDEHISCENT* gene's role in fruit patterning.

univ_ucsd_samuel_bazzi.gold: Samuel Bazzi is the Pacific Economic Cooperation Chair in International Economic Relations and Professor of Economics at UC San Diego. A 2020 NSF CAREER Award recipient, he co-edits the *Journal of Development Economics*. His research on "rugged individualism" and the causes of economic growth has appeared in *Econometrica* and the *American Economic Review*.

univ_ucsd_cory_m_root.gold: Cory M. Root is an Assistant Professor whose research investigates neural circuits underlying olfactory-driven behaviors. He authored a landmark 2014 *Nature* study on the cortical amygdala's role in innate odor responses and a 2011 *Cell* paper on neuropeptide signaling in *Drosophila*. A former Hellman Fellow, he earned his Ph.D. at UCSD and trained at Columbia.

univ_ucsd_davor_hrovat.gold: Davor Hrovat is an Adjunct Distinguished Professor at UC San Diego and a retired Henry Ford Technical Fellow. Elected to the National Academy of Engineering, he is a Fellow of IEEE, ASME, and IFAC. He received the 2023 Rufus Oldenburger Medal and holds over 110 patents in automotive control, vehicle dynamics, and vibration systems.

univ_ucsd_david_g_victor.gold: David G. Victor is a Distinguished Professor of Innovation and Public Policy at UC San Diego and co-director of the Deep Decarbonization Initiative. A former Stanford Law professor and convening lead author for the IPCC, he authored *Global Warming Gridlock*. He is a senior fellow at the Brookings Institution and holds a Ph.D. from MIT.

univ_ucsd_kaustuv_roy.gold: Kaustuv Roy is a Professor of Biological Sciences at the University of California, San Diego. A macroecologist, he developed the "Out of the Tropics" model to explain global biodiversity gradients. His research investigates anthropogenic impacts on coastal marine ecosystems, including historical body size declines in intertidal invertebrates and the evolution of molluscan microbiomes.

univ_ucsd_randolph_e_bank.gold: Randolph E. Bank is a Distinguished Professor of Mathematics Emeritus at UC San Diego and Co-Director of the Center for Computational Mathematics. A SIAM Fellow and Humboldt Research Award recipient, he developed the PLTMG software package for solving elliptic partial differential equations. His research specializes in adaptive finite element methods and multilevel iterative solvers.

univ_ucsd_dwayne_g_stupack.gold: Dwayne G. Stupack is a Professor of Obstetrics, Gynecology, and Reproductive Sciences at UC San Diego and Associate Director of Shared Resources at Moores Cancer Center. His research identifies how focal adhesion kinase (FAK) and caspase-8 regulate tumor metastasis and chemotherapy resistance in ovarian cancer. He co-leads the NIH-funded Cancer Therapeutics Training Program.

univ_ucsd_chaitanya_baru.gold: Chaitanya Baru is a Distinguished Scientist at the San Diego Supercomputer Center, UC San Diego. He serves as Senior Advisor in the NSF Technology, Innovation, and Partnerships Directorate and co-chaired the Harnessing the Data Revolution initiative. He directs the Center for Large-scale Data Systems Research and helped establish the TPCx-BB big data benchmark standard.

univ_ucsd_john_mongan.gold: John Mongan is a Professor of Radiology at UCSF who conducted protein structural dynamics research at UC San Diego. In the McCammon Group, he developed constant pH molecular dynamics and implicit solvent models. A co-author of the AMBER software, he created the Interactive Essential Dynamics tool and the CLAIM checklist for medical AI.

univ_uc_irvine_martha_s_feldman.gold: Martha S. Feldman is a Distinguished Professor Emerita at the University of California, Irvine, where she held the Johnson Chair for Civic Governance and Public Management. Renowned for pioneering routine dynamics, she received the 2015 Academy of Management Distinguished Scholar Award. Her influential works include "Organizational routines as a source of continuous change" (2000).

univ_uc_irvine_stanley_ng.gold: Stanley Ng is an Assistant Professor of Biological Chemistry at the University of California, Irvine. He led the development of the LSC17 score, a 17-gene expression signature that predicts chemotherapy response in acute myeloid leukemia. His research integrates machine learning and single-cell genomics to investigate leukemia stem cell regulatory networks and clonal evolution.

univ_uc_irvine_saurabh_sahar.gold: Saurabh Sahar is a researcher at the University of California, Irvine, specializing in the molecular links between circadian rhythms, metabolism, and cancer. Collaborating with Paolo Sassone-Corsi, he authored the influential *Nature Reviews Cancer* study "Metabolism and cancer: the circadian clock connection." His research examines how chromatin remodeling and melatonin signaling regulate the biological clock.

univ_uc_irvine_simone_chambers.gold: Professor and Chair of Political Science at the University of California, Irvine, Chambers specializes in deliberative democracy and the digital public sphere. A Columbia University Ph.D., she authored Reasonable Democracy (1996) and Contemporary Democratic Theory (2024). She is a Senior Adviser at THE NEW INSTITUTE and researches digital misinformation and democratic theory.

univ_uc_irvine_michael_k_jones.gold: Michael K. Jones is an Assistant Professor in Residence at the University of California, Irvine School of Medicine. His research investigates the molecular mechanisms of angiogenesis and gastrointestinal wound healing, specifically the effects of NSAIDs and cyclooxygenase (COX) inhibition on gastric mucosa. He earned his Ph.D. in Biochemistry and Molecular Biology from UC Santa Barbara.

univ_uc_irvine_douglas_a_granger.gold: Douglas A. Granger is Emeritus Chancellor’s Professor at the University of California, Irvine, and founding director of the Institute for Interdisciplinary Salivary Bioscience Research. A pioneer in salivary bioscience, he developed foundational methods for measuring cortisol and other analytes to study stress. He founded Salimetrics and SalivaBio and previously held faculty positions at Johns Hopkins.

univ_uc_irvine_jonathon_e_ericson.gold: Jonathon E. Ericson (1942–2009) was a professor and chair of Environmental Health Science and Policy at UC Irvine. Specializing in obsidian hydration dating and prehistoric exchange systems, he served as president of the Society for Archaeological Sciences and received the International Association for Obsidian Studies Lifetime Achievement Award in 1998.

univ_uc_irvine_carroll_seron.gold: Carroll Seron is Professor Emerita of Criminology, Law and Society at the University of California, Irvine. A former president of the Law and Society Association, she edited Law & Society Review and Law & Social Inquiry. Her research examines legal professions and institutions, notably in her book The Business of Practicing Law. She received the 2020 LSA Legacy Award.

univ_uc_irvine_bernard_c_k_choi.gold: Bernard C. K. Choi is the Director of the UC Irvine Beckman Laser Institute & Medical Clinic and a Professor of Biomedical Engineering and Surgery. He leads the Microvascular Therapeutics and Imaging Laboratory, specializing in biophotonics and optical imaging. A Fellow of SPIE and AIMBE, he earned his Ph.D. from the University of Texas at Austin.

univ_uc_irvine_mark_steyvers.gold: Professor and Chair of Cognitive Sciences at the University of California, Irvine, Steyvers co-developed the author-topic model and the Retrieving Effectively from Memory (REM) model. His research applies statistical machine learning to human memory, semantic networks, and human-AI collaboration. He received the APA New Investigator Award and is a Fellow of the Cognitive Science Society.

univ_uc_irvine_medha_m_pathak.gold: Medha M. Pathak is an Associate Professor of Physiology & Biophysics at the University of California, Irvine. A recipient of the NIH Director’s New Innovator Award, she specializes in mechanobiology, focusing on the Piezo1 ion channel's role in neural stem cell fate and erythrocyte homeostasis. She earned her PhD in Biophysics from UC Berkeley.

univ_uc_irvine_alan_l_goldin.gold: Alan L. Goldin, MD, PhD, is Professor Emeritus at the University of California, Irvine, and former director of its Medical Scientist Training Program. A Lucille P. Markey Scholar, he specializes in voltage-gated sodium channel function and its link to epilepsy, including Dravet Syndrome. His publications include "Resurgence of sodium channel research" and SCN1A mutation studies.

univ_uc_irvine_karen_s_rook.gold: Karen S. Rook is a Distinguished Professor Emerita of Psychological Science at the University of California, Irvine. She specializes in the dynamics of social relationships and health in aging, particularly the impact of positive and negative exchanges. A former department chair, she received the 2018 Baltes Distinguished Research Award from the American Psychological Association.

univ_uc_irvine_daniela_a_bota.gold: Daniela A. Bota is a Professor of Neurology and Vice Dean for Clinical Research at the University of California, Irvine. She directs the UCI Alpha Stem Cell Clinic and the Comprehensive Brain Tumor Program. Her research examines mitochondrial homeostasis, focusing on LonP1 and MAGMAS proteins to develop glioblastoma therapies and mitigate chemotherapy-induced cognitive dysfunction.

univ_uc_irvine_michael_b_dillencourt.gold: Michael B. Dillencourt is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. He earned his PhD from the University of Maryland, College Park. His research focuses on algorithm design, computational geometry, and graph theory. He co-authored a seminal paper on connected-component labeling, a fundamental technique in digital image processing and representation.

univ_uc_irvine_lowell_o_randall.gold: Lowell O. Randall (1910–2005) was the Director of Pharmacology Research at Hoffmann-La Roche, where he led the development of Librium and Valium. He co-developed the Randall-Selitto test to measure analgesic effects on inflamed tissue. Post-retirement, he maintained a laboratory at the University of California, Irvine, focusing on inflammatory mediators and pharmacology.

univ_uc_irvine_b_l_mcnaughton.gold: Bruce L. McNaughton is a Distinguished Professor of Neurobiology and Behavior at the University of California, Irvine. A Fellow of the Royal Society of Canada, he pioneered tetrode recording technology for large-scale neuronal monitoring. His research established the biological basis of Hebbian learning and explores hippocampal-cortical interactions, memory replay during sleep, and spatial orientation mechanisms.

univ_uc_irvine_janos_lanyi.gold: Janos K. Lanyi is Professor Emeritus of Physiology and Biophysics at the University of California, Irvine. He earned a Ph.D. from Harvard and was a research scientist at NASA-Ames Research Center. Renowned for his work on bacteriorhodopsin, he chaired UCI’s Department of Physiology and Biophysics (1995–2005) and investigates the molecular mechanisms of ion translocation.

univ_uc_irvine_nancy_tyler_burley.gold: Nancy Tyler Burley is a Professor of Ecology and Evolutionary Biology at the University of California, Irvine. Specializing in avian sexual selection and social monogamy, she established the "differential allocation" and "differential access" hypotheses. She is widely known for her experiments using colored leg bands on zebra finches to study mate choice and sex-ratio manipulation.

univ_uc_irvine_faysal_a_yafi.gold: Faysal A. Yafi is an Associate Professor and Director of Men’s Health at the University of California, Irvine. He is Secretary-elect of the Sexual Medicine Society of North America and Editor-in-Chief of the Video Journal of Sexual Medicine. He specializes in erectile dysfunction and Peyronie’s disease, leading the first U.S. Vertica device clinical trial.

univ_uc_irvine_gregory_hickok.gold: Gregory Hickok is a Distinguished Professor and Chair of Language Science at UC Irvine. He co-developed the Hickok-Poeppel dual-stream model of speech processing and authored *The Myth of Mirror Neurons*. His research investigates the neural basis of rhythm in speech and music. He was founding president of the Society for the Neurobiology of Language.

univ_uc_irvine_rollin_k_daniel.gold: Rollin K. Daniel is a Clinical Professor of Plastic Surgery at the University of California, Irvine. A world-renowned expert in rhinoplasty, he co-founded The Rhinoplasty Society and authored the textbook Aesthetic Rhinoplasty. His academic research encompasses systemic sclerosis and microvascular surgery, with a focus on preservation rhinoplasty and the surgical management of complex nasal deformities.

univ_uc_irvine_matt_law.gold: A Professor of Chemistry at the University of California, Irvine, Law specializes in nanomaterials for solar energy conversion. He earned his Ph.D. from UC Berkeley and was a postdoctoral fellow at the National Renewable Energy Laboratory. His work includes quantum dot solar cells and iron pyrite photovoltaics. He received the IUPAC Prize for Young Chemists.

univ_uc_irvine_elysia_poggi_davis.gold: Elysia Poggi Davis is a Professor of Psychology at the University of Denver and faculty at the University of California, Irvine. She directs the Neurodevelopmental Research Program, investigating how prenatal stress and cortisol influence fetal and infant brain development. Her NIH-funded research explores the impact of early-life environments on long-term mental and physical health.

univ_uc_irvine_harry_t_haigler.gold: Harry T. Haigler (1951–2026) was a Professor of Physiology and Biophysics and Associate Dean at the University of California, Irvine. A cell biologist, he specialized in epidermal growth factor signal transduction and annexin calcium-binding proteins. He was instrumental in characterizing the annexin family and their roles in cellular signaling, membrane dynamics, and growth regulation.

univ_uc_irvine_r_w_taft.gold: Robert W. Taft (1922–1996) was a professor and founding faculty member of the UC Irvine chemistry department. He developed the Taft equation to quantify steric and polar substituent effects and co-created the Kamlet-Taft solvatochromic parameters. He edited Progress in Physical Organic Chemistry and established linear free energy relationships fundamental to antioxidant and radical chemistry.

univ_uc_irvine_elizabeth_r_jarvo.gold: Elizabeth R. Jarvo is a Chancellor’s Professor of Chemistry at UC Irvine and a 2025 AAAS Fellow. Her research focuses on nickel-catalyzed stereoselective cross-coupling and organometallic catalysis. She earned her Ph.D. from Boston College and completed postdoctoral training at Harvard. Her honors include the NSF CAREER and ACS WCC Rising Star awards.

univ_uc_irvine_catherine_c_coombs.gold: Catherine C. Coombs is an Associate Professor at the University of California, Irvine, specializing in acute myeloid leukemia and chronic lymphocytic leukemia. An investigator for the BRUIN trial, she previously led the CLL program at UNC Chapel Hill. She earned her MD from the University of Cincinnati and trained at Duke and Memorial Sloan Kettering.

univ_uc_irvine_hope_jensen_schau.gold: Hope Jensen Schau is the Gilly Family Endowed Chair and Professor of Marketing at UC Irvine’s Paul Merage School of Business. Her research focuses on brand communities, consumer identity, and collaborative value creation, notably in her work "How brand community practices create value." She previously led the Filene Research Institute’s Center of Excellence for Consumer Decision Making.

univ_uc_irvine_s_h_i_ou.gold: Sai-Hong Ignatius Ou is a Clinical Professor at the University of California, Irvine. In HIV research, he is distinguished for cloning and characterizing TDP-43, a protein that binds to the HIV-1 TAR DNA element to regulate gene expression. He is also a leading thoracic oncologist specializing in targeted lung cancer therapies.

univ_uc_irvine_deborah_lowe_vandell.gold: Founding Dean of the University of California, Irvine School of Education, Vandell is a Chancellor’s Professor Emerita. As a principal investigator for the NICHD Study of Early Child Care and Youth Development, she researches the long-term impacts of early education and after-school programs. She is a member of the National Academy of Education.

univ_uc_irvine_alessandro_lusci.gold: Alessandro Lusci is a researcher specializing in chemoinformatics and computational drug discovery. At the University of California, Irvine, he collaborated with Pierre Baldi to apply deep learning to molecular property prediction. He co-authored the 2013 study "Deep architectures and deep learning in chemoinformatics," which developed neural network methods to predict the aqueous solubility of drug-like molecules.

univ_uc_irvine_william_h_batchelder.gold: William H. Batchelder (1940–2018) was a Professor of Cognitive Sciences at the University of California, Irvine, where he co-founded the Department of Cognitive Sciences. A pioneer in mathematical psychology, he developed Multinomial Processing Tree (MPT) models and Cultural Consensus Theory. He also served as President of the Society for Mathematical Psychology.

univ_uc_irvine_r_h_liebeck.gold: Robert H. Liebeck was a Boeing Senior Fellow and pioneer in aerodynamics, best known for developing high-lift Liebeck airfoils and leading the Blended Wing Body (BWB) aircraft program. A National Academy of Engineering member, he received the 2010 Daniel Guggenheim Medal and designed aerodynamic components for Formula One cars and America’s Cup-winning yachts.

univ_uc_irvine_diana_glovaci.gold: Diana Glovaci is a cardiologist and researcher affiliated with the Heart Disease Prevention Program at the University of California, Irvine. Her research focuses on the epidemiology of diabetes mellitus and cardiovascular risk stratification. She authored "Epidemiology of Diabetes Mellitus and Cardiovascular Disease" (2019) and investigated global CVD risk and lipoprotein-cholesterol goal achievement in diabetic populations.

univ_uc_irvine_irene_m_pedersen.gold: Irene Munk Pedersen is an Associate Professor at the Scintillon Institute and former faculty at UC Irvine. She researches microRNA regulation in cancer and neurological diseases, notably identifying miR-128 as an antiviral mediator and miR-155’s role in B-cell lymphoma. She earned her PhD from the Burnham Institute and received an NIH Pathway to Independence award.

univ_uc_irvine_wenqi_wang.gold: Wenqi Wang is a Professor of Developmental and Cell Biology at UC Irvine. He earned his Ph.D. from the Shanghai Institute for Biochemistry and Cell Biology. His research investigates Hippo pathway regulation and YAP/TAZ signaling, notably discovering phosphatidic acid-mediated lipid-protein interactions and the pathway's noncanonical role in driving autophagy during energy stress.

univ_uc_irvine_claire_henchcliffe.gold: Claire Henchcliffe is the Stanley van den Noort Professor and Chair of Neurology at the University of California, Irvine. An Oxford-educated researcher, she was an investigator in the QE3 Phase III clinical trial of high-dose Coenzyme Q10 for Parkinson’s disease. Her work focuses on stem cell-based regenerative therapies and gene therapy for movement disorders.

univ_uc_irvine_stergios_skaperdas.gold: Stergios Skaperdas is a Professor of Economics and Clifford S. Heinz Chair at the University of California, Irvine. He directs the Center for Global Peace and Conflict Studies and advises the Experimental Social Science Laboratory. His research examines the political economy of conflict, appropriation, and state emergence, featuring works like "Contest Success Functions."

univ_uc_irvine_timothy_s_kern.gold: Timothy S. Kern is a Professor of Ophthalmology at the University of California, Irvine, and a member of the Brunson Center for Translational Vision Research. He investigates the pathogenesis of diabetic retinopathy, focusing on photoreceptors and Advanced Glycation End Products. In 2023, he received the Gavin Herbert Eye Institute Lifetime Research Achievement Award.

univ_uc_irvine_chuansheng_chen.gold: Chuansheng Chen is a Distinguished Professor of Psychological Science and Education at the University of California, Irvine. A University of Michigan alumnus, he specializes in cross-cultural psychology, cognitive neuroscience, and behavior genetics. Named a Chancellor’s Professor in 2014, his research examines cultural variations in developmental trajectories and the relations among genes, brain, and behavior.

univ_uc_irvine_vibhanshu_abhishek.gold: Vibhanshu Abhishek is an Associate Professor of Information Systems at the University of California, Irvine. He earned a PhD from the Wharton School and a B.Tech from IIT Kanpur. His research focuses on multi-channel retail and digital advertising. He is the co-founder of Alltius and was named a 2019 Marketing Science Institute Young Scholar.

univ_uc_irvine_ilona_s_yim.gold: Ilona S. Yim is a Professor of Psychological Science at UC Irvine and director of the Maternal Psychobiology and Women’s Health Research (MPWR) Lab. She earned her Ph.D. from the University of Trier. Her research identifies biological markers for postpartum depression, notably placental corticotropin-releasing hormone and prenatal beta-endorphin as early predictors.

univ_uc_irvine_gerardo_a_okhuysen.gold: Gerardo A. Okhuysen is a Professor of Organization and Management at the University of California, Irvine’s Paul Merage School of Business. He earned his PhD from Stanford University. His research examines group dynamics, team performance, and organizational routines, with work published in the Academy of Management Journal, Academy of Management Review, and Organization Science.

univ_uc_irvine_travis_e_huxman.gold: Travis E. Huxman is Professor and Chair of Ecology and Evolutionary Biology at UC Irvine. He directs the Center for Environmental Biology and the Steele/Burnand Anza-Borrego Desert Research Center. Formerly Director of Biosphere 2, his research focuses on climate-ecosystem interactions and the coupling of carbon and water cycles in arid landscapes.

univ_uc_irvine_valerie_olson.gold: An environmental anthropologist and Associate Professor at the University of California, Irvine, Olson researches large-scale sociocultural systems and human-animal relations. She authored *Into the Extreme: U.S. Environmental Systems and Politics Beyond Earth* (2018) and co-authored *The Ethnographer’s Way* (2024). Her work examines the ecobiopolitics of space exploration, marine restoration, and reproductive cryoconservation.

univ_uc_irvine_alys_caitlyn_thomas.gold: Alys Caitlyn Thomas earned her PhD in Earth System Science from the University of California, Irvine in 2014. Her research utilizes GRACE satellite gravity data to analyze hydrological drought and water storage deficits. A former NASA Graduate Student Researchers Program fellow, she has contributed to geophysics research and later joined the Department of Homeland Security.

univ_uc_irvine_edward_telles.gold: A Distinguished Professor of Sociology and member of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, Telles authored the award-winning *Race in Another America* and *Pigmentocracies*. He previously held professorships at Princeton and UCLA and led the Project on Ethnicity and Race in Latin America (PERLA). His research extensively examines Mexican American integration and mobility.

univ_uc_irvine_omid_mazdiyasni.gold: Omid Mazdiyasni is a researcher at the University of California, Irvine, specializing in hydrology and hydroclimatology. His research focuses on concurrent climate extremes, notably droughts and heatwaves. He co-authored high-impact studies in *PNAS* and *Nature Communications* regarding anthropogenic impacts on global drought and the increasing frequency of compound hot-dry events in the United States.

univ_uc_irvine_stefan_o_ciurea.gold: Stefan O. Ciurea is the Director of the Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplantation and Cellular Therapy Program at UC Irvine. Previously a faculty member at MD Anderson Cancer Center, he specializes in haploidentical transplantation and adoptive NK cell therapy. He has authored over 150 publications and earned his MD from Grigore T. Popa University in Romania.

univ_uc_irvine_dewayne_p_williams.gold: DeWayne P. Williams is an Assistant Professor of Psychological Science at the University of California, Irvine. His research in psychophysiology examines health disparities, self-regulation, and heart rate variability. He investigates the links between social stressors, inflammation, and adiposity, studying how physical activity and diet influence cardiovascular health, particularly in African American and female populations.

univ_uc_irvine_jan_k_brueckner.gold: Jan K. Brueckner is a Distinguished Professor of Economics Emeritus at the University of California, Irvine. A Stanford PhD, he authored the textbook *Lectures on Urban Economics* and edited the *Journal of Urban Economics* for 16 years. His research focuses on urban and public economics, real estate finance, and the economics of the airline industry.

univ_uc_irvine_han_li.gold: Han Li is an Associate Professor of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering at UC Irvine. She develops non-canonical redox cofactors and synthetic metabolic pathways for biomanufacturing. A 2021 Sloan Research Fellow and 2023 Blavatnik National Award finalist, she received the NIH New Innovator and NSF CAREER awards. She earned her Ph.D. from UCLA in 2013.

univ_uc_irvine_dion_khodagholy.gold: Dion Khodagholy is the Henry Samueli Faculty Excellence Professor at the University of California, Irvine. Formerly at Columbia University, he directs the Translational Neuroelectronics Lab. He is known for developing the NeuroGrid, a high-density organic electrode array, and organic electrochemical transistors for biocompatible neural interfaces. He holds a PhD from École des Mines.

univ_uc_irvine_kirk_r_williams.gold: Kirk R. Williams is Professor Emeritus of Criminology, Law and Society at the University of California, Irvine. Formerly Chair of Sociology and Criminal Justice at the University of Delaware, he specializes in youth violence and intimate partner violence. He developed the Domestic Violence Screening Instrument–Revised (DVSI-R) and co-authored a seminal meta-analysis on predictors of childhood bullying.

univ_uc_irvine_martin_brandon.gold: Martin Brandon is a researcher at the University of California, Irvine’s Center for Molecular and Mitochondrial Medicine and Genetics. Collaborating with Douglas C. Wallace, he co-developed MITOMASTER and contributed to the MITOMAP human mitochondrial genome database. His research focuses on mitochondrial mutations in cancer and the role of mtDNA variation in human evolutionary adaptation.

univ_uc_irvine_yun_wang.gold: Yun Wang is a Professor of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering at the University of California, Irvine. An ASME and RSC Fellow, he specializes in polymer electrolyte membrane (PEM) fuel cells and multi-phase flow. He authored *PEM Fuel Cell Water and Thermal Management Fundamentals* and received the OCEC President’s Award and Outstanding Engineering Merit Award.

univ_uc_irvine_j_wolff.gold: J. Wolff is a researcher associated with the University of California, Irvine, specializing in neurological disorders. Wolff has contributed to clinical research on neuromuscular conditions, including amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) and spinal muscular atrophy (SMA), collaborating on trials evaluating treatments such as reldesemtiv. Wolff also participates in initiatives like the national honor society in neuroscience.

univ_uc_irvine_mathew_blurton_jones.gold: Mathew Blurton-Jones is a Professor of Neurobiology and Behavior at the University of California, Irvine, where he directs the ADRC iPS cell and Stem Cell CRISPR cores. He pioneered methods for differentiating human induced pluripotent stem cells into microglia and utilizes chimeric mouse models to investigate molecular mechanisms of Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s diseases.

univ_uc_irvine_ioan_andricioaei.gold: Ioan Andricioaei is a Professor of Chemistry at the University of California, Irvine. He earned his Ph.D. from Boston University in 1999 and conducted postdoctoral research at Harvard. A recipient of the NSF CAREER Award, he develops theoretical techniques, including stochastic path integral formalisms, to simulate the dynamics and thermodynamics of proteins, DNA, and RNA.

univ_uc_irvine_norman_m_weinberger.gold: Norman M. Weinberger (1935–2016) was a founding faculty member at the University of California, Irvine, and co-founder of the Center for the Neurobiology of Learning and Memory. A pioneer in auditory neuroplasticity, he demonstrated that the primary auditory cortex stores specific memory traces and that memories can be created through nucleus basalis stimulation.

univ_uc_irvine_raymond_w_novaco.gold: Raymond W. Novaco is a Professor of Psychological Science at the University of California, Irvine. He pioneered stress inoculation training for anger management and developed the widely used Novaco Anger Scale (NAS). His research focuses on the assessment and therapeutic regulation of anger and aggression, particularly in forensic settings and among individuals with histories of violence.

univ_uc_irvine_kurt_squire.gold: Kurt Squire is a Professor of Informatics at the University of California, Irvine, and co-director of the Games + Learning + Society Center. Formerly at the University of Wisconsin–Madison, he authored *Video Games and Learning* and *Making Games for Impact*. His research focuses on game-based learning, participatory environments, and educational software design.

univ_uc_irvine_fred_fung.gold: Fred Fung is a Clinical Professor (Voluntary) at the University of California, Irvine School of Medicine’s Division of Occupational and Environmental Medicine. A specialist in toxicology and epidemiology, he researches innovative food safety technologies and hygiene. He co-authored "Food safety in the 21st century" and collaborates with Menon Biosensors on rapid pathogen detection systems.

univ_uc_irvine_surya_m_nauli.gold: Surya M. Nauli is a Professor at Chapman University and the University of California, Irvine. He earned a PhD from Loma Linda University and trained at Harvard Medical School. His research focuses on primary cilia and polycystic kidney disease, notably discovering that polycystins 1 and 2 mediate mechanosensation in the primary cilium of kidney cells.

univ_uc_irvine_donald_j_patterson.gold: Donald J. Patterson is a project scientist and former tenured professor at the University of California, Irvine. A recipient of the UBICOMP 10-year impact award, he specializes in ubiquitous computing, localization, and "collapse informatics." He co-founded the fintech startup Blockpliance and currently serves as VP of Machine Learning and AI at LedgerLink.

univ_uc_irvine_judith_borghouts.gold: Judith Borghouts is an Associate Specialist at the University of California, Irvine’s iTEQC Research Program. She earned her PhD in Human-Computer Interaction from University College London. Her research focuses on user engagement and barriers to digital mental health interventions, notably lead-authoring a 2021 systematic review on factors influencing the uptake of these technologies.

univ_uc_irvine_david_john_frank.gold: David John Frank is a Professor and Chair of Sociology at the University of California, Irvine. His research analyzes the global institutionalization of environmental protection, biodiversity, and resource management. He co-authored *The University and the Global Knowledge Society* (2020) and *Reconstructing the University* (2006), and received the American Sociological Association’s Best Scholarly Article Award.

univ_uc_irvine_elizabeth_d_pe_a.gold: Elizabeth D. Peña is a Professor at UC Irvine and former George Christian Centennial Professor at UT Austin. A Fellow of the American Speech-Language-Hearing Association, she received its 2024 Honors of the Association. She developed the Bilingual English-Spanish Assessment (BESA) and the Test of English Language Learners (TELL), pioneering dynamic assessment methods.

univ_uc_irvine_edwin_amenta.gold: Edwin Amenta is a Professor of Sociology and Political Science at the University of California, Irvine. He authored the award-winning *Bold Relief: Institutional Politics and the Origins of Modern American Social Policy* and *When Movements Matter*. Known for developing political mediation theory, his research examines the historical development of U.S. social policy and social movements.

univ_uc_irvine_hamid_jafarkhani.gold: Hamid Jafarkhani is a Distinguished Professor at UC Irvine and a primary inventor of space-time block coding. He authored *Space-Time Coding: Theory and Practice* and is an IEEE, AAAS, and NAI Fellow. His honors include the IEEE Eric E. Sumner Award and the IEEE Marconi Prize Paper Award for contributions to MIMO technology.

univ_uc_irvine_eric_t_swanson.gold: Eric T. Swanson is a Professor of Economics at UC Irvine and Editor of the Journal of Monetary Economics. Formerly a Senior Research Advisor at the Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco, he specializes in unconventional monetary policy. He received the 2022 JME Best Paper Prize for his work on forward guidance and asset purchases.

univ_uc_irvine_dimitri_papamoschou.gold: Dimitri Papamoschou is a Chancellor’s Professor of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering at the University of California, Irvine. He is recognized for developing the convective Mach number concept to characterize compressible shear layers. An AIAA Fellow, he received the 2017 AIAA Aeroacoustics Award for his contributions to supersonic jet noise reduction and aeroacoustics.

univ_uc_irvine_hamed_ashouri.gold: Hamed Ashouri is a researcher at the University of California, Irvine’s Center for Hydrometeorology and Remote Sensing. He developed PERSIANN-CDR, a global satellite-based precipitation climate data record. A recipient of the NASA Earth and Space Science Fellowship, he also created the CHRS RainSphere visualization tool and earned his Ph.D. from UCI in 2014.

univ_uc_irvine_frank_l_meyskens.gold: Frank L. Meyskens Jr. is a Professor and Director Emeritus of the Chao Family Comprehensive Cancer Center at the University of California, Irvine. A pioneer in cancer chemoprevention, he holds the Daniel G. Aldrich Jr. Endowed Chair. He received the AACR Prevent Cancer Foundation Award and the ASPO Distinguished Achievement Award for his clinical research.

oa_author_marco_cuturi.gold: A Distinguished Engineer at Apple ML Research and Professor of Statistics at ENSAE, Cuturi was formerly an Associate Professor at Kyoto University. He is a leading researcher in machine learning, renowned for his pioneering work on computational optimal transport and the Sinkhorn algorithm. He previously held research positions at Google Brain and Princeton University.

univ_uc_irvine_dan_m_cooper.gold: Dan M. Cooper is an academic researcher at the University of California, Irvine, specializing in foot and ankle surgery. His work within the university's medical and research framework focuses on clinical and surgical advancements in orthopaedic care. He is affiliated with the UCI School of Medicine and contributes to the field through specialized surgical research.

univ_uc_irvine_john_r_crawford.gold: John R. Crawford is a Professor of Psychology at the University of Aberdeen. He is a prominent psychometrician known for developing and validating the Depression Anxiety Stress Scales (DASS-21) and providing normative data for the Satisfaction with Life Scale (SWLS). His research focuses on quantitative methods in neuropsychology and the assessment of psychological well-being.

oa_author_gerry_tsoukalas.gold: Gerry Tsoukalas is a Senior Fellow at the Wharton School and a Professor at Boston University. He co-founded the Crypto and Blockchain Economics Research (CBER) Forum and was named to the 2025 Thinkers50 Radar. Previously a Morgan Stanley trader, he holds a PhD from Stanford and serves as an Associate Editor for Management Science.

oa_author_cludio_r__mirasso.gold: Cláudio R. Mirasso is a Full Professor at the Universitat de les Illes Balears and researcher at the Institute for Cross-Disciplinary Physics and Complex Systems (IFISC). He pioneered delay-based reservoir computing, co-authoring a landmark Nature Communications study on its experimental implementation. His work explores zero-lag synchronization in neural circuits and neuro-inspired information processing.

oa_author_theo_gevers.gold: Theo Gevers is a Professor of Computer Vision at the University of Amsterdam and a VICI laureate. He directs the Computer Vision Lab and co-directs the Atlas and Delta Labs. A co-founder of Sightcorp and 3DUniversum, he authored *Color in Computer Vision* and was general chair of the 2016 European Conference on Computer Vision.

oa_author_jan_drugowitsch.gold: Jan Drugowitsch is an Associate Professor of Neurobiology at Harvard Medical School and a former researcher at the University of Geneva. He leads a lab specializing in computational neuroscience, focusing on normative models of decision-making under uncertainty. He earned his PhD from the University of Bath and investigates how neural circuits implement Bayesian inference and evidence accumulation.

oa_author_brigitta_sipcz.gold: Brigitta Sipőcz is a Research Software Engineer and astronomer at the University of Cambridge’s Institute of Astronomy. A core maintainer and lead release coordinator for the Astropy Project, she maintains the `astroquery`, `photutils`, and `astroML` packages. She is a Software Sustainability Institute fellow specializing in open-source scientific software and data-intensive astrophysics.

oa_author_abdolreza_hatamlou.gold: Abdolreza Hatamlou developed the Black Hole Algorithm (BHA) in 2013, a nature-inspired metaheuristic for data clustering and global optimization. He co-created the Multi-Verse Optimizer (MVO) and has authored highly cited research on gravitational search algorithms, hybrid clustering methods, and the application of heuristic approaches to NP-hard problems like the knapsack and traveling salesman problems.

oa_author_ilya_alexandrovich.gold: Ilya Alexandrovich is a security researcher at Intel Labs. He co-authored the foundational 2013 paper "Innovative Instructions and Software Model for Isolated Execution," which introduced Intel Software Guard Extensions (SGX). His research includes pre-silicon vulnerability discovery (TEESec), side-channel attack mitigations like AEX-Notify, and the MedPerf federated benchmarking platform for medical AI.

oa_author_tlin_ztrk.gold: Tülin Öztürk is a radiology researcher at Elazığ Eğitim ve Araştırma Hastanesi. She co-developed DarkCovidNet, a deep learning model for automated COVID-19 detection from chest X-ray images. Utilizing the YOLO real-time object detection system, her research achieved 98.08% accuracy in binary classification, providing a significant auxiliary diagnostic tool for medical imaging during the pandemic.

oa_author_paul_m__leonardi.gold: Paul M. Leonardi is the Duca Family Professor and Chair of Technology Management at UC Santa Barbara. He founded the university’s Master of Technology Management program and holds a PhD from Stanford University. A Fellow of the Academy of Management, he authored *The Digital Mindset* and *Digital Exhaustion*, researching organizational networks and knowledge sharing.

oa_author_marco_gruteser.gold: Marco Gruteser is a Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering at Rutgers University and a Research Scientist at Google. An ACM Distinguished Scientist and NSF CAREER awardee, he pioneered location privacy and co-authored the foundational paper "Minimizing Age of Information in Vehicular Networks." His research spans mobile computing, wireless networking, and connected vehicle systems.

oa_author_xuejun_yang.gold: Xuejun Yang earned his PhD from the University of Utah in 2015. He co-developed **Csmith**, a randomized test-case generator for C compilers. He was the lead author of the PLDI 2011 award-winning paper "Finding and Understanding Bugs in C Compilers" and co-authored the 2012 award paper "Test-Case Reduction for C Compiler Bugs."

oa_author_douard_grave.gold: Édouard Grave is an AI researcher and former Research Scientist at Meta. He co-created the **fastText** library and co-authored the **LLaMA** foundation model paper. A co-founder of **Mistral AI**, he is currently a research lead at **Kyutai**. His work includes unsupervised learning and topic modeling, such as the **Unsupervised Phenome Model** for clinical data.

oa_author_hannah_rashkin.gold: Hannah Rashkin is a Research Scientist at Google DeepMind and a former researcher at the Allen Institute for Artificial Intelligence. Specializing in topic modeling and commonsense reasoning, she co-developed the SocialIQA benchmark and the Grover fake news detector. She holds a PhD from the University of Washington, where she was advised by Yejin Choi.

oa_author_zhiqin_chen.gold: Zhiqin Chen is a research scientist at Adobe Research who received the 2024 Governor General’s Gold Medal and Alain Fournier Dissertation Award. His work on BSP-Net won the CVPR 2020 Best Student Paper Award. He developed IM-Net and BAE-NET, pioneering neural implicit representations for 3D reconstruction and generative shape synthesis.

oa_author_murtaza_haider.gold: Murtaza Haider is a professor of Data Science and Real Estate Management at Toronto Metropolitan University and research director of the Urban Analytics Institute. He authored *Getting Started with Data Science* (2016) and *Real Estate Markets* (2020). A syndicated Postmedia columnist, he holds a PhD in Civil Engineering from the University of Toronto.

oa_author_haozhi_qi.gold: Haozhi Qi is a researcher who co-developed Deformable Convolutional Networks and won the 2016 MS COCO segmentation challenge during his tenure at Microsoft Research Asia. He earned his Ph.D. from UC Berkeley, specializing in multisensory robotic dexterity. His work on visuo-tactile perception was featured as a cover article in Science Robotics.

oa_author_timnit_gebru.gold: Timnit Gebru is a computer scientist and founder of the Distributed Artificial Intelligence Research Institute (DAIR). She co-founded Black in AI and formerly co-led Google’s Ethical AI team. Her research includes visual computational sociology and topic modeling for urban characterization. She is widely known for co-authoring the influential "Gender Shades" and "Stochastic Parrots" papers.

oa_author_maciek_chociej.gold: Maciek Chociej is a researcher and software engineer who worked on OpenAI’s robotics team. He co-authored key papers on learning dexterous in-hand manipulation and solving the Rubik’s Cube with a humanoid robot hand. He developed the OpenAI Remote Rendering Backend (ORRB) and currently works at Meta, specializing in large-scale machine learning and physics simulators.

oa_author_xavier_glorot.gold: Xavier Glorot is a computer scientist who developed the Xavier initialization to stabilize deep neural network training. He co-authored the foundational 2011 paper on deep sparse rectifier neural networks, which popularized Rectified Linear Units (ReLU). His research also includes work on domain adaptation and contractive auto-encoders. He is a research scientist at Google DeepMind.

oa_author_oussema_souiai.gold: Oussema Souiai is a Maître assistant at the Institut Pasteur de Tunis, affiliated with the Laboratory of Bioinformatics, Biomathematics and Biostatistics (BIMS). A member of the H3ABioNet Consortium, he specializes in genomic networks, NGS, and microbiome analysis. His research includes the Genome Tunisia Project and studies on SARS-CoV-2 recombination and avian influenza virus evolution.

oa_author_gang_song.gold: Gang Song is a researcher at the University of Pennsylvania’s Penn Image Computing and Science Laboratory (PICSL). He is an originator and core developer of the Advanced Normalization Tools (ANTs) software package. His work focuses on medical image registration and segmentation, specifically developing diffeomorphic transformation models and similarity metrics for neuroimaging and thoracic CT analysis.

oa_author_lifeng_wu.gold: Lifeng Wu is a researcher at the Nanchang Institute of Technology’s School of Soil and Water Conservation. He specializes in plant hydraulics, soil water dynamics, and carbon-water interface modeling. His work includes forecasting reference evapotranspiration and drought using machine learning, as well as investigating submerged plant restoration for carbon sequestration and eutrophication control.

oa_author_ahmed_zoha.gold: Ahmed Zoha is an Associate Professor at the University of Glasgow who earned his PhD from the University of Surrey. Specializing in smart grid energy management and Non-Intrusive Load Monitoring (NILM), he has received two IEEE Best Paper awards. He previously served as a lead research scientist at the Qatar Mobility Innovation Center.

oa_author_marco_dambros.gold: Marco D’Ambros is the director of CodeLounge at the Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) Software Institute. After completing his PhD at USI in 2010, he spent eight years as a technical lead at Palantir Technologies. He received the MSR 2020 Most Influential Paper Award for his foundational research on bug prediction.

oa_author_zongwei_zhou.gold: Zongwei Zhou is an Assistant Research Professor at Johns Hopkins University who earned his PhD from Arizona State University. He is best known for developing UNet++, a widely adopted neural network architecture for medical image segmentation. His research focuses on early cancer detection, earning him the ASU President’s Award for Innovation and the MICCAI Young Scientist Award.

oa_author_ye_yuan.gold: Ye Yuan is a Research Scientist at CMU’s Robotics Institute and a Senior Research Scientist at NVIDIA Research. He earned his Ph.D. from CMU in 2022. His research in advanced neural network applications for computer vision and embodied AI includes developing AgentFormer, DLow, and PhysDiff. He received a SIGGRAPH 2023 Best Paper Honorable Mention.

oa_author_pavlo_o__dral.gold: Pavlo O. Dral is a Full Professor at Xiamen University and a former postdoctoral researcher at the Max-Planck-Institut für Kohlenforschung. He specializes in AI-enhanced quantum chemistry, developing the AIQM1 and AIQM2 methods. Dral founded the MLatom software package and co-founded the Xiamen Atomistic Computing Suite. He won a 2004 International Chemistry Olympiad gold medal.

oa_author_maite_taboada.gold: Maite Taboada is a Distinguished SFU Professor and Director of the Discourse Processing Lab. A Fellow of the Royal Society of Canada, she received the 2021 ACL Test of Time Award for her work on lexicon-based sentiment analysis. She co-developed the Gender Gap Tracker and researches computational linguistics, discourse analysis, and the detection of misinformation.

oa_author_koutarou_matsumoto.gold: Koutarou Matsumoto is an Assistant Professor at Kurume University’s Biostatistics Center. He develops machine learning models for clinical applications, including the prediction of delirium and candidemia using electronic medical record data. His research focuses on model interpretability through SHAP analysis, aiming to integrate explainable AI into learning health systems to support medical decision-making.

oa_author_zhirong_wu.gold: Zhirong Wu is a Senior Researcher at Microsoft Research Asia and a former postdoctoral scholar at UC Berkeley. He specializes in unsupervised feature learning, domain adaptation, and few-shot recognition. He is known for developing non-parametric instance discrimination and co-authoring "Improving Generalization via Scalable Neighborhood Component Analysis." He holds a Ph.D. from the Chinese University of Hong Kong.

oa_author_weidi_xie.gold: Weidi Xie is a Visiting Researcher at the University of Oxford’s Visual Geometry Group, where he earned his DPhil and served as a Senior Research Fellow. He co-developed the VGGFace2 dataset for face recognition and the VoxCeleb speaker verification dataset. His research focuses on computer vision, self-supervised learning, and multimodal analysis.

oa_author_sidney_k__dmello.gold: Sidney K. D’Mello is a Professor of Computer Science and Cognitive Science at the University of Colorado Boulder, formerly at the University of Notre Dame. He directs the NSF National Institute for Student-Agent Teaming (iSAT) and co-edited *The Oxford Handbook of Affective Computing*. His work includes developing the Affective AutoTutor and Gaze Tutor systems.

oa_author_wei_peng.gold: Wei Peng is an Associate Professor at RMIT University and a former Senior Lecturer at La Trobe University. Specializing in network security and industrial AI, he co-authored a major survey on SDN-based intrusion detection. He previously served as Director and Principal AI Scientist at Huawei and held senior roles at Telstra, Lenovo, and AUSTRAC.

oa_author_deb_roy.gold: Deb Roy is a Professor of Media Arts and Sciences at MIT and Director of the Center for Constructive Communication. He co-authored the 2018 *Science* cover study on the spread of false news on Twitter. Formerly Twitter’s Chief Media Scientist and Executive Director of the MIT Media Lab, he co-founded Bluefin Labs and Cortico.

oa_author_norimichi_ukita.gold: Norimichi Ukita is a Professor at the Toyota Technological Institute and an Adjunct Professor at TTIC. He earned his Ph.D. from Kyoto University. Specializing in computer vision, he co-authored the influential "Deep Back-Projection Networks For Super-Resolution" and won the NTIRE 2019 challenge. He previously held research roles at NAIST and Carnegie Mellon University.

univ_uc_irvine_victor_m_morales.gold: Victor M. Morales is an academic researcher at the University of California, Irvine, specializing in bacterial genetics and biotechnology. He is recognized for developing wide-host-range, low-copy-number expression vectors and suicide vectors for transposon mutagenesis in species such as *Pseudomonas solanacearum*. His work significantly advances genetic engineering tools for diverse bacterial populations.

oa_author_leland_mcinnes.gold: Leland McInnes is a researcher at the Tutte Institute for Mathematics and Computing. He is the primary creator of the UMAP dimensionality reduction algorithm and the HDBSCAN clustering library. His work utilizes algebraic topology and Riemannian geometry for unsupervised learning. He also maintains the PyNNDescent and DataMapPlot packages and contributes to Scikit-learn.

oa_author_bradley_christensen.gold: Bradley Christensen is a quantum physicist who earned his Ph.D. from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign in 2016. Within the Kwiat Quantum Information Group, he specialized in entangled photons and nonlocality, conducting landmark loophole-free Bell inequality tests. He received a Department of Commerce gold medal for his research and later studied superconducting qubits at Wisconsin–Madison.

oa_author_p__greenfield.gold: Perry Greenfield is a retired STScI researcher who earned a PhD from MIT studying the first discovered gravitational lens. He led the Science Software Branch, pioneering Python in astronomy by developing PyRAF, numarray, and PyFITS. Greenfield also calibrated Hubble’s Faint Object Camera and developed software for the James Webb Space Telescope.

oa_author_olivier_dauchot.gold: Olivier Dauchot is a Research Director at the CNRS and Director of the Gulliver laboratory at ESPCI Paris. He specializes in active matter, programmable matter, and collective effects in soft matter. Formerly head of the Group Instabilities and Turbulence at CEA-Saclay, he co-founded the ISP Internet-Way and researches swarm robotics and out-of-equilibrium physics.

oa_author_marco_tlio_ribeiro.gold: A researcher at Google DeepMind and Affiliate Assistant Professor at the University of Washington, Ribeiro is best known for creating LIME (Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations) and CheckList, which won the ACL 2020 Best Paper award. Previously a Senior Researcher at Microsoft Research, his work focuses on machine learning interpretability, trust, and model debugging.

oa_author_chao_ma.gold: Chao Ma is a Professor at Shanghai Jiao Tong University’s AI Institute, specializing in computer vision and visual tracking. He developed the LCT and HCF trackers and authored influential works including "VITAL" and "USOT." Formerly a researcher at the University of Adelaide, his research focuses on adaptive correlation filters and deep learning for video analysis.

imo_prafulla_susil_dhariwal.gold: Prafulla Susil Dhariwal is an OpenAI Research Scientist who led the development of GPT-4o. A gold medalist at the 2012 International Mathematical Olympiad, he also won gold at the International Physics and Astronomy Olympiads. He graduated from MIT in computer science and mathematics and co-authored foundational papers for DALL-E 2 and GPT-3.

imo_jihyon_pak.gold: Jihyon Pak represented the Democratic People's Republic of Korea at the 2013 International Mathematical Olympiad in Santa Marta, Colombia. Pak won a gold medal, scoring 32 points to rank 30th individually. This performance helped the North Korean team achieve a fifth-place overall finish among 97 competing nations.

imo_martin_vodicka.gold: Martin Vodička is a Slovak mathematician and four-time International Mathematical Olympiad medalist, winning gold in 2012. He earned his PhD from the Max Planck Institute for Mathematics in the Sciences and is an assistant professor at Pavol Jozef Šafárik University. His research involves combinatorial algebraic geometry. He has also served as Slovakia’s IMO team leader.

imo_lisa_sauermann.gold: Lisa Sauermann is a professor at the University of Bonn, holding the Hausdorff Chair. A record-breaking competitive mathematician, she earned four gold medals and one silver at the International Mathematical Olympiad, including the only perfect score in 2011. She previously taught at MIT and specializes in extremal and probabilistic combinatorics.

imo_evan_odorney.gold: Evan O'Dorney is an American mathematician who won the 2007 Scripps National Spelling Bee and the 2011 Intel Science Talent Search. A four-time International Mathematical Olympiad medalist, he was a three-time Putnam Fellow at Harvard. He earned a PhD from Princeton and is a postdoctoral researcher specializing in number theory.

imo_motoki_takigiku.gold: Motoki Takigiku is a Japanese mathematician who won a gold medal at the 2009 International Mathematical Olympiad. He earned his PhD from the University of Tokyo, specializing in algebraic combinatorics. He proved Nandi’s partition identities with Shunsuke Tsuchioka and has held research positions at the Tokyo Institute of Technology and Okayama University.

imo_yong_hyon_ri.gold: Yong Hyon Ri won a gold medal at the 2009 International Mathematical Olympiad in Bremen, Germany, scoring 32 points to rank 44th individually. Representing North Korea, he also competed at the 2011 competition in Amsterdam, Netherlands, where he earned a silver medal with a score of 25.

imo_victor_omelyanenko.gold: Victor Omelyanenko is a Russian competitive programmer who won gold medals at the 2009 and 2010 International Mathematical Olympiads, ranking fourth and seventh. Representing Moscow State University, he earned two consecutive gold medals at the ACM-ICPC World Finals in 2014 and 2015 as a member of the MSU Tapirs team.

imo_benjamin_gunby.gold: Benjamin Gunby won gold medals at the 2010 and 2011 International Mathematical Olympiads. He was a Putnam Fellow at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology and a member of its 2013 first-place Putnam team. He earned his PhD from Stanford University in 2020 and is a postdoctoral researcher in mathematics at Rutgers University specializing in combinatorics.

imo_hak_myong_mun.gold: Representing North Korea, Hak Myong Mun won a gold medal at the 52nd International Mathematical Olympiad held in Amsterdam in 2011. He achieved an individual score of 29 points, placing 25th among 563 contestants. His performance contributed to the North Korean team’s seventh-place overall finish in the competition.

imo_zichao_long.gold: Zichao Long won a gold medal at the 2011 International Mathematical Olympiad, ranking 14th individually. An alumnus of the High School Affiliated to Hunan Normal University, he studied at Peking University. He co-developed PDE-Net, a deep learning framework for discovering partial differential equations from data, published at the 2018 International Conference on Machine Learning.

imo_john_berman.gold: John Berman won a gold medal at the 2009 International Mathematical Olympiad. He earned a BS from MIT and a PhD from the University of Virginia. A former NSF Postdoctoral Fellow at UT Austin, he became the USA IMO team leader in 2024 and served as deputy leader during the team’s 2015 first-place finish.

oa_author_hang_bai.gold: Hang Bai is an academic researcher at Xidian University’s School of Computer Science and Technology. His research focuses on privacy-preserving technologies, including federated learning and information security. He co-authored the highly cited "A survey on federated learning" (2021) and has published in journals including Knowledge-Based Systems and IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics.

univ_uc_irvine_joseph_r_bishop.gold: Joseph R. Bishop is a glycobiology researcher who investigated heparan sulfate proteoglycans and their roles in mammalian physiology. While affiliated with the University of California, San Diego, he co-authored a prominent 2007 *Nature* review on the subject. His research also explored the importance of heparan sulfate in lipoprotein clearance and parasite invasion.

imo_sung_gi_park.gold: Park won a gold medal at the 2011 International Mathematical Olympiad and a silver medal in 2010. An MIT alumnus, he earned his PhD from Harvard University. He is currently a Veblen Research Instructor at Princeton University and the Institute for Advanced Study, where he researches birational geometry and Hodge theory.

imo_domagoj_evid.gold: Domagoj Ćevid is a Croatian mathematician who won two gold medals and one silver at the International Mathematical Olympiad, becoming Croatia's first gold medalist in 2012. He also earned two silver medals at the International Olympiad in Informatics. He studied at the University of Cambridge and completed a PhD at ETH Zurich in 2021.

imo_hunter_spink.gold: Hunter Spink is an Assistant Professor of mathematics at the University of Toronto. Representing Canada, he won a gold medal at the 2010 International Mathematical Olympiad, ranking 11th. He earned his PhD from Harvard University under Béla Bollobás and was previously a Szegő Assistant Professor at Stanford and a fellow at Trinity College, Cambridge.

imo_szymon_kanonowicz.gold: Szymon Kanonowicz won a gold medal at the 2010 International Mathematical Olympiad, ranking 18th individually. He authored the book *Matematyka elementarna* (2015), published by the University of Warsaw’s Faculty of Mathematics, Informatics, and Mechanics. He has served on the Main Committee of the Polish Junior Mathematical Olympiad and conducted seminars for mathematics teachers.

imo_suthee_ruangwises.gold: Suthee Ruangwises is a Thai computer scientist who won a gold medal at the 2009 International Mathematical Olympiad. He earned his Ph.D. from the Tokyo Institute of Technology after graduating from MIT. Currently a lecturer at Chulalongkorn University, he researches card-based cryptography, matching under preferences, and the computational complexity of logic puzzles.

imo_posheng_wu.gold: Po-Sheng Wu represented Taiwan at the International Mathematical Olympiad in 2013 and 2014, winning gold medals both years. In 2014, he achieved a perfect score, ranking first in the world. A graduate of Taipei Municipal Jianguo High School, he later studied mathematics at National Taiwan University.

imo_aref_sadeghi.gold: Aref Sadeghi won a gold medal at the 2013 International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO) in Santa Marta, Colombia, ranking 34th individually with 31 points. He also earned a gold medal at the 30th Iranian National Mathematical Olympiad. He attended Shahid Soltani High School in Karaj and represented the Iranian national team as one of its six members.

imo_jirawat_anunrojwong.gold: Gold medalist at the 2012 International Mathematical Olympiad, Jirawat Anunrojwong is an Assistant Professor of Operations at the Yale School of Management. He earned degrees in applied mathematics and statistics from Harvard University and a PhD from Columbia Business School. His research focuses on market design, robustness, and applications in electricity markets.

imo_donat_nagy.gold: Donát Nagy is a Hungarian mathematician and software developer. Representing Hungary, he won a gold medal at the 2010 International Mathematical Olympiad and a silver medal in 2011. He earned a PhD in theoretical mathematics from Eötvös Loránd University, specializing in real analysis and descriptive set theory, and currently works at Ericsson Hungary.

imo_se_hyun_ji.gold: Se Hyun Ji is a South Korean mathematician who won a gold medal at the 2013 International Mathematical Olympiad. A Seoul Science High School alumnus, he graduated summa cum laude from Seoul National University and pursued doctoral studies at the University of Chicago. He researches kinetic theory, partial differential equations, and the Landau equation.

imo_sang_hoon_lee.gold: Sang Hoon Lee is a South Korean mathematician who won consecutive gold medals at the International Mathematical Olympiad in 2009 and 2010. He earned his Ph.D. in mathematics from Princeton University in 2023. He has served as a postdoctoral researcher at the Korea Institute for Advanced Study and the Chinese University of Hong Kong.

imo_zhuo_qun_alex_song.gold: Zhuo Qun (Alex) Song is the most decorated participant in International Mathematical Olympiad history, winning five gold medals and one bronze for Canada from 2010 to 2015, including a perfect score. A Princeton graduate and former Citadel quantitative researcher, he is currently a PhD student in number theory at the University of Illinois.

imo_mengxi_wu.gold: Mengxi Wu won a gold medal at the 2011 International Mathematical Olympiad representing China. An alumnus of Jiangsu Nanjin Senior High School, he studied at Peking University and earned a PhD from Brown University in 2021. He is currently a research assistant professor at the Hong Kong University of Science and Technology specializing in climate science.

imo_nipun_pitimanaaree.gold: Nipun Pitimanaaree is a Thai mathematician and entrepreneur who won four gold medals (2010–2013) and one silver medal (2009) at the International Mathematical Olympiad. An MIT graduate, he co-founded the decentralized finance platforms Alpha Finance and INFINIT, and developed Alpha Homora, the first product to introduce leveraged yield farming to the DeFi market.

imo_george_arzeno.gold: George Arzeno Soltero was the first Puerto Rican to win a gold medal at the International Mathematical Olympiad, placing 18th overall in 2010. He previously earned an honorable mention at the 2009 IMO and two bronze medals at the Ibero-American Mathematical Olympiad. He served as Puerto Rico's deputy leader for the 2011 IMO.

imo_chao_gu.gold: Chao Gu is a mathematician who won a gold medal at the 2013 International Mathematical Olympiad for China. A graduate of Shanghai Gezhi High School, he earned a bachelor’s from Peking University and a Ph.D. from the University of Chicago. He is a postdoctoral researcher at the Shanghai Institute for Mathematics and Interdisciplinary Sciences.

imo_umut_varolgunes.gold: Umut Varolgüneş is a mathematician and Assistant Professor at Koç University. A two-time International Mathematical Olympiad gold medalist for Türkiye (2008, 2009), he earned his PhD from MIT in 2018. He previously served as a Szegő Assistant Professor at Stanford University. His research focuses on symplectic geometry, Floer theory, and homological mirror symmetry.

imo_matei_mandache.gold: Matei Mandache is a mathematician who won a gold medal at the 2013 International Mathematical Olympiad and bronze in 2012. He attended Loughborough Grammar School and earned BA and MMath degrees from the University of Cambridge. His research includes extremal combinatorics and the Corners theorem. He later became an actuarial analyst and founded Spirit Communication Technologies.

univ_fac_mit_david_j_perreault.gold: David J. Perreault is the Ford Professor of Engineering at MIT’s Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science. A member of the National Academy of Engineering and an IEEE Fellow, he received the 2024 IEEE William E. Newell Power Electronics Award. He co-founded Eta Devices and Eta Wireless and specializes in high-frequency power electronics and energy conversion.

imo_xiaolin_danny_shi.gold: Xiaolin (Danny) Shi is an Assistant Professor of Mathematics at the University of Washington, specializing in algebraic topology. Representing Canada, he won a gold medal at the 2009 International Mathematical Olympiad. He earned his S.B. from MIT and a Ph.D. from Harvard University, serving as a L.E. Dickson Instructor at the University of Chicago.

univ_fac_mit_ankur_moitra.gold: Ankur Moitra is the Norbert Wiener Professor of Mathematics at MIT and Director of the Statistics and Data Science Center. A theoretical computer scientist, he received the 2026 Gödel Prize for work on robust high-dimensional estimation. His research, supported by Packard and Sloan fellowships, explores the algorithmic foundations of machine learning and robust statistics.

imo_junhwa_jung.gold: Junhwa Jung represented the Republic of Korea at the 2010 International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO) in Astana, Kazakhstan. He won a gold medal, ranking 27th individually with a score of 28 points. His performance contributed to the South Korean team's fourth-place overall finish among 105 competing nations.

univ_fac_mit_julie_arnold_shah.gold: Julie Arnold Shah is the H.N. Slater Professor and Head of the Department of Aeronautics and Astronautics. She directs the Interactive Robotics Group at CSAIL and serves as Associate Dean in the Schwarzman College of Computing. A specialist in human-robot collaboration, she co-authored *What to Expect When You're Expecting Robots* (2020) and received an NSF CAREER award.

univ_fac_mit_john_n_tsitsiklis.gold: John N. Tsitsiklis is Clarence J. Lebel Professor of Electrical Engineering at MIT and a member of the National Academy of Engineering. He received the 2018 INFORMS John von Neumann Theory Prize and the 2018 IEEE Control Systems Award. He formerly directed the Laboratory for Information and Decision Systems and co-authored Neuro-Dynamic Programming.

univ_fac_mit_daniel_jackson.gold: Daniel Jackson is the Thomas J. Perkins Professor at MIT and associate director of CSAIL. He is the principal designer of the Alloy modeling language and pioneered lightweight formal methods. His books include *Software Abstractions* and *The Essence of Software*. He is an ACM Fellow and recipient of the ACM SIGSOFT Impact Award.
