univ_fac_mit_m_frans_kaashoek.gold: M. Frans Kaashoek is the Charles Piper Professor at MIT and a member of CSAIL. He is recognized for his influential research on exokernels, distributed hash tables, and the xv6 operating system. A recipient of the ACM Prize in Computing and member of the National Academy of Engineering, he co-founded Sightpath and Mazu Networks.

univ_fac_mit_david_a_sontag.gold: David A. Sontag is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT, affiliated with CSAIL and IMES. He leads a research group applying machine learning to healthcare, including clinical decision support and precision oncology. A co-founder of Layer Health, he previously taught at NYU and received the MIT Sprowls Award for his doctoral thesis.

univ_fac_mit_robert_c_miller.gold: Robert C. Miller is a Distinguished Professor of Computer Science at MIT and a member of CSAIL. His research focuses on human-computer interaction, crowd computing, and online education. A MacVicar Faculty Fellow and NSF CAREER award recipient, he has served as Education Officer for EECS and the Schwarzman College of Computing. He co-authored the paper "Soylent."

univ_fac_mit_armando_solar_lezama.gold: Armando Solar-Lezama is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT and the Associate Director and COO of CSAIL. He leads the Computer-Aided Programming Group and was named an inaugural Distinguished Professor of Computing. He is best known for developing the Sketch system for automated program synthesis.

univ_fac_mit_vincent_sitzmann.gold: Vincent Sitzmann is an Associate Professor at MIT EECS and the Jamieson Career Development Professor. He leads the Scene Representation Group at CSAIL, specializing in neural scene representations. He developed SIREN and received a NeurIPS 2019 Honorable Mention for Scene Representation Networks. He holds a PhD from Stanford and won the 2026 Junior Bose Award.

univ_fac_mit_dylan_hadfield_menell.gold: Dylan Hadfield-Menell is an Associate Professor of EECS at MIT and leads the Algorithmic Alignment Group at CSAIL. His research focuses on AI alignment, designing systems that pursue intended human goals. A Schmidt Futures AI2050 Early Career Fellow, he earned his PhD from UC Berkeley and co-authored "Cooperative Inverse Reinforcement Learning" and "The Off-Switch Game."

univ_fac_mit_daniel_p_huttenlocher.gold: Daniel P. Huttenlocher is the inaugural dean of the MIT Schwarzman College of Computing and former founding dean of Cornell Tech. An ACM Fellow specializing in computer vision, he co-authored The Age of AI with Henry Kissinger and Eric Schmidt. He serves on the boards of Amazon and Corning and previously chaired the MacArthur Foundation.

univ_fac_mit_joel_moses.gold: An Institute Professor at MIT, Joel Moses served as the university's Provost and Dean of Engineering. He is best known for leading the development of Macsyma, a pioneering computer algebra system. A doctoral student of Marvin Minsky, he was elected to the National Academy of Engineering for his foundational work in symbolic algebraic manipulation.

univ_fac_mit_yoon_kim.gold: Yoon Kim is an Associate Professor at MIT EECS and CSAIL, specializing in natural language processing and machine learning. He earned his PhD from Harvard University and co-created the OpenNMT toolkit. His honors include the 2025 NSF CAREER Award and the 2024 Schmidt Sciences AI2050 Early Career Fellowship.

univ_fac_mit_stefanie_m_ller.gold: Stefanie Müller is the TIBCO Career Development Associate Professor at MIT, joint between EECS and Mechanical Engineering. She leads the HCI Engineering Group at CSAIL, specializing in personal fabrication and human-computer interaction. Her honors include an Alfred P. Sloan Fellowship, an NSF CAREER Award, and recognition as an MIT Technology Review Innovator Under 35.

univ_fac_mit_jacob_white.gold: Jacob K. White is the Cecil H. Green Professor and a MacVicar Teaching Fellow at MIT. A pioneer in numerical methods, he developed the FastCap and FastHenry programs for 3D extraction. He contributed to the Spectre simulation tools and received the 2022 ACM/IEEE A. Richard Newton Technical Impact Award for his work on steady-state analysis.

univ_fac_mit_joel_voldman.gold: Joel Voldman is the Clarence J. LeBel Professor and Faculty Head of Electrical Engineering at MIT. A principal investigator in the Research Laboratory of Electronics, he specializes in BioMEMS and microfluidics for biological applications. He received the 2023 Teaching with Digital Technology Award and teaches the Engineering for Impact course.

univ_fac_mit_antonio_torralba.gold: Antonio Torralba is the Delta Electronics Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT and head of the AI+D faculty. He was the inaugural director of the MIT Quest for Intelligence and the MIT-IBM Watson AI Lab. An ACM and AAAI Fellow, he co-authored Foundations of Computer Vision and specializes in computer vision.

univ_fac_mit_manolis_kellis.gold: Manolis Kellis is a Professor of Computer Science at MIT and director of the MIT Computational Biology Group. He co-led the NIH Roadmap Epigenomics Project and contributed to the ENCODE and GENCODE consortia. A recipient of the Presidential Early Career Award and NIH Director's Pioneer Award, he researches disease genomics, epigenomics, and gene regulation.

univ_fac_mit_randall_davis.gold: Randall Davis is a Professor of Computer Science and Electrical Engineering at MIT and former Associate Director of CSAIL. A past president of the AAAI, he pioneered knowledge-based systems and human-computer interaction. He served as a court expert in *Computer Associates v. Altai*, establishing the abstraction-filtration-comparison test for software copyright.

univ_fac_mit_russ_tedrake.gold: Russ Tedrake is the Toyota Professor at MIT and Director of the Center for Robotics at CSAIL. He is Vice President of Robotics Research at the Toyota Research Institute and led Team MIT in the DARPA Robotics Challenge. He created the open-source robotics toolbox Drake and founded a physical AI startup.

univ_fac_mit_robert_c_berwick.gold: A Professor of Computational Linguistics at MIT, Berwick co-founded the Center for Biological and Computational Learning. He co-authored *Why Only Us: Language and Evolution* with Noam Chomsky. A Guggenheim Fellow, he earned his Ph.D. under Chomsky and Marvin Minsky, implementing the first modern computer program to learn language.

univ_fac_mit_pulkit_agrawal.gold: Pulkit Agrawal is an Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT, where he directs the Improbable AI Lab. He earned his PhD from UC Berkeley and a BS from IIT Kanpur. His research focuses on robotics and reinforcement learning, specifically agile locomotion and dexterous manipulation. He co-founded SafelyYou and Eka Robotics.

univ_fac_mit_adam_belay.gold: Adam Belay is an Associate Professor at MIT EECS and CSAIL. He earned his PhD from Stanford University and previously worked at Google and VMware. His research focuses on datacenter operating systems and networking, including projects like IX and Caladan. He received the OSDI Jay Lepreau Best Paper Award and a 2024 Sloan Research Fellowship.

univ_fac_mit_ron_weiss.gold: Ron Weiss is a pioneer of synthetic biology and Director of the MIT Synthetic Biology Center. A Professor of Biological Engineering and EECS, he earned his Ph.D. from MIT in 2001. His research focuses on programming cells using synthetic gene networks and logic circuits. He previously served on the Princeton faculty and co-founded Strand Therapeutics.

univ_fac_mit_patrick_henry_winston.gold: Patrick Henry Winston (1943–2019) was the Ford Professor of Artificial Intelligence at MIT and directed the MIT Artificial Intelligence Laboratory from 1972 to 1997. A past president of the AAAI, he authored the seminal textbook *Artificial Intelligence* and the "On To" programming series. He was renowned for his annual "How to Speak" lectures.

univ_fac_mit_song_han.gold: Song Han is an Associate Professor specializing in energy-efficient deep learning. He earned his PhD from Stanford and pioneered "Deep Compression" and the "Efficient Inference Engine." A recipient of the NSF CAREER Award and Sloan Research Fellowship, he co-founded DeePhi Tech (acquired by Xilinx) and OmniML (acquired by NVIDIA), and leads NVIDIA’s Efficient AI team.

univ_fac_mit_jonathan_ragan_kelley.gold: An Associate Professor and CSAIL principal investigator, Ragan-Kelley co-created the Halide programming language for high-performance image processing. He received the 2021 ACM SIGGRAPH Significant New Researcher Award and previously served as an assistant professor at UC Berkeley. He earned his PhD from MIT in 2014 and has collaborated with Industrial Light & Magic.

univ_fac_mit_gregory_w_wornell.gold: Gregory W. Wornell is the Sumitomo Professor of Engineering at MIT, where he leads the Signals, Information, and Algorithms Laboratory. An IEEE Fellow, he specializes in signal processing, information theory, and statistical inference. He received the IEEE Leon K. Kirchmayer Graduate Teaching Award and co-directs the MIT Center for Wireless Networking.

univ_fac_mit_jing_kong.gold: Jing Kong is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT and a principal investigator in the Research Laboratory of Electronics. She specializes in the synthesis of nanomaterials, including carbon nanotubes and graphene. A Stanford alumna, she pioneered chemical vapor deposition methods for 2D material production and received the MIT 3M Award.

univ_fac_mit_constantinos_daskalakis.gold: Constantinos Daskalakis is the Avanessians Professor at MIT and a CSAIL member. He received the 2018 Nevanlinna Prize and the ACM Grace Murray Hopper Award for proving the computational complexity of Nash equilibria. A co-recipient of the Kalai Prize, his research spans algorithmic game theory, mechanism design, and high-dimensional statistics.

univ_fac_mit_costis_daskalakis.gold: Costis Daskalakis is the Avanessians Professor at MIT and a member of CSAIL. He received the 2018 Rolf Nevanlinna Prize and the ACM Grace Murray Hopper Award. He is renowned for proving the computational complexity of the Nash equilibrium, establishing its PPAD-completeness with Paul Goldberg and Christos Papadimitriou, and for work on multi-item auctions.

univ_fac_mit_david_gifford.gold: David K. Gifford is a Professor of Computer Science and Engineering and Biological Engineering at MIT. He leads the Programming Systems Research Group at CSAIL, focusing on machine learning for genomics and immunoengineering. He developed programmed mutagenesis and creates computational models to analyze transcriptional regulatory networks, antibody design, and peptide presentation.

univ_fac_mit_bonnie_berger.gold: Bonnie Berger is the Simons Professor of Mathematics and Computer Science at MIT, heading the Computation and Biology group at CSAIL. A pioneer in computational molecular biology, she is a member of the National Academy of Sciences and a Fellow of the ACM, ISCB, and SIAM. She received the 2019 ISCB Senior Scientist Award.

univ_fac_mit_sara_beery.gold: Sara Beery is an Assistant Professor at MIT EECS and CSAIL. She earned her PhD from Caltech, receiving the Amori Doctoral Prize. Her research develops computer vision for biodiversity monitoring. An NSF CAREER Award recipient and AI2050 Early Career Fellow, she founded the CV4Ecology Workshop and was previously a professional ballerina.

univ_fac_mit_vivienne_sze.gold: Vivienne Sze is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT, leading the Energy-Efficient Multimedia Systems group. She received a Primetime Engineering Emmy Award for developing the HEVC video compression standard. Her research focuses on low-power hardware for AI, including the Eyeriss accelerator, and she co-authored *Efficient Processing of Deep Neural Networks*.

univ_fac_mit_saman_p_amarasinghe.gold: Saman P. Amarasinghe is the Thomas and Gerd Perkins Professor at MIT, leading the Commit compiler research group at CSAIL. He pioneered high-performance domain-specific languages, including Halide and TACO, and co-led the Raw architecture project. An ACM Fellow, he received the 2025 Ken Kennedy Award and co-founded Sri Lanka’s first internet service provider.

univ_fac_mit_barbara_liskov.gold: Barbara Liskov is an MIT Institute Professor and 2008 Turing Award laureate. She pioneered data abstraction and developed the Liskov substitution principle, a cornerstone of object-oriented programming. Her work includes designing the CLU and Argus languages, which significantly influenced modern software reliability, modularity, and distributed systems.

univ_fac_mit_bill_freeman.gold: William T. Freeman is the Thomas and Gerd Perkins Professor of EECS at MIT and a CSAIL member. Elected to the National Academy of Engineering, he pioneered steerable filters and motion magnification. He shared the 2020 Breakthrough Prize in Physics for his contributions to the Event Horizon Telescope’s first image of a black hole.

univ_fac_mit_jacob_andreas.gold: Jacob Andreas is an associate professor at MIT EECS and CSAIL, where he leads the Language and Intelligence Group. His research explores the computational foundations of language learning, including decoding sperm whale communication. A Churchill Scholar with a PhD from UC Berkeley, he received a Sloan Fellowship, NSF CAREER award, and MIT’s Edgerton Award.

univ_fac_mit_hae_seung_lee.gold: Hae-Seung (Harry) Lee is the Advanced Television and Signal Processing Professor at MIT and Director of the Microsystems Technology Laboratories. An IEEE Fellow, he developed widely used self-calibration techniques for analog-to-digital converters. He also directs the Center for Integrated Circuits and Systems and co-founded companies including Omni Design Technologies and SMaL Camera Technologies.

univ_fac_mit_max_marcel_shulaker.gold: Max Marcel Shulaker is an Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT. He developed the first carbon nanotube computer and the first modern microprocessor built from carbon nanotube transistors. A 2021 Moore Inventor Fellow, he pioneered monolithic 3D integrated circuits and led DARPA’s Three Dimensional Monolithic System-on-a-Chip program.

univ_fac_mit_samuel_r_madden.gold: Samuel R. Madden is the Faculty Head of Computer Science and a Distinguished Professor at MIT. A principal investigator at CSAIL, he specializes in database systems and sensor networks, leading projects like C-Store, H-Store, and TinyDB. An ACM Fellow, he received the 2024 SIGMOD Edgar F. Codd Innovations Award and co-founded Cambridge Mobile Telematics.

univ_fac_mit_andrew_lo.gold: Andrew W. Lo is the Harris Professor at MIT Sloan and a Principal Investigator at the Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL). He directs the Laboratory for Financial Engineering and authored *Adaptive Markets*. His research applies machine learning and financial engineering to systemic risk, healthcare finance, and evolutionary models of investor behavior.

univ_fac_mit_hal_abelson.gold: Hal Abelson is the Class of 1922 Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at MIT. He co-authored the textbook *Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs* and created MIT App Inventor. A founding director of Creative Commons and the Free Software Foundation, he also co-founded MIT OpenCourseWare and led development of the Logo programming language.

univ_fac_mit_dirk_englund.gold: Dirk Englund is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT and head of the Quantum Photonics Laboratory. He specializes in quantum technologies, including photonic integrated circuits and diamond-based quantum systems. A recipient of the PECASE and the Adolph Lomb Medal, he is an Optica Fellow and a Sloan Research Fellow.

univ_fac_mit_phillip_isola.gold: Phillip Isola is an Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT and a member of CSAIL. He earned his PhD from MIT and was a postdoc at UC Berkeley. Known for pioneering image-to-image translation (Pix2Pix), he co-authored the textbook *Foundations of Computer Vision* and is a Packard and Sloan Fellow.

univ_fac_mit_leslie_pack_kaelbling.gold: Leslie Pack Kaelbling is the Panasonic Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at MIT. She earned her PhD from Stanford University and founded the Journal of Machine Learning Research. A recipient of the IJCAI Computers and Thought Award, she is recognized for her research in reinforcement learning, robotics, and partially observable Markov decision processes.

univ_fac_mit_ron_rivest.gold: Ronald Rivest is an MIT Institute Professor and co-inventor of the RSA public-key cryptosystem, for which he shared the 2002 Turing Award. He co-authored the foundational textbook *Introduction to Algorithms* and founded RSA Data Security. A member of the National Academies of Sciences and Engineering, he also designed the MD5 hash function and RC4 cipher.

univ_fac_mit_barbara_h_liskov.gold: An MIT Institute Professor and 2008 Turing Award laureate, Liskov pioneered abstract data types and the Liskov substitution principle. She led the development of the CLU and Argus programming languages and was the first woman in the United States to earn a computer science PhD, which she received from Stanford University in 1968.

univ_fac_mit_john_v_guttag.gold: John V. Guttag is the Dugald C. Jackson Professor at MIT and served as head of the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from 1999 to 2004. He authored *Introduction to Computation and Programming Using Python* and leads the Clinical and Applied Machine Learning Group, focusing on data-driven medicine. He is an ACM Fellow.

univ_fac_mit_caroline_uhler.gold: Caroline Uhler is the Andrew and Erna Viterbi Professor of Engineering and a core member of the Broad Institute, directing the Eric and Wendy Schmidt Center. Her research focuses on causal inference and gene regulation. A SIAM and IMS Fellow, she has received an NIH New Innovator Award, Simons Investigator Award, and NSF CAREER Award.

univ_fac_mit_yael_tauman_kalai.gold: The Ellen Swallow Richards Professor at MIT and a Senior Principal Researcher at Microsoft Research, Kalai received the 2022 ACM Prize in Computing for breakthroughs in verifiable delegation of computation. Her work in cryptography includes co-inventing ring signatures and analyzing the Fiat-Shamir heuristic. She earned her PhD from MIT under advisor Shafi Goldwasser.

univ_fac_mit_hari_balakrishnan.gold: Hari Balakrishnan is the Fujitsu Professor of Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence at MIT. He received the 2023 Marconi Prize for contributions to mobile sensing and networking. A co-founder of Cambridge Mobile Telematics, he is known for developing the CarTel mobile sensor system, the Cricket indoor location system, and the Chord distributed hash table.

univ_fac_mit_erik_d_demaine.gold: Erik D. Demaine is an MIT computer science professor who became the youngest faculty member in the institute's history at age 20. A 2003 MacArthur Fellow, he pioneered computational origami and solved the carpenter's rule problem. His mathematical sculptures, co-created with his father Martin, are in the Museum of Modern Art’s permanent collection.

univ_fac_mit_mohammad_alizadeh.gold: Mohammad Alizadeh is the NEC Professor of Software Science and Engineering at MIT and a member of CSAIL. He earned his PhD from Stanford University. A recipient of the 2022 ACM Grace Murray Hopper Award and the SIGCOMM Rising Star Award, he developed Data Center TCP (DCTCP) and the Pensieve adaptive video streaming system.

univ_fac_mit_martin_c_rinard.gold: An ACM Fellow and Professor at MIT, Rinard received the 2025 ACM SIGSOFT Outstanding Research Award for pioneering program repair and approximate computing. His research introduced failure-oblivious computing and loop perforation to enhance software robustness. He earned his Ph.D. from Stanford University and previously served as an assistant professor at the University of California, Santa Barbara.

univ_fac_mit_victor_zue.gold: Victor Zue is the Delta Electronics Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT and former Director of the Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL). He pioneered spoken language interfaces leading the Spoken Language Systems Group. A member of the National Academy of Engineering, he received the Okawa Prize and the IEEE James L. Flanagan Award.

univ_fac_mit_michael_carbin.gold: Michael Carbin is an Associate Professor at MIT researching programming systems for uncertain environments, including approximate and unreliable hardware. He co-authored the "lottery ticket hypothesis" for neural networks and co-founded MosaicML. His honors include a Presidential Early Career Award (PECASE), a Sloan Research Fellowship, and an NSF CAREER Award. He holds a PhD from MIT.

univ_fac_mit_daniel_s_nchez.gold: Daniel Sánchez is the Elihu Thomson Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT and a member of CSAIL. He earned his PhD from Stanford University. Specializing in computer architecture, he focuses on multicore processors and memory hierarchies. He is a 2018 MICRO Hall of Fame inductee and received a 2015 NSF CAREER Award.

univ_fac_mit_nancy_a_lynch.gold: Nancy A. Lynch is the NEC Professor of Software Science and Engineering at MIT. A pioneer in distributed computing, she is renowned for the "FLP" impossibility result for asynchronous distributed consensus. She authored *Distributed Algorithms* and received the Knuth Prize, two Dijkstra Prizes, and membership in the National Academies of Sciences and Engineering.

univ_fac_mit_david_k_gifford.gold: David K. Gifford is a Professor of Computer Science and Engineering and Biological Engineering at MIT. An ACM Fellow, he leads the Gifford Lab, applying machine learning to genomics and immunology. He developed programmed mutagenesis and co-authored influential work on Semantic File Systems, distributed systems, and the modeling of transcriptional regulatory networks.

univ_fac_mit_stefanie_mueller.gold: Stefanie Mueller is the TIBCO Career Development Associate Professor at MIT, joint between EECS and Mechanical Engineering. She leads the HCI Engineering Group at CSAIL, focusing on personal fabrication and computational design. A Sloan Fellow and NSF CAREER Award recipient, she was named to MIT Technology Review’s "Innovators Under 35" and Forbes 30 Under 30.

univ_fac_mit_nir_shavit.gold: Nir Shavit is a computer scientist who co-authored the textbook *The Art of Multiprocessor Programming*. He received the 2004 Gödel Prize for applying algebraic topology to shared memory and the 2012 Dijkstra Prize for introducing Software Transactional Memory. He leads the Multiprocessor Algorithmics and Computational Connectomics groups, researching concurrent data structures and brain mapping.

univ_fac_mit_polina_golland.gold: Polina Golland is the Sunlin and Priscilla Chou Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT. She serves as an associate director of CSAIL and leads the Medical Vision Group. Her research focuses on biomedical image analysis and statistical modeling. She is a Fellow of both AIMBE and the MICCAI Society.

univ_fac_mit_qing_hu.gold: A Distinguished Professor in MIT’s Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, Hu pioneered high-performance terahertz quantum cascade lasers and real-time terahertz imaging. He is a Fellow of IEEE, APS, AAAS, and Optica. His honors include the 2015 Nick Holonyak, Jr. Award and the 2012 IEEE Photonics Society William Streifer Scientific Achievement Award.

univ_fac_mit_robert_tappan_morris.gold: Robert Tappan Morris is a professor of computer science at MIT. In 1988, he created the Morris worm, the first major Internet worm, resulting in the first conviction under the Computer Fraud and Abuse Act. He co-founded the startup accelerator Y Combinator and the software company Viaweb. He earned his PhD from Harvard University.

univ_fac_mit_sam_madden.gold: Samuel Madden is the Faculty Head of Computer Science and a Distinguished Professor at MIT CSAIL. A leader in database systems, he co-founded Vertica and Cambridge Mobile Telematics. He is known for developing C-Store and TinyDB. An ACM Fellow, he received the 2024 SIGMOD Edgar F. Codd Innovations Award and a Sloan Research Fellowship.

univ_fac_mit_peter_szolovits.gold: Peter Szolovits is a Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at MIT and head of the Clinical Decision-Making Group at CSAIL. His research focuses on applying artificial intelligence to clinical decision support and medical informatics. He is a member of the National Academy of Medicine and received the 2013 Morris F. Collen Award.

univ_fac_mit_virginia_vassilevska.gold: Virginia Vassilevska Williams is the Cecil H. Green Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT. She is renowned for breakthrough algorithms in fast matrix multiplication and for co-founding the field of fine-grained complexity. A 2023 Simons Investigator and 2018 ICM invited speaker, she earned her PhD from Carnegie Mellon University in 2008.

univ_fac_mit_samuel_madden.gold: Samuel Madden is the Faculty Head of Computer Science and a Distinguished Professor at MIT. An ACM Fellow and recipient of the 2024 SIGMOD Edgar F. Codd Innovations Award, he specializes in database systems. He co-founded Vertica and Cambridge Mobile Telematics and led influential projects including C-Store, TinyDB, and the CarTel sensor network.

univ_fac_mit_harold_abelson.gold: Harold Abelson is the Class of 1922 Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at MIT. He co-authored the textbook *Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs* and created the MIT App Inventor platform. A proponent of open education, he co-founded MIT OpenCourseWare and is a founding director of Creative Commons and the Free Software Foundation.

univ_fac_mit_virginia_vassilevska_willia.gold: Virginia Vassilevska Williams is the Cecil H. Green Professor at MIT. She is renowned for breakthroughs in fast matrix multiplication and co-founding the field of fine-grained complexity. A 2023 Simons Investigator, she was an invited speaker at the 2018 International Congress of Mathematicians and received a FOCS 2024 Test of Time Award.

univ_fac_mit_dan_huttenlocher.gold: Daniel Huttenlocher is the inaugural dean of the MIT Schwarzman College of Computing and the Henry Ellis Warren Professor. He previously served as the founding dean of Cornell Tech. He co-authored *The Age of AI* with Henry Kissinger and Eric Schmidt and serves on the boards of Amazon and Corning Incorporated.

univ_fac_mit_rachit_nigam.gold: Rachit Nigam is an Assistant Professor at MIT EECS and CSAIL. He earned his PhD from Cornell University in 2025. His research focuses on programming languages and computer architecture, specifically the Calyx compiler infrastructure and the Filament and Dahlia languages. He received the 2025 SIGPLAN John C. Reynolds Doctoral Dissertation Award.

univ_fac_mit_duane_s_boning.gold: Duane S. Boning is the Clarence J. LeBel Professor at MIT and the Vice Provost for International Activities. An IEEE Fellow who earned his PhD from MIT, he researches machine learning and statistical methods for semiconductor and photonic manufacturing. He previously served as Associate Head of EECS and Editor-in-Chief of IEEE Transactions on Semiconductor Manufacturing.

univ_fac_mit_devavrat_shah.gold: Devavrat Shah is the Viterbi Professor of EECS at MIT and director of the Statistics and Data Science Center. Recipient of the Erlang Prize and ACM SIGMETRICS Achievement Award, he researches statistical inference and stochastic networks. He co-founded the startups Celect and Ikigai Labs and is Editor-in-Chief of the journal Stochastic Systems.

univ_fac_mit_mohsen_ghaffari.gold: Mohsen Ghaffari is the Steven and Renee Finn Career Development Professor at MIT, specializing in distributed and parallel algorithms. Previously a tenured professor at ETH Zurich, he earned his PhD from MIT in 2016. His honors include the 2017 ACM-EATCS Principles of Distributed Computing Doctoral Dissertation Award and a 2025 Sloan Research Fellowship.

univ_fac_mit_rob_miller.gold: Robert C. Miller is a Distinguished Professor of Computer Science at MIT and a member of CSAIL. His research focuses on human-computer interaction, crowdsourcing, and online education. A MacVicar Faculty Fellow and NSF CAREER award recipient, he has served as Education Officer for EECS and the Schwarzman College of Computing. He co-authored the influential paper "Soylent."

univ_fac_mit_tommi_s_jaakkola.gold: Tommi S. Jaakkola is the Thomas Siebel Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science. He earned his Ph.D. from MIT in computational neuroscience. His research focuses on machine learning theory, including interpretable models and applications in molecular design. A Sloan Research Fellow and AAAI Fellow, he co-developed the DiffDock model for molecular docking.

univ_fac_mit_jacob_k_white.gold: Jacob K. White is the Cecil H. Green Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science. A pioneer in numerical methods, he developed the FASTCAP and FASTHENRY simulation programs and was a key contributor to Spectre and SpectreRF. An IEEE Fellow and MacVicar Teaching Fellow, he received the ACM/IEEE A. Richard Newton Technical Impact Award.

univ_fac_mit_yury_polyanskiy.gold: Yury Polyanskiy is the Cutten Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT and a member of LIDS. He earned his PhD from Princeton University in 2010. An IEEE Fellow, his research focuses on information theory and statistical machine learning. He received the 2020 James Massey Award and 2013 NSF CAREER Award.

univ_fac_mit_gerald_j_sussman.gold: Gerald J. Sussman is the Panasonic Professor of Electrical Engineering at MIT. He co-authored the seminal textbook *Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs* and co-invented the Scheme programming language. He also co-authored *Structure and Interpretation of Classical Mechanics* and designed the Digital Orrery. He is a member of the National Academy of Engineering.

univ_fac_mit_julie_a_shah.gold: Julie A. Shah is the H.N. Slater Professor of Aeronautics and Astronautics and director of the Interactive Robotics Group at CSAIL. She serves as Associate Dean of Social and Ethical Responsibilities of Computing. A 2014 MIT Technology Review TR35 innovator, she co-authored *What to Expect When You're Expecting Robots* and specializes in human-robot collaboration.

univ_fac_mit_paul_pu_liang.gold: Paul Pu Liang is the Sony Career Development Professor and an Assistant Professor at the MIT Media Lab and EECS. He directs the Multisensory Intelligence research group and earned his PhD from Carnegie Mellon University. A Forbes 30 Under 30 honoree, Liang received the CMU School of Computer Science Distinguished Dissertation Award.

univ_fac_mit_alexandre_megretski.gold: Alexandre Megretski is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT and a member of the Laboratory for Information and Decision Systems. He earned his Ph.D. from Leningrad University in 1988. He is best known for developing the Integral Quadratic Constraints (IQC) framework for analyzing feedback systems with nonlinearities and uncertainties.

univ_fac_mit_jeffrey_lang.gold: Jeffrey H. Lang is the Vitesse Professor of Electrical Engineering at MIT. An IEEE Fellow and 2022 MacVicar Faculty Fellow, he co-authored the textbook *Foundations of Analog and Digital Electronic Circuits*. His research focuses on electromechanical systems, including MEMS, energy harvesters, and high-torque motors for the MIT Cheetah Robot.

univ_fac_mit_jeffrey_h_lang.gold: Jeffrey H. Lang is the Vitesse Professor of Electrical Engineering at MIT. An IEEE Fellow, he co-authored the textbook *Foundations of Analog and Digital Electronic Circuits*. His research focuses on electromechanical systems, MEMS, and actuators for the MIT Cheetah Robot. He earned his PhD from MIT in 1980 and was named a MacVicar Faculty Fellow in 2022.

univ_fac_mit_jae_s_lim.gold: Jae S. Lim is an MIT Professor of Electrical Engineering and Director of the Advanced Telecommunications Research Program. He played a pivotal role in developing the U.S. digital television standard as a member of the HDTV Grand Alliance. A 2020 Ho-Am Prize recipient, he authored *Two-Dimensional Signal and Image Processing* and holds over 40 patents.

univ_fac_mit_christina_delimitrou.gold: Christina Delimitrou is an Associate Professor at MIT and the KDD Career Development Professor. Her research focuses on computer architecture and cloud computing, specifically applying machine learning to datacenter resource management. A Sloan Research Fellow and NSF CAREER Award recipient, she previously taught at Cornell University and earned her PhD from Stanford University.

univ_fac_mit_alexander_rakhlin.gold: Alexander Rakhlin is a Professor at MIT in the Institute for Data, Systems, and Society and the Department of Brain and Cognitive Sciences. His research focuses on machine learning, online prediction, and statistical learning theory. A recipient of the NSF CAREER and COLT Best Paper awards, he earned his PhD from MIT in 2006.

univ_fac_mit_daniela_rus.gold: Daniela Rus is the first female Director of MIT’s Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL) and the Viterbi Professor of EECS. A MacArthur Fellow and member of the National Academy of Engineering, she specializes in robotics and AI. She co-founded Liquid AI and authored *The Heart and the Chip* and *The Mind’s Mirror*.

univ_fac_mit_anant_agarwal.gold: Anant Agarwal is an MIT professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science and the founder of edX. He previously directed MIT’s Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (CSAIL). A computer architecture pioneer, he co-founded Tilera Corporation and received the Maurice Wilkes Prize, the Padma Shri, and the Yidan Prize for Education Development.

univ_fac_mit_bruce_tidor.gold: Bruce Tidor is a Professor of Biological Engineering and Computer Science at MIT. He serves as Faculty Director of the MIT Hong Kong Innovation Node and was interim CEO of the Singapore-MIT Alliance for Research and Technology. A former Whitehead Fellow, Tidor founded MIT’s computational and systems biology graduate program and researches molecular modeling.

univ_fac_mit_priya_l_donti.gold: Priya L. Donti is the Silverman (1968) Family Career Development Professor and an Assistant Professor at MIT. She co-founded and chairs the nonprofit Climate Change AI. Her research focuses on machine learning for power grid optimization. She earned her PhD from Carnegie Mellon University and was named a 2021 MIT Technology Review Innovator Under 35.

univ_fac_mit_ronald_l_rivest.gold: An Institute Professor, Rivest is a co-inventor of the RSA public-key cryptosystem and a 2002 Turing Award recipient. He co-founded RSA Data Security and Peppercoin, and co-authored the standard textbook *Introduction to Algorithms*. His cryptographic designs include the MD5 hash function and the RC2, RC4, RC5, and RC6 symmetric ciphers.

univ_fac_mit_elfar_adalsteinsson.gold: Elfar Adalsteinsson is the Eaton-Peabody Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science and the Institute for Medical Engineering and Science at MIT. A principal investigator in the Research Laboratory of Electronics, he leads the Magnetic Resonance Imaging Group, focusing on fetal and placental MRI. He holds a Stanford PhD and is an AIMBE Fellow.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_vern_paxson.gold: Vern Paxson is a Professor of Computer Science at UC Berkeley and researcher at the International Computer Science Institute. He created the Zeek (formerly Bro) network monitoring system and the `flex` lexical analyzer. Recipient of the ACM Grace Murray Hopper and SIGCOMM Awards, he co-founded Corelight and chaired the Internet Research Task Force.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_jaijeet_s_roychowdh.gold: Jaijeet Roychowdhury is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. He specializes in electronic design automation, developing Oscillator Ising Machines (OIM) and the Berkeley Model and Algorithm Prototyping Platform (MAPP). A former Bell Laboratories researcher and University of Minnesota faculty member, he received the 2019 Bell Labs Prize.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_marti_a_hearst.gold: Marti A. Hearst is a professor and former Interim Dean of the UC Berkeley School of Information. She invented "Hearst patterns" for hyponymy discovery and developed the TextTiling algorithm. A pioneer in faceted navigation via the Flamenco project, she authored *Search User Interfaces* and served as President of the Association for Computational Linguistics.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_steven_m_conolly.gold: Steven M. Conolly is the Montford G. Cook Professor of Bioengineering and Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. A pioneer in Magnetic Particle Imaging (MPI), his lab developed the first MPI scanners in North America. He is an AIMBE Fellow and recipient of the 2022 Bakar Prize and Stanford’s Outstanding Inventor Award.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_matei_a_zaharia.gold: Matei Zaharia is an Associate Professor at UC Berkeley and the co-founder and CTO of Databricks. He created Apache Spark and co-developed MLflow, Delta Lake, and Apache Mesos. His honors include the 2025 ACM Prize in Computing, the SIGOPS Mark Weiser Award, and the ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_anthony_d_joseph.gold: Anthony D. Joseph is a Chancellor's Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley and Faculty Director of the Fung Institute for Engineering Leadership. He earned his Ph.D. from MIT. His research focuses on secure machine learning, genomics, and datacenters, contributing to major projects including Mesos, Tapestry, and the DETERlab cybersecurity testbed.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_shafi_goldwasser.gold: Shafi Goldwasser is the C. Lester Hogan Professor at UC Berkeley and former director of the Simons Institute. A 2012 Turing Award laureate, she co-invented zero-knowledge proofs and probabilistic encryption. Her foundational work in cryptography and complexity theory earned her two Gödel Prizes. She also holds professorships at MIT and the Weizmann Institute.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_jiantao_jiao.gold: Jiantao Jiao is a professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences and Statistics at UC Berkeley and Director of Research at NVIDIA. He co-founded the AI startup Nexusflow and co-directs Berkeley’s CLIMB center. A Stanford PhD and Tsinghua alumnus, his research focuses on generative AI, foundation models, reinforcement learning, and information theory.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_chunlei_liu.gold: Chunlei Liu is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences and Neuroscience at UC Berkeley and co-director of the Brain Imaging Center. He specializes in MRI technology and magnetogenetics, notably developing the FeRIC technique for remote neural modulation. He is a Fellow of the ISMRM and the American Institute for Medical and Biological Engineering.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_jelani_nelson.gold: Jelani Nelson is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley and a former Harvard faculty member. A specialist in sketching and streaming algorithms, he founded the AddisCoder and JamCoders programs. His honors include the ACM Eugene L. Lawler Award, the SIGACT Distinguished Service Prize, and the Presidential Early Career Award.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_christopher_w_fletc.gold: Christopher W. Fletcher is an Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. He earned his PhD from MIT, receiving the George M. Sprowls Award for his thesis on Oblivious RAM. Formerly a faculty member at UIUC, his research focuses on computer architecture and hardware security, including microarchitectural attacks and domain-specific acceleration.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_sylvia_ratnasamy.gold: Sylvia Ratnasamy is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Berkeley. She is a co-inventor of Distributed Hash Tables (DHTs) and the Content-Addressable Network (CAN). A recipient of the ACM Grace Murray Hopper Award and an ACM Fellow, she co-founded Nefeli Networks to commercialize her research in Network Functions Virtualization.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_ken_goldberg.gold: Ken Goldberg is the William S. Floyd Jr. Distinguished Chair in Engineering at UC Berkeley and Director of the AUTOLab. A pioneer in internet robotics, he created *The Telegarden* and co-founded the IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering. An IEEE Fellow and Chief Scientist of Ambi Robotics, he specializes in robot learning and automation.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_angjoo_kanazawa.gold: Angjoo Kanazawa is an Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley EECS, leading the KAIR lab. Her research in 3D computer vision and graphics includes the nerf.studio project. A 2023 Sloan Research Fellow and 2024 PAMI Young Researcher Award recipient, she also serves as an Amazon Scholar and advisor to Luma AI.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_kris_pister.gold: Kristofer S. J. Pister is the John R. Whinnery Chair in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. He pioneered "Smart Dust" and synthetic insects, specializing in MEMS and wireless sensor networks. He co-founded Dust Networks and co-directs the Berkeley Sensor and Actuator Center, receiving the 2009 ISA Alfred F. Sperry Founder Award.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_venkat_anantharam.gold: Venkat Anantharam is an IEEE Fellow and Professor at UC Berkeley specializing in information theory and stochastic control. He is a two-time recipient of the IEEE Information Theory Society Paper Award (2008, 2023). Previously a Cornell University faculty member, he earned his PhD and MA in mathematics from UC Berkeley.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_kannan_ramchandran.gold: Kannan Ramchandran is the Gilbert Henry Gates Endowed Chair at UC Berkeley. An IEEE Fellow and 2019 Padovani Lecturer, he received the 2017 IEEE Koji Kobayashi Computers and Communications Award for pioneering distributed source and storage coding. He founded the BASiCS Lab and previously held positions at UIUC and AT&T Bell Labs.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_james_f_o_brien.gold: James F. O'Brien is a computer graphics researcher who received a 2015 Academy Award for Technical Achievement for his pioneering work in destruction modeling. His simulation methods have been used in over 200 films and games, including *Avatar*. A Sloan Fellow and Georgia Tech alumnus, he co-founded Avametric and served as Chief Scientist at Pixelux.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_borivoje_nikolic.gold: Borivoje Nikolic is the National Semiconductor Distinguished Professor of Engineering at UC Berkeley. An IEEE Fellow, he co-authored the textbook *Digital Integrated Circuits: A Design Perspective*. His research focuses on energy-efficient digital and mixed-signal integrated circuits and VLSI implementation. He served as General Chair for the 2024 IEEE Symposium on VLSI Technology and Circuits.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_ion_stoica.gold: Ion Stoica is a computer science professor at UC Berkeley and director of the Sky Computing Lab. He co-founded Databricks, Anyscale, and Conviva. A co-creator of Apache Spark, Ray, and Apache Mesos, he is an ACM Fellow and recipient of the Mark Weiser Award. He previously directed Berkeley's AMPLab and RISELab.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_stuart_russell.gold: Stuart Russell is the Smith-Zadeh Chair in Engineering at UC Berkeley and director of the Center for Human-Compatible AI. He co-authored the standard textbook *Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach* and wrote *Human Compatible*. A Fellow of the Royal Society and OBE recipient, he developed a global seismic monitoring system for the nuclear-test-ban treaty.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_john_wawrzynek.gold: John Wawrzynek is a Professor and Division Chair of Computer Science at UC Berkeley. He co-directs the Berkeley Wireless Research Center and specializes in reconfigurable computing and computer architecture. A Caltech alumnus, he co-founded startups including BEECube, Bina Technologies, and Andes Networks. His research includes the development of the Chisel hardware construction language.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_prasad_raghavendra.gold: Prasad Raghavendra is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. He received the 2018 Michael and Sheila Held Prize for revolutionary contributions to optimization and complexity theory. His research focuses on semidefinite programming, the Unique Games Conjecture, and constraint satisfaction problems. He earned his PhD from the University of Washington in 2009.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_sewon_min.gold: Sewon Min is an assistant professor of EECS at the University of California, Berkeley, and a research scientist at the Allen Institute for AI. She earned her PhD from the University of Washington. Her research on large language models received the 2025 ACL Doctoral Dissertation Award and an ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award honorable mention.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_yakun_sophia_shao.gold: An Associate Professor at UC Berkeley, Yakun Sophia Shao specializes in computer architecture and hardware accelerators. Formerly a Senior Research Scientist at NVIDIA, she earned her Ph.D. from Harvard. Her honors include the 2024 Anita Borg Early Career Award, a Sloan Research Fellowship, an NSF CAREER Award, and the IEEE TCCA Young Computer Architect Award.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_ana_c_arias.gold: Ana C. Arias is a Professor and Chair of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. She specializes in flexible and printed electronics, developing wearable medical sensors and MRI coils. Previously, she managed research at PARC and led the semiconductor group at Plastic Logic. She is also a co-founder of Inkspace Imaging.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_alvin_cheung.gold: Alvin Cheung is a computer science professor at UC Berkeley and member of the Sky Computing Lab. He earned his PhD from MIT in 2015. Specializing in data management and program synthesis, he developed "verified lifting." He received the 2023 VLDB Early Career Award and the 2025 ACSIC Rock Star Award.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_jonathan_richard_sh.gold: Jonathan Richard Shewchuk created Triangle, a two-dimensional mesh generator that won the 2003 James Hardy Wilkinson Prize in Numerical Software. He earned his PhD from Carnegie Mellon University and authored the influential paper "An Introduction to the Conjugate Gradient Method Without the Agonizing Pain." He co-authored the textbook "Delaunay Mesh Generation."

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_alexei_a_efros.gold: Alexei A. Efros is a computer science professor at UC Berkeley known for pioneering data-driven approaches to computer vision and graphics. He received the 2016 ACM Prize in Computing and the 2026 SIGGRAPH Computer Graphics Achievement Award. His research on texture synthesis and image-to-image translation influenced Adobe Photoshop’s "Content-Aware Fill" and modern generative AI.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_yun_s_song.gold: Yun S. Song is a Professor of EECS and Statistics at UC Berkeley and Director of the Center for Computational Biology. He earned a Ph.D. from Stanford and B.S. degrees from MIT. A Packard and Sloan Fellow, he develops AI and statistical methods for genomics, notably DNA language models for predicting variant effects.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_laura_waller.gold: Laura Waller is the Charles A. Desoer Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley, where she directs the Computational Imaging Lab. She earned her BS, MEng, and PhD from MIT. A Packard Fellow and Max Planck–Humboldt Medalist, her research integrates optical hardware and algorithms for advanced microscopy and 3D phase imaging.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_avishay_tal.gold: Avishay Tal is an Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. He earned his PhD from the Weizmann Institute of Science in 2015. A 2022 Sloan Research Fellow, he is best known for proving the oracle separation of BQP and PH. His research focuses on computational complexity, pseudorandomness, and Boolean functions.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_murat_arcak.gold: Murat Arcak is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences and Mechanical Engineering at UC Berkeley. He specializes in control theory, multi-agent systems, and synthetic biology. An IEEE and IFAC Fellow, he received the Donald P. Eckman Award, the SIAM Control and Systems Theory Prize, and the Antonio Ruberti Young Researcher Prize.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_serina_chang.gold: Serina Chang is an Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley, jointly appointed in EECS and Computational Precision Health. She earned her PhD from Stanford University. Her research integrates AI and human behavior to address societal challenges, notably modeling COVID-19 spread in Nature. She was named to the 2026 Forbes 30 Under 30 Healthcare list.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_ali_m_niknejad.gold: Ali M. Niknejad is the Donald O. Pederson Distinguished Professor of EECS and faculty director of the Berkeley Wireless Research Center. An IEEE Fellow, he received the 2020 SIA/SRC University Research Award and the 2012 ASEE Terman Award. He specializes in RF, mm-wave, and THz integrated circuits for 5G communications and biomedical imaging.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_armando_fox.gold: Armando Fox serves as Faculty Advisor for Digital Learning Strategy. He co-authored the textbook Engineering Software as a Service and co-developed the university's first Massive Open Online Course. A recipient of the ACM Karl V. Karlstrom Outstanding Educator Award, he coined the term "Small Private Online Course" (SPOC) and helped design the Intel Pentium Pro.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_jacob_steinhardt.gold: Jacob Steinhardt is an Associate Professor of Statistics and EECS at UC Berkeley. He earned his PhD from Stanford University. A specialist in AI safety and alignment, he co-authored "Concrete Problems in AI Safety" and designed the MMLU benchmark. He is also the founder and CEO of the AI research lab Transluce.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_jitendra_malik.gold: Jitendra Malik is the Arthur J. Chick Professor of EECS at UC Berkeley. A pioneer in computer vision, he developed foundational techniques including anisotropic diffusion, normalized cuts, and R-CNN. He is a member of the National Academy of Sciences and National Academy of Engineering, and a 2026 Fellow of the Royal Society.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_prabal_dutta.gold: Prabal Dutta is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences specializing in low-power embedded systems and wireless sensor networks. A Sloan Research Fellow and NSF CAREER Award recipient, he received the 2022 ACM SenSys Test of Time Award. He co-founded several startups, including CubeWorks, nLine, and Gridware, and co-directs the CONIX Research Center.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_jaijeet_roychowdhur.gold: Jaijeet Roychowdhury is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley, where he earned his PhD in 1993. An IEEE Fellow, he co-founded Berkeley Design Automation and developed the Berkeley Model and Algorithm Prototyping Platform (MAPP). He received the 2019 Bell Labs Prize and 2023 Bakar Prize for Oscillator Ising Machines.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_joseph_e_gonzalez.gold: Joseph E. Gonzalez is an Associate Professor of Computer Science at UC Berkeley and co-director of the Sky Computing and RISE Labs. He co-founded Turi Inc. (acquired by Apple) and created the GraphX project for Apache Spark. His research focuses on machine learning systems, including large language models like Gorilla and vLLM.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_chun_lei_liu.gold: Chun-Lei Liu is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences and Neuroscience at UC Berkeley, where he co-directs the Brain Imaging Center. He specializes in MRI technology and magnetogenetics, notably developing the FeRIC technique for remote neuronal modulation. A Fellow of AIMBE and ISMRM, he previously served as faculty at Duke University.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_kathy_yelick.gold: Kathy Yelick is the Robert S. Pepper Distinguished Professor at UC Berkeley and Director of Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. Formerly the university’s Vice Chancellor for Research, she co-invented the Unified Parallel C and Titanium languages. A National Academy of Engineering member, she received the ACM/IEEE Ken Kennedy Award for contributions to parallel computing.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_claire_j_tomlin.gold: Claire J. Tomlin is the James and Katherine Lau Chair in Engineering and Chair of the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. A 2006 MacArthur Fellow and 2026 Richard E. Bellman Control Heritage Award recipient, she pioneered hybrid systems for air traffic control, collision avoidance, and unmanned aerial vehicles.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_eric_paulos.gold: Eric Paulos is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley, directing the Hybrid Ecologies Lab and the Jacobs Institute for Design Innovation. He coined the term "Urban Computing" in 2004 and developed early internet telepresence robots. A member of the ACM CHI Academy, he earned his PhD from UC Berkeley.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_rikky_muller.gold: Rikky Muller is an Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley, holding the S. Shankar Sastry Professorship. She co-founded Cortera Neurotechnologies and co-directs the Berkeley Wireless Research Center. An MIT Technology Review TR35 innovator, her research develops implantable neural interfaces and wireless microsystems for neurological applications.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_alane_suhr.gold: Alane Suhr is an Assistant Professor of Computer Science at UC Berkeley. She earned her PhD from Cornell University under Yoav Artzi. Her research focuses on natural language processing and situated interaction. She received an honorable mention for the 2022 ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award and paper awards at EMNLP, NAACL, and ACL.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_shankar_sastry.gold: S. Shankar Sastry is the Thomas M. Siebel Professor of Computer Science at UC Berkeley, where he served as Dean of Engineering (2007–2018). A member of the National Academy of Engineering, he previously directed DARPA’s Information Technology Office and CITRIS. He is also the founding chancellor of Plaksha University in India.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_dan_klein.gold: Dan Klein is a Professor of Computer Science at UC Berkeley and leader of the Berkeley Natural Language Processing Group. A specialist in statistical NLP and unsupervised learning, he received the 2006 ACM Grace Murray Hopper Award. He co-founded Semantic Machines, acquired by Microsoft, and has received the UC Berkeley Distinguished Teaching Award.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_max_willsey.gold: Max Willsey is an Assistant Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. He leads the development of the egg toolkit, which utilizes e-graphs for equality saturation and program optimization. He earned his PhD from the University of Washington in 2021 and received a Google Research Scholar Award and two Distinguished Paper awards.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_jennifer_listgarten.gold: Jennifer Listgarten holds the Jeffrey Huber and Angel Vossough Chancellor's Chair in Computational Biomedicine at UC Berkeley. A Chan Zuckerberg Investigator, she specializes in machine learning for protein engineering and CRISPR. She previously spent ten years at Microsoft Research and earned her PhD in computer science from the University of Toronto.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_aditya_g_parameswar.gold: An Associate Professor of EECS and co-director of the EPIC Data Lab, Parameswaran co-founded Ponder, which was acquired by Snowflake in 2023. His research in human-in-the-loop data analytics produced widely adopted tools including Modin and Lux. He is a recipient of the VLDB Early Career Research Contributions Award and a Sloan Research Fellowship.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_raluca_a_popa.gold: Raluca A. Popa is the head of security and privacy research at Google DeepMind and co-founder of the RISELab and SkyLab. She co-founded the security startups Opaque Systems and PreVeil. A recipient of the 2021 ACM Grace Murray Hopper Award, she earned her PhD from MIT, where she developed the CryptDB encrypted database system.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_koushik_sen.gold: Koushik Sen is a Professor of Computer Science at UC Berkeley. He pioneered concolic testing and Directed Automated Random Testing (DART). His research earned him the ACM SIGSOFT Impact Paper Award, a Sloan Research Fellowship, and the NSF CAREER Award. He holds a Ph.D. from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_trevor_darrell.gold: Trevor Darrell is the founding co-director of the Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research (BAIR) lab and Berkeley DeepDrive (BDD). He co-developed the Caffe deep-learning framework and received multiple Test of Time awards for his work in computer vision. Previously an MIT faculty member, he earned his PhD from MIT and BSE from Penn.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_shankar_s_sastry.gold: S. Shankar Sastry is the Thomas Siebel Distinguished Professor at UC Berkeley and former Dean of the College of Engineering (2008–2018). A member of the National Academy of Engineering, he is the founding chancellor of Plaksha University and previously directed DARPA’s Information Technology Office. His research focuses on robotics, control theory, and hybrid systems.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_ben_recht.gold: Ben Recht is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences and Statistics at UC Berkeley. He earned his PhD from the MIT Media Laboratory. A Presidential Early Career Award and 2015 William O. Baker Award recipient, he specializes in optimization and machine learning. He co-founded the Learning for Decision and Control conference.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_daniel_klein.gold: Daniel Klein is a Professor of Computer Science at UC Berkeley, specializing in natural language processing. He received the ACM Grace Murray Hopper Award for pioneering unsupervised grammar induction. A Marshall Scholar with a PhD from Stanford, he leads the Berkeley NLP Group and co-founded Semantic Machines, which was acquired by Microsoft in 2018.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_amy_pavel.gold: Amy Pavel is an Assistant Professor of EECS at UC Berkeley and a Visiting Faculty Researcher at Google Research. Her research focuses on human-computer interaction and accessibility. Previously a faculty member at UT Austin and researcher at Apple, she earned her PhD from UC Berkeley and received a Google ML and Systems Faculty Award.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_somayeh_sojoudi.gold: Somayeh Sojoudi is an Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences and Mechanical Engineering at UC Berkeley. She earned her PhD from Caltech and was named an IEEE Fellow in 2026. Her research focuses on optimization theory, machine learning, and control systems, earning her the NSF CAREER and ONR Young Investigator awards.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_ren_ng.gold: Ren Ng is a professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at UC Berkeley. He founded Lytro, Inc. to commercialize his Stanford PhD research on light field photography, which won the 2006 ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award. A 2026 National Geographic visionary changemaker, his research spans computational imaging, computer graphics, and vision science.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_irene_y_chen.gold: Irene Y. Chen is an Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley and UCSF in the Computational Precision Health program, with a joint appointment in EECS. She earned her PhD from MIT and an AB/SM from Harvard. Her research focuses on machine learning for healthcare equity and auditing algorithmic bias. She previously worked at Microsoft Research.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_bin_yu.gold: Bin Yu is the Chancellor’s Distinguished Professor of Statistics and EECS at UC Berkeley. A member of the National Academy of Sciences, she pioneered the PCS framework for veridical data science. She served as President of the Institute of Mathematical Statistics and received the 2023 COPSS Distinguished Achievement Award for her work in machine learning.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_katherine_a_yelick.gold: Katherine A. Yelick is the Director of Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory and the Robert S. Pepper Distinguished Professor at UC Berkeley. A member of the National Academy of Engineering and ACM Fellow, she previously served as Berkeley’s Vice Chancellor for Research. She co-invented the Unified Parallel C (UPC) and Titanium languages for parallel computing.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_stuart_j_russell.gold: Stuart J. Russell is the Smith-Zadeh Chair in Engineering at UC Berkeley and director of the Center for Human-Compatible AI. He co-authored the definitive textbook *Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach* with Peter Norvig. A Fellow of the Royal Society and OBE recipient, he delivered the 2021 BBC Reith Lectures and authored *Human Compatible*.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_matei_zaharia.gold: Matei Zaharia is an Associate Professor of Computer Science at UC Berkeley and the co-founder and CTO of Databricks. He created Apache Spark and co-developed widely used open-source software including MLflow and Delta Lake. His honors include the 2014 ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award and the 2025 ACM Prize in Computing.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_lijie_chen.gold: Lijie Chen is an Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley and a researcher at OpenAI. A theoretical computer scientist, he earned his Ph.D. from MIT and a bachelor’s from Tsinghua University’s Yao Class. He received Best Student Paper awards at STOC and FOCS in 2019 and won a gold medal at the International Olympiad in Informatics.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_kristofer_s_j_piste.gold: Kristofer S. J. Pister is a professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences known for inventing "Smart Dust," millimeter-scale wireless sensor networks. He founded Dust Networks and co-directs the Berkeley Sensor & Actuator Center. His research involves MEMS, microrobotics, and low-power circuits. He received the ISA Albert F. Sperry Founder Award and Alexander Schwarzkopf Prize.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_ali_javey.gold: Ali Javey is the Lam Research Distinguished Chair in Semiconductor Processing and a senior faculty scientist at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. He co-directs the Berkeley Sensor & Actuator Center. Known for developing wearable sweat sensors and flexible electronics, he received the 2020 Dan Maydan Prize and the 2015 MRS Outstanding Young Investigator Award.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_michael_lustig.gold: Michael (Miki) Lustig specializes in computational medical imaging, particularly compressed sensing for Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI). A co-founder of InkSpace Imaging, he received the ISMRM Gold Medal and is a Fellow of the International Society for Magnetic Resonance in Medicine. He earned his PhD from Stanford University and his B.Sc. from the Technion.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_benjamin_recht.gold: Benjamin Recht is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. He earned his PhD from the MIT Media Lab. A recipient of the PECASE and two NeurIPS Test of Time Awards, he co-founded the Conference on Learning for Decision and Control and is known for the "Hogwild!" algorithm for parallel optimization.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_jean_c_walrand.gold: Jean C. Walrand is a Professor Emeritus of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. An IEEE Fellow, he received the IEEE Koji Kobayashi Award and the ACM SIGMETRICS Achievement Award. His books include *An Introduction to Queueing Networks* and *High-Performance Communication Networks*. He co-founded Odyssia Systems and specializes in stochastic processes and communication networks.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_sayeef_s_salahuddin.gold: Sayeef S. Salahuddin is the TSMC Distinguished Professor at UC Berkeley. He is renowned for discovering the negative capacitance effect in ferroelectric materials for low-power computing. His honors include the Presidential Early Career Award (PECASE), the IEEE Andrew S. Grove Award, and the APS James C. McGroddy Prize for New Materials.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_jennifer_t_chayes.gold: Jennifer T. Chayes is Dean of the College of Computing, Data Science, and Society at UC Berkeley. A co-founder of the field of graphons and recipient of the John von Neumann Prize, she previously led Microsoft Research labs in New England, New York, and Montreal. She is a National Academy of Sciences member.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_jose_m_carmena.gold: Chancellor’s Professor of Electrical Engineering and Neuroscience, Carmena co-directs the Center for Neural Engineering and Prostheses. An IEEE Fellow, he specializes in brain-machine interfaces and neuroprosthetics. He co-developed "neural dust," an ultrasonic wireless technology for neural recording, and received the McKnight Technological Innovations in Neuroscience Award and a Sloan Research Fellowship.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_ana_claudia_arias.gold: Ana Claudia Arias chairs the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. An IEEE and National Academy of Inventors Fellow, she specializes in flexible electronics, notably developing flexible MRI coils for infants. She previously managed printed electronics at PARC and led the semiconductor group at the Cambridge spin-off Plastic Logic.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_jason_d_lee.gold: Jason D. Lee is an associate professor of EECS and Statistics at UC Berkeley. Formerly at Princeton and Google DeepMind, he earned his PhD from Stanford. His research focuses on machine learning theory and optimization. His honors include the Samsung AI Researcher of the Year award, an NSF CAREER Award, and a Sloan Research Fellowship.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_clark_t_c_nguyen.gold: Clark T.-C. Nguyen is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley and Executive Director of the Berkeley Sensor & Actuator Center. He pioneered MEMS-based timing, founded Discera, Inc., and served as a DARPA Program Manager. He is an IEEE Fellow and recipient of the IEEE Bosch MEMS Medal.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_alex_dimakis.gold: Alexandros G. Dimakis co-directs the National AI Institute for Foundations of Machine Learning (IFML) and co-founded BespokeLabs.ai. An IEEE Fellow and recipient of the 2018 James Massey Award, he specializes in generative AI, information theory, and coding for distributed storage. He was previously a professor at the University of Texas at Austin.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_alexandros_g_dimaki.gold: Alexandros G. Dimakis co-directs the National AI Institute for Foundations of Machine Learning (IFML) and co-founded BespokeLabs.ai. An IEEE Fellow, he received the James Massey Award and an NSF CAREER Award for research in deep generative models, information theory, and distributed storage. He was previously a professor at the University of Texas at Austin.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_satish_rao.gold: Satish Rao is a Professor of Computer Science at UC Berkeley. He earned his PhD from MIT in 1989 and was named an ACM Fellow in 2013. He received the 2012 Fulkerson Prize for developing approximation algorithms for the sparsest cut problem. His research specializes in combinatorial optimization, graph partitioning, and multicommodity flow.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_john_d_kubiatowicz.gold: John D. Kubiatowicz is a Professor of Computer Science at UC Berkeley. He led the OceanStore global storage project and co-developed the Tapestry peer-to-peer network and Tessellation operating system. A recipient of the Presidential Early Career Award (PECASE) and Scientific American 50, he also conducts research in quantum computing and manycore architectures.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_alexandre_m_bayen.gold: Alexandre M. Bayen is the Liao-Cho Innovation Endowed Chair and Professor of EECS and CEE at UC Berkeley. He serves as Director of CITRIS and the Banatao Institute and Associate Provost for the Berkeley Space Center. Known for the Mobile Millennium and MegaVanderTest projects, he received the 2010 PECASE and IEEE ITS Outstanding Research Award.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_umesh_v_vazirani.gold: Umesh V. Vazirani is the Roger A. Strauch Professor of EECS at UC Berkeley and director of the Berkeley Quantum Computation Center. A pioneer in quantum computing, he co-invented the Bernstein-Vazirani algorithm and co-authored the influential textbook *Algorithms*. He is a member of the National Academy of Sciences and a 2012 Fulkerson Prize recipient.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_nir_yosef.gold: Nir Yosef is an Associate Adjunct Professor of Computer Science at UC Berkeley and an Associate Professor at the Weizmann Institute of Science. He specializes in computational biology and systems immunology, developing models for gene expression regulation using single-cell genomics. He earned his PhD from Tel Aviv University and received a 2016 Okawa Research Grant.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_pieter_abbeel.gold: Pieter Abbeel is a UC Berkeley professor and Director of the Berkeley Robot Learning Lab. He pioneered deep reinforcement learning for robotics, enabling autonomous helicopter aerobatics and laundry folding. Abbeel co-founded Covariant and Gradescope and received the ACM Prize in Computing. He was a research scientist at OpenAI and hosts The Robot Brains podcast.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_emma_pierson.gold: Emma Pierson is the Zhang Family Endowed Professor of Computer Science at UC Berkeley. A Rhodes Scholar with a Stanford PhD, she develops machine learning methods to study healthcare and social inequality. Her research, published in Nature and Science, earned her an NSF CAREER Award and recognition as an MIT Technology Review Innovator Under 35.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_claire_jennifer_tom.gold: Claire Jennifer Tomlin is the James and Katherine Lau Chair in Engineering at UC Berkeley. A 2006 MacArthur Fellow, she specializes in hybrid systems, control theory, and unmanned aerial vehicles. Formerly a Stanford professor, she received the 2017 IEEE Transportation Technologies Award and 2024 IEEE Mildred Dresselhaus Medal for her contributions to aviation safety.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_anant_sahai.gold: Anant Sahai is the Qualcomm Chair Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. He earned his PhD from MIT in 2001. His research spans information theory, decentralized control, and machine learning, focusing on overparameterized models and cognitive radio. He is a former Treasurer of the IEEE Information Theory Society.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_alistair_sinclair.gold: Alistair Sinclair is the Kikuo Ogawa and Kaoru Ogawa Professor of Computer Science and Statistics at UC Berkeley. A specialist in randomized algorithms, he was founding associate director of the Simons Institute. He received the 1996 Gödel Prize and 2006 Fulkerson Prize for his work on Markov chains, approximation algorithms, and the permanent.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_bj_rn_hartmann.gold: Björn Hartmann is an Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley, holding the Paul and Judy Gray Alumni Presidential Chair. He co-founded the CITRIS Invention Lab and served as Faculty Director of the Jacobs Institute for Design Innovation. His Human-Computer Interaction research has received an NSF CAREER Award and a Sloan Research Fellowship.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_sergey_levine.gold: Sergey Levine is an Associate Professor at UC Berkeley and co-founder of Physical Intelligence. He earned his PhD from Stanford in 2014. A pioneer in deep reinforcement learning, he co-created the Soft Actor-Critic algorithm and led development of vision-language-action models like OpenVLA. His research focuses on autonomous agents and offline reinforcement learning.

univ_fac_mit_asuman_e_ozdaglar.gold: Asuman E. Ozdaglar is the MathWorks Professor and Head of the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT. She is also Deputy Dean of Academics for the Schwarzman College of Computing. Her research focuses on optimization, game theory, and network economics. She co-authored Convex Analysis and Optimization and is an IEEE Fellow.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_alyosha_a_efros.gold: Alexei Efros is a professor at UC Berkeley and member of the Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab. He received the 2016 ACM Prize in Computing for pioneering data-driven computer vision. His research includes CycleGAN for image translation and texture synthesis techniques that influenced Adobe’s content-aware fill. He was previously faculty at Carnegie Mellon.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_michel_m_maharbiz.gold: Michel M. Maharbiz is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. He co-invented "neural dust," an ultrasonic wireless interface for neural implants. His group developed the first remotely radio-controlled cyborg beetles, named one of Time’s Top 50 Inventions of 2009. He co-founded Microreactor Technologies, Inc. and is a Bakar Fellow.

univ_fac_ucsd_nuno_bandeira.gold: Nuno Bandeira is a Professor of Computer Science and Engineering and Pharmaceutical Sciences at UC San Diego. He serves as Executive Director of the Center for Computational Mass Spectrometry. His research focuses on bioinformatics and mass spectrometry, notably introducing the Spectral Networks and Shotgun Protein Sequencing paradigms. He received the 2006 HUPO Young Investigator Award.

univ_fac_ucsd_zhijian_liu.gold: Zhijian Liu is an Assistant Professor at UC San Diego directing the Z Lab and a Research Scientist at NVIDIA. He earned his PhD from MIT and a B.Eng. from Shanghai Jiao Tong University. Specializing in efficient AI, he developed TorchSparse and received the Qualcomm Innovation Fellowship and NVIDIA Graduate Fellowship.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_natacha_crooks.gold: Natacha Crooks is an Associate Professor of Computer Science at UC Berkeley and co-founder of the Berkeley Sky Computing Lab. Her research focuses on distributed systems, databases, and security. She received the ACM Dennis Ritchie Doctoral Dissertation Award, a Sloan Research Fellowship, and an NSF CAREER Award. She earned her PhD from UT Austin.

univ_fac_ucsd_deepak_kumar.gold: Deepak Kumar is an Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at the University of California, San Diego. He specializes in sociotechnical cybersecurity, focusing on online abuse and misinformation. He earned his PhD from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign and conducted postdoctoral research at Stanford. Kumar is also an award-winning playwright and composer.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_lining_yao.gold: Lining Yao is an Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley and director of the Morphing Matter Lab. Formerly a computer science faculty member at Carnegie Mellon University, she specializes in morphing materials and computational fabrication. An NSF CAREER Award recipient, her projects include the E-seed autonomous seed carrier. She earned her Ph.D. from MIT Media Lab.

univ_fac_ucsd_sylvia_l_herbert.gold: Sylvia L. Herbert is an Assistant Professor of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering and a Computer Science affiliate at UC San Diego, leading the Safe Autonomous Systems Lab. She earned her PhD from UC Berkeley. Recipient of the ONR Young Investigator Award and Hellman Fellowship, she specializes in safety-critical control, reachability analysis, and autonomous robotics.

univ_fac_ucsd_ravi_ramamoorthi.gold: Ravi Ramamoorthi is the Ronald L. Graham Professor of Computer Science at UC San Diego and founding director of the Center for Visual Computing. An ACM and IEEE Fellow, he received the ACM SIGGRAPH Significant New Researcher Award. His pioneering work on spherical harmonic lighting and neural radiance fields (NeRF) transformed computer graphics and vision.

univ_fac_ucsd_nadia_heninger.gold: Nadia Heninger is a computer science professor at UC San Diego and a 2026 IACR Fellow. She is renowned for developing the "cold boot attack" and identifying widespread cryptographic vulnerabilities, including the Logjam and DROWN attacks. Her research on RSA key reconstruction earned Test of Time awards from both USENIX Security and the IACR.

univ_fac_ucsd_jun_kun_wang.gold: Jun-Kun Wang is an Assistant Professor at the University of California, San Diego, with a joint appointment in the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering and the Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute. He specializes in optimization, machine learning, and game theory. Previously a Yale postdoctoral associate, he earned his Ph.D. in computer science from Georgia Tech.

univ_fac_ucsd_jingbo_shang.gold: Associate Professor at UC San Diego, jointly appointed in Computer Science and Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute. He earned his PhD from UIUC in 2019. His research in data mining and NLP focuses on "Extremely Weak Supervision." He received a 2023 NSF CAREER Award and the ACM SIGKDD Doctoral Dissertation Award runner-up.

univ_fac_ucsd_virginia_r_de_sa.gold: Virginia R. de Sa is a Professor of Cognitive Science and Interim Co-Director of the Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute at UC San Diego. Affiliated with the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, she specializes in machine learning, brain-computer interfaces, and computational neuroscience. She earned her PhD in computer science from the University of Rochester.

univ_fac_ucsd_biwei_huang.gold: Biwei Huang is an Assistant Professor at UC San Diego’s Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute. She earned her PhD from Carnegie Mellon University in 2022. Specializing in causal discovery and machine learning, she was an Apple Scholar in AI/ML. She co-organized the Conference on Causal Learning and Reasoning and won a CLeaR 2024 Best Paper Award.

univ_fac_ucsd_thomas_bewley.gold: Thomas Bewley is a Professor of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering and Affiliate Professor of Computer Science at UC San Diego. He directs the Flow Control and Coordinated Robotics Labs and authored the *Numerical Renaissance* and *Renaissance Robotics* textbook series. His research focuses on control theory, optimization, and robotics. He earned his PhD from Stanford.

univ_fac_ucsd_yian_ma.gold: Yian Ma is an assistant professor at the Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute and the Computer Science and Engineering Department at UC San Diego. He specializes in scalable Bayesian inference and uncertainty quantification for machine learning. Previously a postdoctoral fellow at UC Berkeley, he earned his PhD from the University of Washington and has received Google and Facebook Research Awards.

univ_fac_ucsd_alin_deutsch.gold: Deutsch is a Professor specializing in XML databases, data integration, and query optimization. He received the 2018 Alberto O. Mendelzon PODS Test-of-Time Award for his paper "The Chase Revisited." He earned his Ph.D. from the University of Pennsylvania in 2002 and received an NSF CAREER award for work on privacy-preserving database publishing.

univ_fac_ucsd_jishen_zhao.gold: Jishen Zhao is a Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego. Her research focuses on computer architecture and system software, emphasizing memory systems and machine learning co-design. Previously an assistant professor at UC Santa Cruz and HP Labs research scientist, she earned her Ph.D. from Pennsylvania State University.
