univ_fac_ucsd_adalbert_gerald_soosai_raj.gold: Adalbert Gerald Soosai Raj is an Associate Teaching Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego. He co-leads the Computing Education Research Lab, specializing in bilingual CS education and student-centered pedagogy. He holds a PhD from the University of Wisconsin-Madison and co-authored open-access interactive textbooks for Python and Java programming.

univ_fac_ucsd_jim_hollan.gold: James D. Hollan is a Distinguished Professor of Cognitive Science and Computer Science. He received the 2015 ACM SIGCHI Lifetime Research Award and was elected to the CHI Academy. He co-founded the UCSD Design Lab and the Distributed Cognition and HCI Lab, researching the cognitive consequences of computationally-based media and direct manipulation interfaces.

univ_fac_ucsd_bill_lin.gold: Bill Lin is a Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering and Associate Dean for Research at UC San Diego’s Jacobs School of Engineering. He served as ECE Department Chair from 2019 to 2024. A UC Berkeley Ph.D. graduate, his research focuses on VLSI architectures, wireless communications, and embedded systems. He previously led research at IMEC.

univ_fac_ucsd_yatish_turakhia.gold: Yatish Turakhia is an Assistant Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering at UC San Diego, holding a joint appointment in Computer Science. He earned his PhD from Stanford University. A 2023 MIT Technology Review Innovator Under 35, he developed the genomic tools UShER and RIPPLES to track SARS-CoV-2 variants and evolutionary dynamics.

univ_fac_ucsd_christian_dameff.gold: Christian Dameff, MD, MS, is an Associate Professor of Emergency Medicine, Biomedical Informatics, and Computer Science at UC San Diego. He serves as Medical Director of Cybersecurity for UC San Diego Health and co-directs the Center for Healthcare Cybersecurity. A 2025–2027 National Academy of Medicine ABEM Fellow, he co-founded the CyberMed Summit.

univ_fac_ucsd_andrew_b_kahng.gold: Andrew B. Kahng is a Distinguished Professor and Endowed Chair in High-Performance Computing. A pioneer in VLSI physical design and Design for Manufacturability (DFM), he co-founded Blaze DFM and received the 2019 Ho-Am Prize in Engineering. He is a Fellow of the ACM and IEEE and has led international semiconductor technology roadmapping.

univ_fac_ucsd_mikhail_belkin.gold: Mikhail Belkin is a Professor at UC San Diego’s Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute and Computer Science and Engineering department. An ACM Fellow, he is known for developing Laplacian Eigenmaps and the "double descent" risk curve. Previously at Ohio State University, he serves as editor-in-chief of the SIAM Journal on Mathematics of Data Science.

univ_fac_ucsd_steven_swanson.gold: Steven Swanson is a Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego, holding the Halicioğlu Endowed Chair in Memory Systems. He directs the Non-Volatile Systems Laboratory, researching computer architecture and non-volatile memory. A co-founder of the Non-Volatile Memories Workshop, he earned his PhD from the University of Washington in 2006.

univ_fac_ucsd_julian_j_mcauley.gold: Julian J. McAuley is a Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego. He specializes in recommender systems and personalized machine learning, authoring the textbook *Personalized Machine Learning*. A former Stanford postdoctoral researcher, he earned his Ph.D. from the Australian National University. His research analyzes massive social datasets from platforms like Amazon.

univ_fac_ucsd_lilly_irani.gold: Lilly Irani is an Associate Professor at UC San Diego, holding appointments in Communication and Computer Science. She co-founded Turkopticon, a digital labor activism tool, and authored *Chasing Innovation* (2019), which won the Diana Forsythe Prize. Formerly a Google designer, she earned a PhD from UC Irvine and computer science degrees from Stanford University.

univ_fac_ucsd_siavash_mirarab.gold: Siavash Mirarab is a Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering at UC San Diego. He earned his Ph.D. from UT Austin, receiving an ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award Honorable Mention. A Sloan Fellow and NSF CAREER awardee, he developed the widely used phylogenomic tools ASTRAL and PASTA for large-scale evolutionary analysis and microbiome profiling.

univ_fac_ucsd_imani_n_sherman.gold: Imani N. Sherman (now Imani Munyaka) is an Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego, where she leads the Ujima Security and Privacy Research Lab. She earned her Ph.D. from the University of Florida. Her research focuses on usable security and privacy for marginalized communities. She is a 2025 Hellman Fellow.

univ_fac_ucsd_tauhidur_rahman.gold: Tauhidur Rahman directs the MOSAIC Lab at UC San Diego, where he is an Assistant Professor in the Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute and CSE department. He specializes in mobile sensing and ubiquitous computing for digital health. Previously at UMass Amherst, he earned his PhD from Cornell and received a 2023 Google Research Scholar Award.

univ_fac_ucsd_earlence_fernandes.gold: Earlence Fernandes is an Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego. He earned his PhD from the University of Michigan in 2017. A recipient of the NSF CAREER and Google Research Scholar awards, his research on physical adversarial machine learning is featured in the Science Museum in London’s permanent collection.

univ_fac_ucsd_virginia_de_sa.gold: Virginia de Sa is a Professor of Cognitive Science and affiliate of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego. She is Interim Co-Director of the Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute and holds an endowed chair. Her research focuses on machine learning, visual perception, and brain-computer interfaces. She earned her PhD from the University of Rochester.

univ_fac_ucsd_scott_r_klemmer.gold: Scott R. Klemmer co-founded the UC San Diego Design Lab and leads the Interaction Design Specialization on Coursera. Previously an associate professor at Stanford, he co-directed their HCI Group. He is a recipient of the Sloan Fellowship and Katayanagi Emerging Leadership Prize; his former students include the founders of Instagram.

univ_fac_ucsd_hao_zhang.gold: Hao Zhang is an Assistant Professor at UC San Diego’s Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute and Department of Computer Science and Engineering. He cofounded LMSYS Org and developed influential machine learning systems including Vicuna, vLLM, and Alpa. He earned his PhD from Carnegie Mellon University and received the OSDI 2021 Jay Lepreau Best Paper Award.

univ_fac_ucsd_prithviraj_ammanabrolu.gold: Prithviraj Ammanabrolu is an Assistant Professor at UC San Diego, where he leads the PEARLS Lab. He is also a Senior Research Scientist at NVIDIA. Previously at the Allen Institute for AI and MosaicML, he earned his PhD from Georgia Tech. His research focuses on reinforcement learning, natural language processing, and human-AI collaboration.

univ_fac_ucsd_julian_mcauley.gold: Julian McAuley is a Professor of Computer Science at UC San Diego, specializing in recommender systems and personalization. An NSF CAREER award recipient, he authored *Personalized Machine Learning* and is known for analyzing massive datasets from Amazon and Yelp. He earned his Ph.D. from Australian National University and was a postdoctoral researcher at Stanford.

univ_fac_ucsd_daniele_micciancio.gold: Daniele Micciancio is an IACR Fellow and professor at UC San Diego specializing in lattice-based cryptography. He received FOCS Test of Time Awards in 2022 and 2024 for foundational work on cyclic lattices and worst-case to average-case reductions. He co-authored *Complexity of Lattice Problems* and co-developed the FHEW homomorphic encryption scheme.

univ_fac_ucsd_yuxiang_wang.gold: Yu-Xiang Wang is an Associate Professor at UC San Diego’s Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute and Department of Computer Science and Engineering. He directs the Scalable Statistical Machine Learning Lab and received an NSF CAREER Award. His research focuses on differential privacy, reinforcement learning, and statistical machine learning. He earned his PhD from Carnegie Mellon University.

univ_fac_ucsd_ramamohan_paturi.gold: Ramamohan Paturi, a professor of computer science at UC San Diego since 1986, is the Associate Dean for Artificial Intelligence Strategy and Implementation. He directs the Laboratory for Emerging Intelligence and focuses his research on algorithms and computational complexity. Paturi founded Parity Computing and initiated the university's Summer Program for Incoming Students.

univ_fac_ucsd_ndapandula_nakashole.gold: Ndapandula Nakashole is an Associate Professor of Computer Science at UC San Diego and Founder of Okalai AI. Her research focuses on natural language processing and machine learning. She earned her PhD from the Max Planck Institute for Informatics and received the NSF CAREER Award and the Otto Hahn Medal.

univ_fac_ucsd_tara_javidi.gold: Tara Javidi is the Jerzy Lewak Chair and Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering at UC San Diego, with an affiliate appointment in Computer Science. An IEEE Fellow and founding faculty of the Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute, she co-directs the Center for Machine-Integrated Computing and Security. Her research focuses on active learning and information theory.

univ_fac_ucsd_joe_gibbs_politz.gold: Joe Gibbs Politz is an Associate Teaching Professor at UC San Diego. He earned his PhD from Brown University and is a lead designer of the Pyret programming language. His work focuses on computer science education and programming languages, including contributions to the Bootstrap project and research on web security and compiler design.

univ_fac_ucsd_qi_rose_yu.gold: Qi Rose Yu is a computer scientist specializing in physics-guided deep learning and spatiotemporal machine learning. A recipient of the Presidential Early Career Award (PECASE) and DARPA Young Faculty Award, she was named a 2024 MIT Technology Review Innovator Under 35 and 2025 Samsung AI Researcher of the Year. She holds a PhD from USC.

univ_fac_ucsd_steven_p_dow.gold: Steven P. Dow is a Professor of Cognitive Science and Design at UC San Diego, where he directs the ProtoLab. His research focuses on human-computer interaction, social computing, and collective intelligence. A 2015 NSF CAREER Award recipient, he previously taught at Carnegie Mellon University and holds a PhD from the Georgia Institute of Technology.

univ_fac_ucsd_rajesh_gupta.gold: Rajesh Gupta is the founding dean of UCSD’s School of Computing, Information and Data Sciences and founding director of the Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute. A Distinguished Professor and Qualcomm Chair, he co-developed SystemC and SPARK. He is a Fellow of IEEE, ACM, and AAAS, and received the IEEE W. Wallace McDowell Award.

univ_fac_ucsd_nikolay_a_atanasov.gold: Nikolay A. Atanasov is an Associate Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering at UC San Diego and director of the Existential Robotics Laboratory. He earned his Ph.D. from the University of Pennsylvania in 2015. His robotics research received the 2023 IEEE RAS Early Academic Career Award and a 2021 NSF CAREER Award.

univ_fac_ucsd_alon_orlitsky.gold: Alon Orlitsky is the Qualcomm Professor of Information Theory and its Applications at UC San Diego and founding director of the Information Theory and Applications Center. An IEEE Fellow, he received the 2021 Claude E. Shannon Award and the IEEE W.R.G. Baker Award for his contributions to communication complexity, source coding, and probability estimation.

univ_fac_ucsd_mihir_bellare.gold: Mihir Bellare is the S. Gill Williamson Chancellor's Endowed Chair at UC San Diego. A pioneer of practice-oriented provable security, he co-developed the HMAC authentication algorithm and OAEP encryption standard. He received the 2009 ACM Paris Kanellakis Award and the 2019 Levchin Prize for his foundational contributions to real-world cryptographic protocols and standards.

univ_fac_ucsd_michael_thomas_tolley.gold: Michael Thomas Tolley is a Professor of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering at UC San Diego and director of the Bioinspired Robotics and Design Lab. His research focuses on bioinspired, soft, and origami-inspired robotics. An ONR Young Investigator, he earned his PhD from Cornell and was a postdoctoral fellow at Harvard’s Wyss Institute.

univ_fac_ucsd_yoav_freund.gold: Co-developer of the AdaBoost algorithm, Freund received the 2003 Gödel Prize and the 2004 Paris Kanellakis Award. An AAAI Fellow, he earned his PhD from UC Santa Cruz and co-authored *Boosting: Foundations and Algorithms*. His research focuses on computational learning theory and statistics, with applications in bioinformatics, computer vision, and high-performance computing.

univ_fac_ucsd_barna_saha.gold: Barna Saha is the Harry E. Gruber Endowed Chair Professor at UC San Diego, appointed in Computer Science and Engineering and the Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute. She directs the NSF EnCORE Institute. A recipient of the PECASE, Sloan Fellowship, and NSF CAREER Award, she previously held faculty positions at UC Berkeley and UMass Amherst.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_john_wright.gold: John Wright is an Assistant Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. He specializes in quantum computing and complexity theory. A 2026 Sloan Research Fellow, he received the FOCS 2019 Best Paper Award for his work on complexity class MIP* and quantum state tomography. He earned his PhD from Carnegie Mellon University.

univ_fac_ucsd_george_porter.gold: George Porter is a Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego and Co-Director of the Center for Networked Systems. He specializes in data center networking and sustainable systems. A recipient of the NSF CAREER Award, he earned his PhD from UC Berkeley and co-founded the startup inFocus Networks.

univ_fac_ucsd_amy_ousterhout.gold: Amy Ousterhout is an Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego, specializing in operating systems and datacenter networks. She earned her PhD from MIT and was a postdoctoral researcher at UC Berkeley. Her honors include the 2025 Google ML and Systems Junior Faculty Award and a Hertz Fellowship.

univ_fac_ucsd_henrik_iskov_christensen.gold: Henrik Iskov Christensen is the Qualcomm Chancellor's Chair of Robot Systems and Director of the Contextual Robotics Institute. He is the main editor of the US National Robotics Roadmap. Previously, he was the KUKA Chair of Robotics at Georgia Tech and founding director of its Institute for Robotics and Intelligent Machines. He co-founded Robust.AI.

univ_fac_ucsd_yeshaiahu_fainman.gold: Yeshaiahu Fainman is the ASML/Cymer Chair and a Distinguished Professor at the University of California, San Diego. A pioneer in nanophotonics and ultrafast optics, he developed thresholdless nanoscale coaxial lasers and silicon photonics for information technology. He is a Fellow of IEEE and SPIE, and recipient of the Dennis Gabor and Nick Holonyak Jr. Awards.

univ_fac_ucsd_deian_stefan.gold: Deian Stefan is an Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego. He earned his PhD from Stanford University. He co-developed RLBox, a sandboxing framework integrated into the Firefox browser to isolate third-party libraries. A 2022 Sloan Research Fellow and NSF CAREER awardee, he co-founded the web security startup Intrinsic.

univ_fac_ucsd_philip_jia_guo.gold: Philip Jia Guo is a Professor of Cognitive Science at UC San Diego. He created Python Tutor, a code visualization tool used by 25 million people, which received the ACM SIGCSE Test of Time award. He authored the memoir *The PhD Grind* and earned his PhD from Stanford University. His research focuses on computing education.

univ_fac_ucsd_sicun_gao.gold: Sicun Gao is an Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego. He developed dReal, an automated reasoning tool for verifying cyber-physical systems. A recipient of the NSF CAREER Award and Gödel Research Prize Silver Medal, he earned his Ph.D. from Carnegie Mellon University and was a postdoc at MIT.

univ_fac_ucsd_ben_bergen.gold: Benjamin Bergen is a Professor of Cognitive Science and Associate Dean for Research Initiatives at UC San Diego. He directs the Language and Cognition Lab, researching language comprehension and large language models. He authored *What the F: What Swearing Reveals About Our Language, Our Brains, and Ourselves* and *Louder Than Words: The New Science of Meaning*.

univ_fac_ucsd_rob_knight.gold: Rob Knight is the founding Director of the Center for Microbiome Innovation and a professor of computer science and pediatrics at UC San Diego. He co-founded the Earth Microbiome Project and American Gut Project. A National Academy of Engineering member, he developed QIIME and UniFrac and received the Massry Prize and NIH Pioneer Award.

univ_fac_ucsd_leon_bergen.gold: Leon Bergen is an Associate Professor of Linguistics and Computer Science at UC San Diego and Associate Director of the Language and Evolution Institute. He earned a Ph.D. in Brain and Cognitive Sciences from MIT and was a postdoctoral scholar at Stanford. His research focuses on computational linguistics, formal pragmatics, and noisy-channel models of communication.

univ_fac_ucsd_vikas_bansal.gold: Vikas Bansal is an Associate Professor of Pediatrics and affiliate faculty in Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego. He specializes in bioinformatics, developing computational algorithms for human genetic variation analysis. His notable contributions include the HapCUT and HapCUT2 tools for haplotype assembly and methods for variant calling using long-read sequencing data.

univ_fac_ucsd_trey_ideker.gold: Trey Ideker is a Professor of Medicine, Bioengineering, and Computer Science at UC San Diego and Director of Oxford’s Big Data Institute. A systems biology pioneer, he co-directs the Cancer Cell Map Initiative and leads the Cytoscape platform. He received the 2009 ISCB Overton Prize and is a Fellow of AAAS and ISCB.

univ_fac_ucsd_f_r_k_chung.gold: Fan Chung Graham is a Distinguished Professor of Mathematics and Computer Science at UC San Diego, holding the Paul Erdős Chair in Combinatorics. A National Academy of Sciences member, she is a leading expert in spectral graph theory and random graphs. She previously held positions at Bell Laboratories and the University of Pennsylvania.

univ_fac_ucsd_yuanyuan_shi.gold: Yuanyuan Shi is an Assistant Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering at UC San Diego. She earned her Ph.D. from the University of Washington and was a postdoctoral fellow at Caltech. Her research focuses on machine learning and control for energy systems. She received the NSF CAREER Award and Schmidt Sciences AI2050 Early Career Fellowship.

univ_fac_ucsd_soohyun_nam_liao.gold: Soohyun Nam Liao is an Assistant Teaching Professor at UC San Diego’s Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute. A UCSD PhD alumna, she previously served as teaching faculty at Princeton University. Her research in computer science education focuses on diversity and equity, notably developing the Basic Data Structures Concept Inventory (BDSI) to assess student conceptual understanding.

univ_fac_ucsd_terrence_j_sejnowski.gold: A pioneer in computational neuroscience, Sejnowski co-invented the Boltzmann machine with Geoffrey Hinton and developed NETtalk. He is the Francis Crick Professor at the Salk Institute, president of the NeurIPS Foundation, and founding editor of *Neural Computation*. A 2024 Brain Prize winner, he is elected to all four United States National Academies.

univ_fac_ucsd_daniel_m_kane.gold: A 2026 Gödel Prize recipient, Daniel M. Kane is a professor of computer science and mathematics. He is recognized for contributions to robust high-dimensional statistics and number theory. He was a four-time Putnam Fellow at MIT and earned his Ph.D. from Harvard University. He also received the 2007 Morgan Prize and two IMO gold medals.

univ_fac_ucsd_ranjit_jhala.gold: Ranjit Jhala is a Professor of Computer Science at UC San Diego. An ACM Fellow and UC Berkeley alumnus, he specializes in program verification and type systems. He co-developed the BLAST model checker and pioneered Liquid Types, leading to the creation of Liquid Haskell and Flux. He received the 2018 SIGPLAN Robin Milner Young Researcher Award.

univ_fac_ucsd_lilly_c_irani.gold: Lilly C. Irani is an Associate Professor of Communication and Computer Science at UC San Diego and Faculty Director of the UCSD Labor Center. She authored *Chasing Innovation*, which won the Diana Forsythe Prize. A former Google designer, she co-founded the labor activism tool Turkopticon and holds a PhD in Informatics from UC Irvine.

univ_fac_ucsd_arun_kumar.gold: Arun Kumar is an Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego and the Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute. He leads the ADA Lab, researching data management for machine learning. Recipient of the 2024 VLDB Early Career Award and an NSF CAREER Award, he co-founded RapidFire AI and earned his PhD at UW-Madison.

univ_fac_ucsd_samuel_r_buss.gold: Samuel R. Buss is a Professor of Mathematics and Computer Science at UC San Diego. A pioneer in bounded arithmetic and proof complexity, he authored the foundational *Bounded Arithmetic* (1986) and *3D Computer Graphics* (2003). He received the 2017 Bolzano Medal, delivered the 2019 Gödel Lecture, and created the widely used `bussproofs.sty` LaTeX package.

univ_fac_ucsd_xinyu_zhang.gold: Xinyu Zhang is a Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering and Computer Science at UC San Diego. He directs the Center for Wireless Communications and holds the Ericsson Endowed Chair. A 2023 ACM SIGMOBILE RockStar Award recipient, he earned his PhD in Computer Science and Engineering from the University of Michigan in 2012.

univ_fac_ucsd_lianhui_qin.gold: Lianhui Qin is an Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego. She earned her PhD from the University of Washington and was a postdoctoral researcher at the Allen Institute for AI. A 2021 Microsoft Research PhD Fellow and 2025 Amazon Research Award recipient, she specializes in natural language processing and machine reasoning.

univ_fac_ucsd_tajana_simunic.gold: Tajana Šimunić Rosing is a Distinguished Professor and Fratamico Endowed Chair at UC San Diego. An IEEE and ACM Fellow, she directs the System Energy Efficiency Lab and the $50.5 million PRISM Center. Her research in energy-efficient computing and cyber-physical systems earned her the 2022 SIA University Research Award for semiconductor design.

univ_fac_ucsd_shankar_subramaniam.gold: Shankar Subramaniam is a Distinguished Professor of Bioengineering and Computer Science & Engineering at UC San Diego, holding the Joan and Irwin Jacobs Endowed Chair. He founded the Bioinformatics Graduate Program and chaired the Department of Bioengineering. A pioneer in bioinformatics, he developed the Biology Workbench and is a Fellow of AAAS, IEEE, and AIMBE.

univ_fac_ucsd_yufei_ding.gold: Yufei Ding is an Associate Professor at UC San Diego and founder of the PICASSO Lab. Formerly at UC Santa Barbara, she earned her PhD from North Carolina State University in 2017. Her research focuses on compiler optimization, quantum computing, and machine learning. She is a recipient of the 2020 NSF CAREER Award.

univ_fac_ucsd_daniel_grier.gold: Daniel Grier is an Assistant Professor of Computer Science and Engineering and Mathematics at UC San Diego. Specializing in quantum complexity theory, he researches near-term quantum advantage and shallow quantum circuits. He earned his PhD from MIT under Scott Aaronson and was a postdoctoral fellow at the University of Waterloo’s Institute for Quantum Computing.

univ_fac_ucsd_xiaolong_wang.gold: Xiaolong Wang is an Associate Professor at UC San Diego and a Research Director at Meta Superintelligence Labs. He co-founded Assured Robot Intelligence and previously worked at NVIDIA. A recipient of the Sloan Research Fellowship and NSF CAREER Award, he earned his PhD from Carnegie Mellon University, specializing in computer vision and robotics.

univ_fac_ucsd_hovav_shacham.gold: Hovav Shacham is a computer scientist and professor at the University of Texas at Austin, formerly a faculty member at UC San Diego. He is best known for inventing return-oriented programming (ROP) and co-developing the Boneh–Lynn–Shacham (BLS) signature scheme. His research in applied cryptography and systems security has earned multiple Test-of-Time awards.

univ_fac_ucsd_babak_salimi.gold: Babak Salimi is an Assistant Professor at UC San Diego’s Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute. He earned his PhD from Carleton University and was a postdoctoral researcher at the University of Washington. His research in causal inference and algorithmic fairness earned a 2024 NSF CAREER Award and the 2019 SIGMOD Best Paper Award.

univ_fac_ucsd_tajana_simunic_rosing.gold: Tajana Simunic Rosing is a Professor and the Fratamico Endowed Chair at UC San Diego, where she directs the PRISM Center and System Energy Efficiency Lab. An IEEE and ACM Fellow, she received the 2022 SIA University Research Award. Her research focuses on energy-efficient computing, hyperdimensional computing, and power management for Systems-on-Chip.

univ_fac_ucsd_sam_buss.gold: Samuel R. Buss is a Distinguished Professor of Mathematics and Computer Science at UC San Diego. A Princeton Ph.D., he is known for his work in proof complexity and bounded arithmetic, authoring the book *Bounded Arithmetic*. He also wrote *3D Computer Graphics: A Mathematical Introduction with OpenGL* and was the 2019 ASL Gödel Lecturer.

univ_fac_ucsd_david_kirsh.gold: David Kirsh is a Professor of Cognitive Science and affiliated Computer Science faculty at UC San Diego. He directs the Interactive Cognition and Extended Reality Labs. An Oxford D.Phil and former President of the Academy of Neuroscience for Architecture, he specializes in embodied and distributed cognition, human-computer interaction, and the cognitive value of physical space.

univ_fac_ucsd_sam_lau.gold: Sam Lau is an Assistant Teaching Professor in the Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute at UC San Diego, where he earned his PhD in 2023. He authored the O’Reilly textbook *Learning Data Science* and created Pandas Tutor. Lau previously helped launch the flagship Data 8 and Data 100 courses at UC Berkeley.

univ_fac_ucsd_tajana_rosing.gold: Tajana Rosing is a Distinguished Professor and Fratamico Endowed Chair at UC San Diego. She directs the System Energy Efficiency Lab and the $50.5 million PRISM Center. An IEEE and ACM Fellow, she specializes in energy-efficient computing and cyber-physical systems. Previously, she was a research scientist at HP Labs and an engineer at Intel.

univ_fac_ucsd_aaron_schulman.gold: Aaron Schulman is an Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego. He earned his PhD from the University of Maryland, winning the ACM SIGCOMM Doctoral Dissertation Award. A 2023 NSF CAREER Award recipient, he researches the security and reliability of wireless networks, internet measurement, and critical infrastructure.

univ_fac_ucsd_steven_dow.gold: Steven Dow is a Professor of Cognitive Science and Computer Science at UC San Diego and director of the ProtoLab research group. A 2015 NSF CAREER Award recipient, he specializes in human-computer interaction, social computing, and collective intelligence. He co-founded the Design for San Diego initiative and previously held a faculty position at Carnegie Mellon.

univ_fac_ucsd_haojian_jin.gold: Haojian Jin is an Assistant Professor at UC San Diego’s Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute. He earned his PhD from Carnegie Mellon University, specializing in human-computer interaction and privacy. His work includes the Moderator and Peekaboo systems. He received the 2020 UbiComp Gaetano Borriello Outstanding Student Award and was named a 2025 Hellman Fellow.

univ_fac_ucsd_falko_kuester.gold: Falko Kuester is the Calit2 Professor for Visualization and Virtual Reality at UC San Diego, holding joint appointments in Computer Science and Structural Engineering. He directs the Cultural Heritage Engineering Initiative (CHEI), the DroneLab, and the Center of Interdisciplinary Science for Art, Architecture and Archaeology (CISA3), specializing in scientific visualization and digital heritage preservation.

univ_fac_ucsd_thomas_r_bewley.gold: Thomas R. Bewley is a professor of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering and affiliate faculty in Computer Science at UC San Diego. He directs the Flow Control and Coordinated Robotics Labs and authored the *Numerical Renaissance* and *Renaissance Robotics* textbooks. His research integrates control theory, fluid mechanics, and optimization for agile robotic systems.

univ_fac_ucsd_sorin_lerner.gold: Sorin Lerner is a professor and former chair of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego. His research focuses on programming languages and formal methods, including the Rhodium framework and the Quark browser. A recipient of an NSF CAREER Award, he became Dean of Cornell’s Bowers College of Computing and Information Science in 2025.

univ_fac_ucsd_vineet_bafna.gold: Vineet Bafna is a Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego. He earned his Ph.D. from Pennsylvania State University. A former director at Celera Genomics, he contributed to the Human Genome Project. He introduced the breakpoint graph for genome rearrangements and is an ACM and ISCB Fellow. He co-founded Boundless Bio.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_chang_ming_wu.gold: Ming C. Wu is the Nortel Distinguished Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at the University of California, Berkeley. He co-directs the Berkeley Sensor and Actuator Center and the Berkeley Emerging Technologies Research Center. His research focuses on optical MEMS, silicon photonics, and optofluidics. He co-founded Berkeley Lights, Inc. and OMM, Inc.

univ_fac_ucsd_nadir_weibel.gold: Nadir Weibel is a Professor of Computer Science and Engineering and Associate Faculty Director of the UCSD Design Lab. He directs the Human-centered eXtended Intelligence (HXI) Research Lab and is a Research Health Science Specialist at the VA San Diego Health System. He earned his PhD from ETH Zurich, focusing on human-computer interaction.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_bala_kameshwar_pool.gold: Bala Kameshwar Poolla is a researcher who served as a postdoctoral scholar at the University of California, Berkeley, within the Energy and Resources Group. He earned his Ph.D. from ETH Zurich in 2019. His research focuses on control theory applications for power grids and renewable energy systems. He is currently a research engineer at NREL.

univ_fac_ucsd_fan_r_k_chung.gold: Fan R. K. Chung is a Distinguished Professor of Mathematics and Computer Science at UC San Diego, holding the Paul Erdős Chair in Combinatorics. A leading authority on spectral graph theory and random graphs, she spent nineteen years at Bell Labs and Bellcore. She was elected to the National Academy of Sciences in 2024.

univ_fac_ucsd_taylor_berg_kirkpatrick.gold: Taylor Berg-Kirkpatrick is an Associate Professor specializing in unsupervised machine learning for natural language and music. He leads the BergLab, earned his PhD from UC Berkeley under Dan Klein, and previously taught at Carnegie Mellon University. He received a 2022 NSF CAREER award and the Qualcomm Innovation Fellowship for research on structured human data.

univ_fac_ucsd_shachar_lovett.gold: Shachar Lovett is a Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at UC San Diego. A Simons Investigator and NSF CAREER Award recipient, he specializes in computational complexity, coding theory, and additive combinatorics. He earned his PhD from the Weizmann Institute of Science and co-authored research that significantly improved the bounds for the sunflower lemma.

univ_fac_ucsd_tzu_mao_li.gold: An assistant professor at the University of California, San Diego, Li specializes in computer graphics and differentiable visual computing. He received the ACM SIGGRAPH 2020 Outstanding Doctoral Dissertation Award for his MIT PhD thesis. His research integrates programming systems and statistical learning, contributing to the Halide language and physically-based differentiable rendering.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_sandy_irani.gold: An ACM Fellow and Associate Director of the Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing, Sandy Irani specializes in quantum complexity theory and online algorithms. She previously chaired the Computer Science Department at UC Irvine and authored a widely used interactive textbook on discrete mathematics. She received her PhD from UC Berkeley in 1991.

univ_fac_ucsd_christine_j_alvarado.gold: Christine J. Alvarado is a Teaching Professor and Interim Associate Vice Chancellor of Education Innovation at UC San Diego. She holds the Paul R. Kube Chair and founded the Early Research Scholars Program (ERSP). An ACM Distinguished Member, she co-designed the AP Computer Science Principles curriculum and earned her PhD from MIT in 2004.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_richard_h_lathrop.gold: Richard H. Lathrop was a Professor Emeritus at UC Irvine who earned his PhD in artificial intelligence from MIT. A pioneer in bioinformatics, he focused on protein structure prediction and DNA self-assembly. He was named the 2009 UCI Professor of the Year and co-founded Arris Pharmaceutical Corp., serving as the ICS Honors Program director for 25 years.

univ_fac_ucsd_rajesh_k_gupta.gold: Rajesh K. Gupta is the founding dean of UC San Diego’s School of Computing, Information and Data Sciences and founding director of the Halıcıoğlu Data Science Institute. A Qualcomm Endowed Chair, he researches cyber-physical systems. He received the IEEE W. Wallace McDowell Award and is a Fellow of the IEEE, ACM, and AAAS.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_alexandru_nicolau.gold: Alexandru Nicolau is a Distinguished Professor and Chair of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. An IEEE Fellow and member of Academia Europaea, he earned his Ph.D. from Yale University. His research focuses on parallelizing compilers and embedded systems, notably developing the EXPRESS retargetable compiler and pioneering work in instruction-level parallelism.

univ_fac_ucsd_christine_alvarado.gold: Christine Alvarado is a Teaching Professor and Paul R. Kube Chair at UC San Diego, serving as Interim Associate Vice Chancellor of Education Innovation. An ACM Distinguished Member, she founded the Early Research Scholars Program and co-designed the AP Computer Science Principles curriculum. She holds an MIT PhD and previously taught at Harvey Mudd College.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_james_paul_dourish.gold: James Paul Dourish is the Steckler Endowed Chair and Chancellor’s Professor of Informatics at UC Irvine. A pioneer in human-computer interaction, he authored *Where the Action Is: The Foundations of Embodied Interaction*. He is an ACM Fellow and recipient of the 2025 SIGCHI Lifetime Research Award and multiple CSCW Lasting Impact Awards.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_yanning_shen.gold: Yanning Shen is an Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at UC Irvine. She earned her Ph.D. from the University of Minnesota in 2019. Specializing in trustworthy machine learning and network science, she received the 2025 NSF CAREER Award, 2022 MIT Technology Review Innovators Under 35 Asia Pacific, and Google Research Scholar Award.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_yunan_chen.gold: Yunan Chen is a Professor and Interim Chair of Informatics at the University of California, Irvine. An ACM Distinguished Member and FACMI, she chairs the Human-Computer Interaction Consortium. Her research focuses on HCI and health informatics. She earned her PhD from Drexel University and a Bachelor of Medicine from China Medical University.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_pierre_baldi.gold: Pierre Baldi is a Distinguished Professor of Computer Science at UC Irvine and director of its Institute for Genomics and Bioinformatics. A Caltech mathematics Ph.D., he authored *Deep Learning in Science* and *Bioinformatics: The Machine Learning Approach*. He is a Fellow of the ACM, IEEE, and AAAI, and received the 2023 Dennis Gabor Award.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_padhraic_smyth.gold: Padhraic Smyth is a Distinguished Professor and Hasso Plattner Endowed Chair in Artificial Intelligence at UC Irvine. A Fellow of the ACM, IEEE, AAAI, and AAAS, he received the ACM SIGKDD Innovation Award. He co-authored Principles of Data Mining and served as founding director of UCI’s Data Science Initiative and Center for Machine Learning.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_david_arthur_eppstein.gold: David Arthur Eppstein is a Distinguished Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. An ACM and AAAS Fellow, he specializes in computational geometry and graph algorithms. He authored *Forbidden Configurations in Discrete Geometry* and is a prominent Wikipedia administrator and arXiv moderator for the data structures and algorithms category.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_zhou_li.gold: Zhou Li is an Associate Professor of Computer Science at UC Irvine. Formerly a Principal Research Scientist at RSA Laboratories, he received an NSF CAREER Award and the 2024 IEEE Big Data Security Junior Research Award. His "TuDoor Attack" work was a 2024 Pwnie Award finalist. He holds a Ph.D. from Indiana University.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_mohsen_imani.gold: Mohsen Imani is an Associate Professor of Computer Science and director of the Bio-Inspired Architecture and Systems Laboratory (BIASLab). A pioneer in hyperdimensional computing, he received the 2023 DARPA, SRC, and ONR Young Faculty awards. He earned his PhD from UC San Diego and has authored over 250 publications and 20 US patents.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_paul_dourish.gold: Paul Dourish is the Steckler Endowed Chair of Information and Computer Science. An ACM and Royal Society of Edinburgh Fellow, he received the 2025 SIGCHI Lifetime Achievement in Research Award. He authored *Where the Action Is* and *The Stuff of Bits*, and directs the Steckler Center for Responsible, Ethical, and Accessible Technology.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_aparna_chandramowlish.gold: Aparna Chandramowlishwaran is an Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at UC Irvine, where she leads the HPC Forge lab. She earned her PhD from Georgia Tech in 2013. Her research focuses on high-performance computing and scientific machine learning. She received the 2010 Gordon Bell Prize and a 2018 NSF CAREER Award.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_alexander_v_veidenbau.gold: Alexander V. Veidenbaum is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine, specializing in computer architecture, compilers, and embedded systems. He earned his Ph.D. from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign in 1985. Affiliated with the Center for Embedded Computer Systems, he previously held faculty positions at UIUC and UIC.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_elaheh_bozorgzadeh.gold: Elaheh Bozorgzadeh is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. She earned her Ph.D. from UCLA in 2003. Her research focuses on design automation for reconfigurable computing, FPGA-based hardware acceleration, and embedded systems. She received the NSF CAREER Award in 2008 and the ACM SIGDA Technical Leadership Award in 2009.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_andr_van_der_hoek.gold: André van der Hoek is a Professor and Associate Dean at the University of California, Irvine, where he formerly chaired the Department of Informatics. An ACM Distinguished Scientist, he heads the Software Design and Collaboration Laboratory and co-authored *Software Design Decoded*. He designed UCI’s B.S. in Informatics and served as ICSE 2014 program co-chair.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_sharad_mehrotra.gold: Sharad Mehrotra is a Distinguished Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. He earned his Ph.D. from the University of Texas at Austin. An IEEE and ACM Fellow, he received the 2012 SIGMOD Test of Time Award for pioneering database-as-a-service and developed the Multimedia Analysis and Retrieval System (MARS).

univ_fac_uc_irvine_marco_levorato.gold: Marco Levorato is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. His research focuses on distributed edge computing, mobile AI, and wireless systems. He received the 2023 UCI Emerging Innovator of the Year Award, the 2019 Dean’s Mid-Career Research Award, and a 2012 IEEE GLOBECOM Best Paper Award. He holds a Ph.D. from the University of Padova.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_geoff_bowker.gold: Geoffrey C. Bowker is a Professor of Informatics at the University of California, Irvine, and Director of the Evoke Laboratory. A Fellow of the AAAS, he co-authored *Sorting Things Out: Classification and its Consequences* and wrote *Memory Practices in the Sciences*. His research examines information infrastructure and the social values embedded in technical systems.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_sam_malek.gold: Sam Malek is a Professor of Informatics at the University of California, Irvine, and Director of the Software Engineering and Analysis Laboratory. He earned his PhD from the University of Southern California. His research focuses on software architecture and mobile security. He received the 2020 ACM SIGSOFT Test-of-Time Award and a 2013 NSF CAREER Award.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_sangeetha_abdu_jyothi.gold: Sangeetha Abdu Jyothi is an Associate Professor at UC Irvine and lead of the NetSAIL lab. She earned her PhD from UIUC in 2019. Renowned for her research on internet resilience against solar superstorms, she received the 2026 ACM SIGMETRICS Rising Star Research Award and an NSF CAREER Award. She is also an Amazon Scholar.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_alexander_t_ihler.gold: Alexander T. Ihler is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. He earned his PhD from MIT and a BS from Caltech. His research focuses on graphical models and approximate inference. An NSF CAREER award recipient, he served as co-program chair for the 2016 Uncertainty in Artificial Intelligence (UAI) conference.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_wayne_b_hayes.gold: Wayne B. Hayes is Associate Professor of Computer Science at UC Irvine. He earned his Ph.D. from University of Toronto and held visiting fellowships at Oxford and Imperial College London. He developed SANA (Simulated Annealing Network Aligner) for biological network alignment. His research focuses on computational biology, dynamical systems, and cross-species protein function prediction.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_stacy_m_branham.gold: Stacy M. Branham is an Associate Professor of Informatics at the University of California, Irvine, specializing in human-computer interaction and accessible computing. Named one of Popular Science’s “Brilliant 10” in 2021, she received an NSF CAREER Award for her research on disability-inclusive technology. She is a Co-PI of AccessComputing and co-designed accessible Chromebook profile images.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_chen_li.gold: An IEEE Fellow and ACM Distinguished Member, Chen Li is a Professor of Computer Science at UC Irvine. He earned his Ph.D. from Stanford University. His research focuses on data management and analytics, notably as a co-PI of Apache AsterixDB and leader of the Texera project. He serves as treasurer of the VLDB Endowment.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_xiaohui_xie.gold: Xiaohui Xie is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. He earned his PhD from MIT and completed postdoctoral training at the Broad Institute. His research focuses on AI and computational genomics, notably developing deep-learning frameworks for single-cell gene regulation prediction published in Science Advances.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_geoffrey_c_bowker.gold: Geoffrey C. Bowker is a Chancellor’s Professor of Informatics at the University of California, Irvine, and director of the EVOKE Laboratory. An AAAS Fellow, he co-authored *Sorting Things Out: Classification and its Consequences*. His book *Memory Practices in the Sciences* won the Ludwig Fleck Prize. He previously held leadership roles at Santa Clara University.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_meenakshisundaram_gop.gold: Meenakshisundaram Gopi is a Professor of Computer Science and Associate Dean of Student Affairs at UC Irvine. Specializing in geometry, topology, and computer graphics, he co-founded Summit Technology Laboratories and received multiple Eurographics Best Paper awards. He earned his Ph.D. from UNC Chapel Hill and is a Senior Member of the IEEE.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_paul_pearce.gold: Paul Pearce is an Associate Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. He earned his PhD from UC Berkeley under Vern Paxson. His research focuses on network security, internet-scale measurement, cybercrime, and censorship. A 2022 DARPA Riser, he previously served as an assistant professor at Georgia Tech and a researcher at Facebook.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_thomas_y_yeh.gold: Thomas Y. Yeh is an Assistant Professor of Teaching in Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. He earned his Ph.D. from UCLA and a B.S. from UC Berkeley. His research focuses on computer science education, machine learning acceleration, and computer architecture. He previously taught at Pomona College and directs the Arcala Research Lab.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_cristina_v_lopes.gold: Cristina Videira Lopes is a Chancellor’s Professor at UC Irvine. A former Xerox PARC researcher, she co-founded Aspect-Oriented Programming and the AspectJ project. She is a core developer of the OpenSimulator virtual world platform and authored *Exercises in Programming Style*. An IEEE Fellow and ACM Distinguished Scientist, she received the 2016 Pizzigati Prize.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_sergio_gago_masague.gold: Sergio Gago-Masague is an Associate Professor of Teaching at UC Irvine, serving as Vice Chair for Professional Programs and Director of the Master of Computer Science program. He directs the ETAD Lab at Calit2 and is Associate Director of the Balsells Fellowship. He holds a D.Engr. from the Polytechnic University of Catalonia.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_eli_bozorgzadeh.gold: Elaheh Bozorgzadeh is a Professor of Computer Science at UC Irvine and a member of the Center for Embedded Computer Systems. She earned her Ph.D. from UCLA in 2003. Her research specializes in reconfigurable computing and FPGA-based hardware acceleration. She received the NSF CAREER Award in 2008 and an IEEE FPL Best Paper Award.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_joshua_garcia.gold: Joshua Garcia is an Associate Professor of Informatics at the University of California, Irvine, where he directs the Software Aurora (SORA) lab. He earned his PhD from the University of Southern California. A recipient of a 2025 NSF CAREER Award, his research focuses on automated software analysis, security, and testing for autonomous driving systems.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_daniel_s_hirschberg.gold: Daniel S. Hirschberg is a Professor Emeritus of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. He is best known for Hirschberg’s algorithm, a 1975 breakthrough that solves the longest common subsequence problem in linear space. His research includes parallel algorithms for connected components and length-limited prefix-free codes for data compression.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_gloria_mark.gold: Gloria Mark is Chancellor’s Professor of Informatics at the University of California, Irvine. She earned her PhD from Columbia University. A specialist in human-computer interaction and visiting researcher at Microsoft, she authored the book *Attention Span* (2023). Inducted into the ACM SIGCHI Academy, her research examines digital media’s impact on multitasking and stress.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_scott_jordan.gold: Scott Jordan is a Professor of Computer Science at UC Irvine who served as Chief Technologist of the Federal Communications Commission from 2014 to 2016. He earned his Ph.D. from UC Berkeley and was an IEEE Congressional Fellow. His research focuses on internet policy, including net neutrality, privacy, and resource allocation in communication networks.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_sven_koenig.gold: Sven Koenig is the Chancellor's Professor and Bren Chair at UC Irvine. A Fellow of AAAI, ACM, IEEE, and AAAS, he received the 2026 ACM/SIGAI Autonomous Agents Research Award for his contributions to AI planning and robotics. He previously served as a program director at the National Science Foundation and chair of ACM SIGAI.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_gene_tsudik.gold: Gene Tsudik is a Distinguished Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. A Guggenheim, ACM, IEEE, AAAS, and IFIP Fellow, he specializes in computer security, privacy, and applied cryptography. He received the ACM SIGSAC Outstanding Innovation Award and the Humboldt Research Award, and formerly edited ACM Transactions on Privacy and Security.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_lubomir_f_bic.gold: Lubomir F. Bic is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. Specializing in parallel and distributed computing, he led the MESSENGERS project on autonomous messages. He co-authored the textbook *Operating Systems Principles* and served as General Chair of the 20th International Symposium on Computer Architecture.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_jennifer_wong_ma.gold: Jennifer Wong-Ma is an Associate Professor of Teaching and Vice Chair of Undergraduate Studies at UC Irvine. She earned her PhD from UCLA and spent 12 years on the faculty at Stony Brook University. Her research focuses on computer science education and embedded systems. She advises the Women in Information and Computer Sciences organization.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_ian_g_harris.gold: Ian G. Harris is a Professor of Computer Science and Vice Chair of Undergraduate Education at the University of California, Irvine. His research focuses on natural language processing, hardware security, and design verification. He earned his PhD from UC San Diego and a BS from MIT. He is affiliated with the Cybersecurity Policy and Research Institute.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_james_a_jones.gold: James A. Jones is an Associate Professor of Informatics at the University of California, Irvine, leading the Spider Lab. He developed the Tarantula technique for spectra-based fault localization, receiving the 2015 ACM SIGSOFT Impact Paper Award. A recipient of the NSF CAREER Award, he earned his Ph.D. in Computer Science from Georgia Tech.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_alexander_ihler.gold: Alexander Ihler is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine, specializing in machine learning and graphical models. He earned his PhD from MIT and a BS from Caltech. A recipient of the NSF CAREER Award, he won first place in the 2012 and 2014 UAI Approximate Inference Challenges.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_vijay_v_vazirani.gold: Vijay V. Vazirani is a Distinguished Professor at UC Irvine known for foundational contributions to algorithm design and complexity theory. He co-authored the Valiant–Vazirani theorem and the Isolation lemma. He wrote the textbook *Approximation Algorithms* and received the 2022 John von Neumann Theory Prize for his work in algorithmic game theory and matching markets.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_yasser_shoukry.gold: Yasser Shoukry is an associate professor of electrical engineering and computer science at the University of California, Irvine, directing the Resilient Cyber-Physical Systems Lab. He earned his Ph.D. from UCLA in 2015. Shoukry received the 2021 IEEE TCCPS Early-Career Award and a 2019 NSF CAREER Award for his work on secure and safe cyber-physical systems.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_habiba_farrukh.gold: Habiba Farrukh is an Assistant Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine, leading the Security and Privacy of Emerging Computing Technology (SPECT) Lab. She earned her Ph.D. from Purdue University. A 2026 NSF CAREER Award recipient, she researches security and privacy for mobile, IoT, and mixed-reality platforms.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_michael_franz.gold: Michael Franz is a Distinguished Professor of Computer Science at UC Irvine. A pioneer in just-in-time compilation, he co-invented the trace compilation technology used in Mozilla’s Firefox browser. He received the ACM Chuck Thacker Breakthrough in Computing Award and is a Fellow of the ACM, IEEE, AAAS, and IFIP.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_aditi_majumder.gold: Majumder earned her PhD from UNC Chapel Hill and specializes in spatially augmented reality and multi-projector displays. A Professor of Computer Science at UC Irvine, she co-founded Summit Technology Laboratory and co-authored the textbook *Introduction to Visual Computing*. Her honors include the NSF CAREER Award and multiple IEEE best paper awards.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_nalini_venkatasubrama.gold: Nalini Venkatasubramanian is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine, where she co-directs the Center for Emergency Response Technologies. Her research focuses on distributed systems, adaptive middleware, and IoT for community resilience. An IEEE Fellow and NSF CAREER Award recipient, she holds a Ph.D. from the University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_brian_demsky.gold: Brian Demsky is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. A Hertz Foundation Fellow, he earned his Ph.D. from MIT. His research focuses on programming languages, compilers, and software reliability. He received an NSF CAREER Award and developed verification tools including C11Tester and Jaaru for testing concurrent data structures.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_daye_nam.gold: Daye Nam is an Assistant Professor of Informatics at the University of California, Irvine. She earned her PhD in Software Engineering from Carnegie Mellon University in 2024. Her research focuses on AI-powered developer tools and human-computer interaction. Previously a Research Scientist at Google, she received a Distinguished Paper Award at ICSE NIER 2026.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_gillian_r_hayes.gold: Gillian R. Hayes is the Vice Provost for Academic Personnel and a Chancellor’s Professor at UC Irvine, holding the Kleist Professorship in Informatics. An ACM SIGCHI Academy member, she specializes in human-computer interaction and assistive technologies. She previously served as Dean of the Graduate Division and earned her PhD from Georgia Institute of Technology.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_mohammad_abdullah_al_.gold: Mohammad Abdullah Al Faruque is the Conexant-Broadcom Endowed Chair Professor at UC Irvine, directing the Cyber-Physical Systems Lab and Autonomous Systems Initiatives. His research focuses on embedded and cyber-physical systems security. An IEEE CEDA Distinguished Lecturer, he received the 2016 Ernest S. Kuh Early Career Award and the Thomas Alva Edison Patent Award.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_sitao_huang.gold: An Assistant Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, Huang earned his Ph.D. from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign in 2021. His research focuses on hardware acceleration and high-level synthesis, including the PyLog flow. He received a 2025 NSF CAREER Award, a 2025 Amazon Research Award, and won the 2019 DAC System Design Contest.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_faisal_nawab.gold: Faisal Nawab is an Associate Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine, where he leads the EdgeLab. His research focuses on edge-cloud data management, blockchain, and distributed systems. He received the 2021 Facebook Next-Generation Data Infrastructure award and co-authored *Consensus in Data Management*. He holds a PhD from UC Santa Barbara.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_michael_j_carey.gold: Michael J. Carey is a Bren Professor at UC Irvine and a Consulting Architect at Couchbase. A member of the National Academy of Engineering, he received the ACM SIGMOD Edgar F. Codd Innovations Award. He previously held positions at IBM Research and BEA Systems and is a primary architect of the Apache AsterixDB system.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_kylie_peppler.gold: Kylie Peppler is a Professor of Informatics and Education at UC Irvine, where she directs the Creativity Labs. A member of the team that designed the Scratch programming platform, her research explores the intersection of arts and computational technologies. She is an NSF CAREER award recipient and co-director of the Connected Learning Lab.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_marios_c_papaefthymio.gold: Marios C. Papaefthymiou is the Dean of the Donald Bren School of Information and Computer Sciences at UC Irvine. Formerly chair of computer science and engineering at the University of Michigan, he co-founded Cyclos Semiconductor. An IEEE and National Academy of Inventors Fellow, he specializes in energy-efficient computing and holds a PhD from MIT.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_unnat_jain.gold: An Assistant Professor of Computer Science at UC Irvine, Unnat Jain researches embodied intelligence and robot learning. He earned his PhD from UIUC and previously held research roles at Meta’s FAIR Labs and Skild AI. His honors include the Director’s Gold Medal from IIT Kanpur and best paper awards at NeurIPS workshops.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_daniel_a_epstein.gold: Daniel A. Epstein is an Associate Professor of Informatics at the University of California, Irvine, where he directs the Personal Informatics Everyday (PIE) Lab. A human-computer interaction researcher, he earned his PhD from University of Washington. He received a 2023 NSF CAREER Award and a Ubicomp 10-Year Impact Award for the Lived Informatics Model.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_qi_alfred_chen.gold: Qi Alfred Chen is an Assistant Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. He earned his PhD from the University of Michigan. Specializing in autonomous vehicle and intelligent transportation security, he received an NSF CAREER Award and co-founded the Symposium on Vehicle Security and Privacy (VehicleSec).

univ_fac_uc_irvine_rina_dechter.gold: Rina Dechter is a Distinguished Professor of Computer Science at UC Irvine. A pioneer in automated reasoning and constraint processing, she received the 2025 IJCAI Research Excellence Award and the 2020 AIJ Classic Paper Award. She is an ACM, AAAI, and AAAS Fellow, and a member of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_alexander_c_berg.gold: Alexander C. Berg is an associate professor of computer science at UC Irvine. He earned his PhD from UC Berkeley. A computer vision researcher, he co-organized the ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge and co-authored the Single Shot MultiBox Detector (SSD) and Segment Anything (SAM) papers. He received the 2013 Marr Prize.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_michael_t_goodrich.gold: Michael T. Goodrich is a Distinguished Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. A Fellow of the ACM, IEEE, and AAAS, he is known for his research in algorithms, data structures, and computer security. He co-authored widely used textbooks, including *Data Structures and Algorithms in Java* and *Algorithm Design and Applications*.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_thomas_zimmermann.gold: Thomas Zimmermann is a Chancellor’s Professor and Bren Chair at UC Irvine. Previously a Senior Principal Researcher at Microsoft Research, he is an ACM, IEEE, and AAAS Fellow. He is recognized for pioneering the mining of software repositories and the SZZ algorithm. He serves as Co-Editor-in-Chief of the *Empirical Software Engineering* journal.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_sang_woo_jun.gold: Sang-Woo Jun is an Assistant Professor at the University of California, Irvine, specializing in hardware accelerators and near-storage computation. He earned his Ph.D. from MIT in 2018. His notable contributions include the GraFBoost graph analytics platform and the BlueDBM system, the latter of which was recognized in the ISCA@50 25-year Retrospective.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_david_eppstein.gold: David Eppstein is a Distinguished Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. An ACM and AAAS Fellow, he specializes in computational geometry and graph algorithms. He directs the Center for Algorithms and Theory of Computation and is a Wikipedia administrator and arXiv moderator. He authored Forbidden Configurations in Discrete Geometry.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_hengrui_cai.gold: Hengrui Cai is an Assistant Professor of Statistics at the University of California, Irvine’s Donald Bren School of Information and Computer Sciences. She earned her PhD from North Carolina State University. Her research focuses on causal inference, reinforcement learning, and large language model alignment. She has served as an Area Chair for NeurIPS 2026.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_tony_givargis.gold: Tony Givargis is the Chair of Computer Science at UC Irvine and Chief Technology Officer of Levyx Inc., which he co-founded. A specialist in embedded and cyber-physical systems, he co-authored the textbook *Embedded System Design: A Unified Hardware/Software Introduction* and holds 13 patents in storage and data management.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_cristina_videira_lope.gold: Cristina Videira Lopes is a Chancellor’s Professor of Informatics at the University of California, Irvine. A pioneer of aspect-oriented programming, she co-founded the AspectJ project at Xerox PARC. She is a core developer of the OpenSimulator virtual world platform and authored *Exercises in Programming Style*. Lopes is an IEEE Fellow and ACM Distinguished Scientist.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_stanislaw_jarecki.gold: Stanislaw Jarecki earned his Ph.D. from MIT in 2001 and was named a 2023 IACR Fellow for contributions to distributed cryptography. He specializes in secure multi-party computation and threshold cryptosystems. He co-developed the OPAQUE password-authenticated key exchange protocol, which was notably adopted to secure billions of WhatsApp users.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_melissa_mazmanian.gold: Melissa Mazmanian is a Professor and Chair of the Department of Informatics at the University of California, Irvine. She co-authored *Dreams of the Overworked* (2020) and researches how digital technologies shape work-life boundaries and organizational practices. She earned her PhD in Organization Studies from the MIT Sloan School of Management in 2009.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_michael_b_dillencourt.gold: Michael B. Dillencourt is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine, and a member of the Center for Algorithms and Theory of Computation. He earned his Ph.D. from the University of Maryland, College Park. His research focuses on computational geometry and graph theory, specifically Delaunay triangulations, inscribable polyhedra, and the GraphTool software.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_anne_marie_piper.gold: Anne Marie Piper is a Professor of Informatics at the University of California, Irvine, specializing in human-computer interaction and accessible computing. A recipient of an NSF CAREER Award and a Kavli Fellow, she directs the Master’s in Human-Computer Interaction and Design. She previously taught at Northwestern University and holds a PhD from UC San Diego.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_ioannis_panageas.gold: Ioannis Panageas is an Associate Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine, and director of the GOALLab. He earned his PhD from Georgia Tech and was a postdoctoral fellow at MIT. Formerly an Assistant Professor at SUTD, he received the 2019 NRF Fellowship for AI, focusing on multi-agent learning and optimization.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_m_gopi.gold: Gopi Meenakshisundaram (M. Gopi) is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine, where he served as Associate Dean of Student Affairs. He earned his PhD from UNC Chapel Hill. Specializing in geometry and topology for computer graphics, he co-founded Summit Technology Laboratories and received multiple Eurographics Best Paper Awards.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_iftekhar_ahmed.gold: Iftekhar Ahmed is an Associate Professor of Informatics at the University of California, Irvine, and Director of the Institute for Software Research. He leads the STAIRS group, researching software engineering, testing, and machine learning. He earned his PhD from Oregon State University and received an IBM PhD Fellowship for his work on mutation analysis.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_erik_b_sudderth.gold: Erik B. Sudderth is a Professor of Computer Science and Statistics at UC Irvine and Director of the Center for Machine Learning and Intelligent Systems. He earned his PhD from MIT and was an associate professor at Brown University. A recipient of the NSF CAREER Award and ISBA Mitchell Prize, he specializes in Bayesian nonparametrics.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_nikil_d_dutt.gold: Nikil D. Dutt is a Distinguished Professor of Computer Science, EECS, and Cognitive Sciences. An IEEE and ACM Fellow, he specializes in embedded systems, electronic design automation, and computer architecture. He served as Editor-in-Chief of ACM TODAES and received the IFIP Silver Core Award. His research includes foundational work on Architecture Description Languages.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_stephan_mandt.gold: Stephan Mandt is a Professor of Computer Science and Statistics at UC Irvine and Director of the UCI Center for Machine Learning. Formerly Head of Statistical Machine Learning at Disney Research, he is a Chan Zuckerberg Investigator and NSF CAREER awardee. His research focuses on generative AI, diffusion models, and neural compression.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_kylie_a_peppler.gold: Kylie A. Peppler is a Professor of Informatics and Education at the University of California, Irvine, where she directs the Creativity Labs. Her research explores the intersection of arts, computational technologies, and interest-driven learning. A recipient of an NSF CAREER Award, she is known for her work on e-textiles and the maker movement.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_bill_tomlinson.gold: Bill Tomlinson is a Professor of Informatics at the University of California, Irvine. He authored *Greening through IT* (2010) and researches ICT for sustainability and human-computer interaction. A recipient of the NSF CAREER award and a Sloan Research Fellow, he holds a Ph.D. from the MIT Media Lab and an MFA from CalArts.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_magda_el_zarki.gold: Magda El Zarki is a Professor Emerita of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine, where she directed the Center for Computer Games and Virtual Worlds. A Fulbright Scholar with a Ph.D. from Columbia University, she researches wireless networks and multimedia. She co-developed the educational game Sankofa and the VR project Elmina.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_ardalan_amiri_sani.gold: Ardalan Amiri Sani is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine, where he directs the Trustworthy Systems Lab (TrussLab). He earned his Ph.D. from Rice University in 2015. His research focuses on mobile computing, security, and operating systems, and he received the 2020 Android Security and PrIvacy REsearch (ASPIRE) Award.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_roy_fox.gold: Roy Fox is an Assistant Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine, where he directs the Intelligent Dynamics Lab. His research focuses on reinforcement learning, robotics, and information theory. He co-developed the RLlib library and previously conducted postdoctoral research at UC Berkeley. He earned his PhD from the Hebrew University of Jerusalem.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_alex_nicolau.gold: Alex Nicolau is a Distinguished Professor and former chair of the Department of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. An IEEE Fellow and member of Academia Europaea, he specializes in parallelizing compilers and high-performance computing. He is the Editor-in-Chief of the International Journal of Parallel Programming and earned his Ph.D. from Yale.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_michael_shindler.gold: Michael Shindler is an Associate Professor of Teaching in Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. He earned his BS from UC Irvine and a PhD from UCLA. Previously a faculty member at the University of Southern California, his research focuses on computing education, specifically student misconceptions in algorithms and dynamic programming.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_charless_c_fowlkes.gold: Specializing in computer vision and 3D scene understanding, Fowlkes is a recipient of the Helmholtz Prize, Marr Prize, and NSF CAREER Award. He earned his Ph.D. from UC Berkeley and a B.S. from Caltech. His research applies computational models to forensic science and biological image analysis, and he was named a UCI Chancellor's Fellow.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_sameer_singh.gold: Sameer Singh is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. Specializing in machine learning robustness and interpretability, he co-developed the LIME and CheckList frameworks. He received the ACL 2020 Best Paper Award, a 2021 NSF CAREER award, and is a 2023 Kavli Fellow. He earned his PhD from UMass Amherst.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_judy_hoffman.gold: Judy Hoffman is an Associate Professor specializing in computer vision and machine learning. She earned her PhD from UC Berkeley and was a research scientist at Facebook AI Research. Her honors include the 2024 ICML Test of Time Award, 2023 PAMI Young Researcher Award, and an NSF CAREER Award. She co-founded Women in Computer Vision.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_elena_agapie.gold: An Assistant Professor of Informatics, Agapie researches human-computer interaction and health informatics, focusing on technology-supported behavior change. She earned her PhD from the University of Washington and an MS from Harvard University. Her research, which includes work at Microsoft Research and NASA JPL, has received Best Paper awards at CHI and HCOMP.

univ_mit_gustavo_m_hobold.gold: Gustavo M. Hobold is the CTO and co-founder of Elementium Materials, an MIT spinoff developing advanced battery electrolytes. He earned his PhD from MIT in 2023, researching lithium metal anodes and solid-electrolyte interphase descriptors in the Gallant Lab. His work in *Nature Energy* identifies chemical markers to improve Coulombic efficiency in next-generation lithium batteries.

univ_mit_joseph_m_replogle.gold: Joseph M. Replogle is an MD-PhD researcher at MIT and the Whitehead Institute. He co-developed Perturb-seq and Perturb-FISH, advancing single-cell and spatial transcriptomics. He was co-first author of a landmark 2022 *Cell* study mapping human gene functions at genome scale. His work focuses on mitochondrial genome regulation and CRISPR-based functional genomics.

univ_mit_namit_chaudhary.gold: Namit Chaudhary earned his Ph.D. from MIT in 2022. In the Whitehead Lab, he developed branched-tail lipid nanoparticles for multiplexed mRNA delivery and in vivo gene editing. He co-authored a seminal review on mRNA vaccines in Nature Reviews Drug Discovery and currently researches lipid nanoparticle immunogenicity as a postdoctoral associate at Scripps Research.

univ_mit_h_c_hottel.gold: Hoyt Clarke Hottel was an MIT chemical engineering professor who chaired the university's Solar Energy Research Committee from 1938 to 1964. He co-developed the Hottel-Whillier-Bliss equation, the fundamental model for solar collector efficiency. Hottel pioneered solar thermal technology through the design of the first MIT Solar Houses and received the U.S. Medal for Merit.

univ_mit_anne_e_deconinck.gold: Anne E. Deconinck is an academic researcher at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, affiliated with the Harvard-MIT Division of Health Sciences and Technology and the Koch Institute. She is recognized for developing the first utrophin-dystrophin double-knockout mouse model for Duchenne muscular dystrophy. Her research investigates the molecular basis of muscle disorders and neuromuscular junction development.

univ_mit_s_h_autler.gold: Stanley H. Autler was a physicist at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology’s Radiation Laboratory and Lincoln Laboratory. He is best known for co-discovering the Autler-Townes effect (ac-Stark splitting) with Charles H. Townes, detailed in their 1955 paper "Stark Effect in Rapidly Varying Fields." He also researched microwave absorption and type II superconductors.

univ_mit_akshay_deshmukh.gold: Akshay Deshmukh is a Research Scientist at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology in the Lienhard Research Group. He earned his PhD from Yale University, specializing in energy-efficient desalination. A UKRI Future Leaders Fellow, his research focuses on membrane distillation, brine concentration, and sustainable metal extraction to reduce the energy intensity of industrial separations.

univ_mit_lisa_e_freed.gold: Dr. Lisa E. Freed is a Research Scientist at the MIT Media Lab’s Biomechatronics group and program manager for the neural interfaces team. She holds an MD from Harvard and a PhD from MIT. Her research in tissue engineering and cartilage regeneration includes pioneering experiments with Robert Langer and NASA on the Space Station Mir.

univ_mit_arthur_r_von_hippel.gold: MIT Institute Professor Arthur R. von Hippel founded the Laboratory for Insulation Research in 1940. A materials science pioneer, he discovered the ferroelectric properties of barium titanate and authored the seminal Dielectrics and Waves. He was the first recipient and namesake of the Materials Research Society’s highest honor, the von Hippel Award.

univ_mit_monty_krieger.gold: Monty Krieger is the Whitehead Professor of Biology at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology and a member of the National Academy of Sciences. He is best known for discovering the first high-density lipoprotein (HDL) receptor, SR-BI. A co-author of the textbook *Molecular Cell Biology*, his research focuses on scavenger receptors, cholesterol metabolism, and Hippo signaling.

univ_mit_sangbae_kim.gold: Sangbae Kim is a Professor of Mechanical Engineering at MIT and director of the Biomimetic Robotics Laboratory. He developed the gecko-inspired Stickybot, which utilized micro-structured directional adhesives and was named one of TIME’s Best Inventions of 2006. He also created the MIT Cheetah and received the NSF CAREER and Nagamori awards.

univ_mit_t_c_harman.gold: Theodore C. Harman was a researcher at MIT Lincoln Laboratory who developed the **Harman method**, the standard technique for measuring the thermoelectric figure of merit. He pioneered high-efficiency nanostructured materials, including quantum dot superlattices, and co-authored the foundational text *Thermoelectric and Thermomagnetic Effects and Applications* while collaborating with Mildred Dresselhaus.

univ_mit_tom_glaser.gold: Tom Glaser is an ocular genetics researcher who earned his PhD from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology. He is best known for identifying the PAX6 gene’s role in aniridia and congenital eye malformations. Currently a professor at UC Davis, he investigates the genetic basis of mammalian eye development and retinal cell fate specification.

univ_mit_h_brenner.gold: Howard Brenner (1929–2014) was the Willard Henry Dow Professor of Chemical Engineering at MIT. A pioneer in transport phenomena, he co-authored the seminal textbook *Low Reynolds Number Hydrodynamics*. He was elected to the National Academy of Sciences and received the APS Fluid Dynamics Prize and the ACS Award in Colloid or Surface Chemistry.

univ_uc_berkeley_steven_v_w_beckwith.gold: Steven V. W. Beckwith is a Professor of Astronomy Emeritus at UC Berkeley. He served as Director of the Space Telescope Science Institute (1998–2005), leading the Hubble Ultra Deep Field project, and Managing Director of the Max Planck Institute for Astronomy. His research focuses on star formation, circumstellar disks, and the origins of life.

univ_uc_berkeley_michael_bates.gold: Michael N. Bates is a Research Professor Emeritus of Epidemiology and Environmental Health Sciences at the University of California, Berkeley. He specializes in environmental and occupational epidemiology, investigating smoking cessation interventions such as the MassBUILT study for building trades workers. His research also includes systematic reviews identifying tobacco smoke as a risk factor for tuberculosis.

univ_mit_jonathan_huang.gold: Jonathan Huang is a computer science researcher known for co-authoring the influential papers "Deep Knowledge Tracing" and "Progressive Neural Architecture Search." Formerly a staff research scientist at Google Research, he is the Head of AI at Scaled Foundations. He earned his PhD from Carnegie Mellon University and has collaborated with MIT researchers on AI-driven environmental monitoring.

univ_uc_berkeley_james_n_smith.gold: James N. Smith is a Chancellor’s Professor of Chemistry at the University of California, Irvine, specializing in atmospheric chemistry and aerosols. Formerly a scientist at the National Center for Atmospheric Research, he developed the Thermal Desorption Chemical Ionization Mass Spectrometer (TDCIMS). He leads research on ultrafine particles for campaigns including SAIL and TRACER.

univ_uc_berkeley_erik_hesse.gold: Erik Hesse is a researcher at the University of California, Berkeley, specializing in attachment theory. He co-developed the Adult Attachment Interview (AAI) with Mary Main and is a leading expert on disorganized attachment and the "unresolved" state of mind. He serves as the Convenor of the Consortium of AAI Trainers.

univ_uc_berkeley_k_hurley.gold: Kevin Hurley is an astrophysicist at UC Berkeley’s Space Sciences Laboratory. He is the Principal Investigator of the 3rd Interplanetary Network (IPN), which localizes gamma-ray bursts using spacecraft triangulation. Hurley led the study of the 2004 giant flare from magnetar SGR 1806-20 and served as PI for the Ulysses GRB experiment.

univ_mit_ye_chen.gold: Ye Chen is a researcher at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology associated with the Darwin Project. Collaborating with Michael Follows and Rogier Braakman, Chen studies the metabolic evolution and self-organization of marine microbial ecosystems. This research focuses on how cellular energy flux drives phytoplankton population dynamics and organic carbon excretion in the ocean.

univ_mit_hyejin_kang.gold: Hyejin Kang is a neuroscientist who conducted research at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology as a postdoctoral fellow in Susumu Tonegawa’s lab. Her work focused on the molecular mechanisms of memory, specifically identifying the role of MAPK signaling and local protein synthesis in long-term synaptic plasticity. She previously earned her PhD from the California Institute of Technology.

univ_uc_berkeley_devin_coleman_derr.gold: Devin Coleman-Derr is an Adjunct Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley and a Research Leader at the USDA-ARS Plant Gene Expression Center. He directs the Coleman-Derr Lab, investigating how drought and heat stress restructure the bacterial and fungal microbiomes of crops like *Sorghum bicolor*. His research in *PNAS* explores microbial-mediated strategies for enhancing plant resilience.

univ_mit_a_l_onard.gold: Anthony Leonard is a prominent researcher in computational fluid dynamics, known for introducing the **Leonard stresses** in large-eddy simulations (LES). A **Theodore von Kármán Professor of Aeronautics, Emeritus** at Caltech, he pioneered **vortex methods** for incompressible flows and collaborated extensively with MIT researchers, including Steven Orszag, on early turbulence simulations.

univ_uc_berkeley_elwin_marg.gold: Elwin Marg (1918–2010) was a professor of physiological optics and optometry at the University of California, Berkeley.
