He co-invented the Mackay-Marg tonometer and was the first PhD graduate of Berkeley’s School of Optometry. He founded the Minerva Foundation, which promotes the study of neuroesthetics and the philosophical foundations of consciousness and decision-making.

univ_mit_yi_xiang_wang.gold: Yi-Xiang Wang is an academic researcher at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology specializing in nanoparticle-based drug delivery. His work focuses on developing advanced nanocarriers, such as polymeric and metal-organic nanoparticles, to enhance the targeted delivery and controlled release of therapeutic agents for cancer treatment and other biomedical applications.

univ_uc_berkeley_j_boguta.gold: John Joseph Boguta (1942–2020) was a physicist at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. He is best known for developing the Boguta-Bodmer model, which introduced nonlinear self-interactions into relativistic mean-field theory to describe nuclear matter and surface properties. He earned his Ph.D. from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign in 1968.

univ_uc_berkeley_jun_l.gold: Jun Lu is an ARC DECRA Fellow at Monash University and a visiting scholar at UC Berkeley. He earned his PhD from Monash in 2020. Specializing in metal-organic framework (MOF) synthesis for nanofluidics, he developed brain-like nanofluidic chips and published on metal ion sieving in *Nature Materials* and fluoride ion conduction in *Nature Communications*.

univ_uc_berkeley_eric_v_linder.gold: Eric V. Linder is a theoretical physicist at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory and the University of California, Berkeley. A member of the Supernova Cosmology Project, he developed the widely used $w_0-w_a$ parametrization for the dark energy equation of state. His research focuses on cosmic acceleration, dark energy dynamics, and testing theories of gravitation.

univ_uc_berkeley_g_william_skinner.gold: G. William Skinner (1925–2008) was a pioneering anthropological sinologist who developed the "physiographic macroregions" model and regional systems analysis of China. He authored the seminal "Marketing and Social Structure in Rural China" and served as President of the Association for Asian Studies. He was a National Academy of Sciences member and professor at Stanford and UC Davis.

univ_uc_berkeley_william_ryan.gold: William Ryan is an academic researcher at the University of California, Berkeley, specializing in Middle East and Rwanda conflicts. He has served as a research assistant for Chancellor’s Professor Emeritus David Collier in the Department of Political Science. His work examines political violence, electoral realignment, and the socio-political dynamics of international conflict zones.

univ_uc_berkeley_mx_tang.gold: Mary X. Tang is a researcher who conducted foundational work in gene delivery at the Joint Group in Bioengineering at UC Berkeley and UCSF. She is best known for developing cationic polymer systems, specifically demonstrating that degraded polyamidoamine (PAMAM) dendrimers significantly enhance gene transfer efficiency. She currently serves as Managing Director of the Stanford Nanofabrication Facility.

univ_uc_berkeley_luca_mastroberardino.gold: Luca Mastroberardino is a researcher formerly at the University of California, Berkeley. He co-identified the serum and glucocorticoid-regulated kinase (SGK) as a mediator of aldosterone-induced sodium transport via the epithelial sodium channel (ENaC). He also identified the E16 protein as a light chain of CD98, forming a complex that mediates L-type amino-acid transport.

univ_uc_berkeley_steven_w_m_crossley.gold: Steven W. M. Crossley is a postdoctoral researcher at the University of California, Berkeley, in the Christopher J. Chang laboratory. He earned his PhD from Scripps Research, specializing in transition metal catalysis. His research includes developing activity-based sensing tools like CarboSen for carbon dioxide and formate. He is a Damon Runyon Fellow and 2022 CAS Future Leader.

univ_ucsd_diana_dehaini.gold: Diana Dehaini is a nanoengineering researcher who earned her PhD from UC San Diego in 2019. Within the Liangfang Zhang Research Group, she developed biomimetic nanoparticles and cell membrane-coated delivery systems. She first-authored research on ultra-small lipid-polymer hybrid nanoparticles for tumor-penetrating drug delivery and published key reviews on bioinspired targeting strategies in nanomedicine.

univ_ucsd_robert_l_engler.gold: Robert L. Engler is a Professor of Medicine at the University of California, San Diego, and former Chief of Research Service at the VA San Diego Healthcare System. A pioneer in myocardial ischemia-reperfusion research, he identified the role of leukocytes in microvascular injury. He also directed the VA’s Coronary Care Unit and served as Chief Medical Advisor for Cardium Therapeutics.

univ_uc_berkeley_j_elkins.gold: John Elkins is an Emeritus Professor at the University of Queensland and a prominent researcher in inclusive education and literacy. He co-edited *Education for Inclusion and Diversity* and *Multiple Perspectives on Difficulties in Learning Literacy and Numeracy*. Inducted into the Reading Hall of Fame in 1996, he previously served as president of the Australian Reading Association.

univ_ucsd_david_arnold.gold: David Arnold is a researcher whose work focuses on early childhood development, specifically the reciprocal relations between **parenting behaviors** and **conduct disorder symptoms** in preschoolers. He has investigated the link between children's **social functioning** and **emergent academic skills**, and studied the **Community Attitudes Towards Mental Illness (CAMI) Scale** and **educational apps** for literacy.

univ_uc_berkeley_rohit_ramesh.gold: Rohit Ramesh is a researcher at the University of California, Berkeley, who earned his PhD in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences in 2024. Advised by Prabal Dutta in Lab11, his research focuses on automated reasoning for embedded design tools, including the development of Polymorphic Blocks and propagator semantics for hardware description languages.

univ_ucsd_r_l_miller.gold: Ronald L. Miller is a nuclear engineer who conducted research in the Fusion Energy Research Program at UC San Diego from 1994 to 2002. A member of the multi-institutional ARIES Team, he specialized in conceptual designs for stellarator and tokamak power plants. He previously spent nineteen years at Los Alamos National Laboratory.

univ_ucsd_ericka_l_anderson.gold: Ericka L. Anderson is a researcher and Program Coordinator at the University of California San Diego. She earned her PhD from MIT, where she identified the **Stra8** gene as a master regulator of **meiotic initiation** in mammals. Her postdoctoral work at UCSD focused on **neutrophil extracellular traps**, and she later conducted **microbiome** research at Human Longevity, Inc.

univ_ucsd_david_m_ward.gold: David M. Ward pioneered the use of 16S rRNA sequencing to investigate uncultured microorganisms in natural environments. His research involves metagenomic analysis of phototrophic microbial mats in Yellowstone National Park, specifically at Mushroom Spring and Octopus Spring, to characterize their diversity, functional genomics, and evolutionary lineages within the context of ecological and evolutionary processes.

univ_ucsd_justin_r_meyer.gold: Justin R. Meyer is an Associate Professor at UC San Diego specializing in bacteriophage coevolution and viral speciation, particularly using Phage lambda. His research, published in *Science* and *Nature*, includes developing "evolutionary training" protocols for phage therapy. He earned his PhD from Michigan State University and was a Systems Biology Fellow at Harvard Medical School.

univ_ucsd_p_w_langhoff.gold: Peter W. Langhoff is a researcher at the University of California San Diego specializing in quantum mechanics and non-Hermitian physics. He pioneered Stieltjes-Tchebycheff moment-theory techniques for molecular photoionization cross sections. His work includes developing atomic spectral methods for electronic energy surfaces and co-authoring a review on the Dirac variation of constants method.

univ_ucsd_stephen_l_foote.gold: Stephen L. Foote is a Professor of Psychiatry at the University of California, San Diego, and former Director of the NIMH Division of Neuroscience and Basic Behavioral Science. His research focuses on the locus coeruleus-norepinephrine system, investigating its role in stress, arousal, and vigilance, and the effects of corticotropin-releasing factor on noradrenergic neurons.

univ_ucsd_robert_m_lavinsky.gold: Robert M. Lavinsky is a researcher who earned his PhD from UC San Diego in 2000. Affiliated with the Howard Hughes Medical Institute, he co-authored seminal studies in Nature and PNAS characterizing nuclear receptor corepressors N-CoR and SMRT. His work detailed their roles in estrogen receptor signaling, tamoxifen resistance, and the discovery of SUN-CoR.

univ_ucsd_judy_e_davidson.gold: Judy E. Davidson is a nurse scientist at UC San Diego Health and the School of Medicine. She led the Society of Critical Care Medicine’s 2017 guidelines for family-centered care. A Fellow of the American Academy of Nursing, she developed the HEAR program for nurse suicide prevention, which received the Academy’s Edge Runner designation.

univ_ucsd_andrea_c_carrano.gold: Andrea C. Carrano is a researcher at the University of California San Diego and the Sanford Consortium for Regenerative Medicine. She specializes in E3 ubiquitin ligases, notably the Nedd4 family. She lead-authored a landmark *Nature* study on SKP2-mediated p27 degradation and co-authored research on longevity-regulating pathways involving the ligase WWP-1.

univ_ucsd_david_schairer.gold: David Otto Schairer, MD, is an Assistant Clinical Professor of Dermatology and Pediatrics at UC San Diego and Rady Children's Hospital. He specializes in nitric oxide-releasing nanoparticles for antimicrobial therapy and wound healing. His research explores the modulation of endothelin and prostanoids by nitric oxide to mitigate ischemia and reperfusion injury.

univ_ucsd_s_kondo.gold: Satoshi Kondo is a researcher at the University of California, San Diego, specializing in wireless communication networks. Collaborating with Laurence B. Milstein, he co-authored foundational research on multicarrier direct-sequence code-division multiple access (MC-DS-CDMA). His 1996 paper, "Performance of Multicarrier DS CDMA Systems," is a seminal work on network robustness against multipath fading and narrowband interference.

univ_ucsd_wei_ying.gold: Wei Ying is an Assistant Professor of Medicine at the University of California San Diego. His research investigates how microRNAs and immune cells regulate obesity-associated metabolic diseases. He notably identified miR-223 as a key mediator of macrophage activation and demonstrated that adipose tissue macrophage-derived exosomal miRNAs can modulate systemic insulin sensitivity.

univ_ucsd_k_seshadri.gold: Kalyanasundaram Seshadri is a Professor of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering at the University of California, San Diego. A Fellow of the Combustion Institute, he specializes in combustion science, asymptotic analysis, and flame structure. He co-developed the San Diego Mechanism and researches heat transfer in flames and the optimization of chemical kinetic models.

univ_ucsd_fleur_m_ferguson.gold: Fleur M. Ferguson is an Assistant Professor at the University of California San Diego. She earned her Ph.D. from the University of Cambridge and conducted postdoctoral research at Dana-Farber Cancer Institute. A 2026 Sloan Research Fellow and NIH New Innovator Award recipient, she specializes in proximity-mediated pharmacology and developed a chemoproteomic map of the degradable kinome.

univ_ucsd_michael_d_marger.gold: Michael D. Marger is a researcher at the University of California, San Diego, specializing in the phylogeny of membrane transport proteins. In the Saier Lab, he co-authored the seminal 1993 paper "A major facilitator superfamily," establishing the evolutionary unity of five distinct protein families, including drug resistance and sugar facilitators, into a single superfamily.

univ_ucsd_cuiling_zhong.gold: Cuiling Zhong is a researcher in the Department of Pathology at the University of California, San Diego. She co-authored a 2020 *Nature Communications* study identifying protein glycosylation inhibition as a pro-angiogenic strategy. Her research with Napoleone Ferrara includes developing heparin-binding VEGFR1 variants for intraocular neovascular disorders, published in *PNAS* in 2021.

univ_ucsd_alexandre_paix.gold: Alexandre Paix is a researcher at the University of California, San Diego, specializing in CRISPR-Cas9 genome engineering. He developed high-efficiency protocols for precision editing using ribonucleoprotein (RNP) complexes and short-homology linear repair templates. His work includes advancing endogenous tagging in *C. elegans* and the sea urchin *Lytechinus pictus* to study developmental biology and genetic inheritance.

univ_ucsd_robert_s_pomeroy.gold: Robert S. Pomeroy is a Teaching Professor of Chemistry and Biochemistry at UC San Diego and a researcher at the Scripps Institution of Oceanography. A co-founder of Algenesis, he develops biodegradable algae-based materials. His research focuses on analytical chemistry, marine plastic biodegradation, and the chemical metabolomics of coral reef ecosystems.

univ_uc_irvine_hazel_cheng.gold: Hazel Cheng is an academic researcher at the University of California, Irvine, specializing in the digestive system. She is renowned for co-developing the "Unitarian theory" of intestinal epithelial cell origins with C.P. Leblond. Her research focuses on intestinal stem cells, enteroendocrine cell renewal, and the molecular mechanisms of gastrointestinal health and malabsorption.

univ_uc_irvine_kwangbeom_hyun.gold: Kwangbeom Hyun is a postdoctoral researcher in the Marazzi Lab at the University of California, Irvine. His research focuses on the biochemical mechanisms of epigenetic regulation, including histone lysine methylation and chromatin remodeling. He authored the 2017 review "Writing, erasing and reading histone lysine methylations" and has published on the BAF complex and MLL family methyltransferases.

univ_uc_berkeley_michael_christ.gold: Michael Christ is a Professor Emeritus of Mathematics at the University of California, Berkeley. A specialist in harmonic analysis, partial differential equations, and complex variables, he is known for the Christ–Kiselev maximal inequality. His honors include the 1997 Stefan Bergman Prize and election as a Fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences.

univ_uc_irvine_karen_l_lindsay.gold: Karen L. Lindsay, MD, MMM, is a hepatologist and former Professor of Medicine at the Keck School of Medicine of USC. A prominent Hepatitis C researcher, she was a principal investigator in the landmark HALT-C Trial and lead author of the 2001 study comparing pegylated interferon alfa-2b to interferon alfa-2b for chronic infection.

univ_uc_irvine_alfred_kobsa.gold: Alfred Kobsa is a Professor Emeritus of Informatics and Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. He is the founding editor of *User Modeling and User-Adapted Interaction* and founding president of User Modeling Inc. His research specializes in privacy, personalization, and context-aware activity recognition systems, including IoT projects like TIPPERS.

univ_uc_irvine_gerardo_r_l_pez.gold: Gerardo R. López is a professor and researcher specializing in Critical Race Theory in education. He served as editor-in-chief of *Educational Administration Quarterly* and was the 2019–2020 president of the University Council for Educational Administration. His prominent works include *Persistent Inequality* and the seminal article "The (Racially Neutral) Politics of Education."

univ_uc_irvine_h_s_moyed.gold: Harris S. Moyed was the founding chairman of the Department of Microbiology and Molecular Genetics at the University of California, Irvine, and served as the university's Executive Vice Chancellor. He is best known for discovering the *hipA* gene in *Escherichia coli*, which established the genetic basis for bacterial persistence and antibiotic tolerance.

univ_uc_irvine_priyambada_nayak.gold: Priyambada Nayak is a Research Scientist at the University of California, Irvine, in the Department of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering. She holds a PhD from NIT Rourkela. Her research applies machine learning and image processing to 3D reconstruction of porous structures and the design of functionally graded parts for additive manufacturing.

univ_uc_irvine_craig_l_bennett.gold: Craig L. Bennett is a researcher at the University of California, Irvine, known for his foundational work in immunology. He identified mutations in the *FOXP3* gene as the cause of IPEX syndrome. His recent research in the La Spada Lab explores neuroimmunology, specifically the role of clonally expanded CD8 T cells in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.

univ_uc_irvine_a_e_blaugrund.gold: A. E. Blaugrund was a physicist at the University of California, Irvine, and the Weizmann Institute of Science. Specializing in plasma physics and high-power particle beams, he co-developed the modified elongated betatron accelerator (MEBA) and the stretched betatron with Norman Rostoker. He also conducted significant research on nuclear structure and nuclear level lifetimes.

univ_uc_irvine_dan_braithwaite.gold: Dan Braithwaite is an academic researcher and programmer at the University of California, Irvine’s Center for Hydrometeorology and Remote Sensing. He co-authored foundational research on the evolution and alignment of DNA polymerase sequences, particularly in bacteriophages. Braithwaite also contributed to the MG-RAST metagenomics platform and the development of the PERSIANN global precipitation dataset.

univ_uc_irvine_qin_yang.gold: Qin Yang is a Professor of Medicine and Chief of Endocrinology at UC Irvine, where he directs the Diabetes Center. He identified Serum Retinol Binding Protein 4 (RBP4) as a mediator of insulin resistance. His research also investigates PRMT1-mediated epigenetic regulation in leukemia and the role of NAD+ metabolism in cellular processes.

univ_uc_irvine_claudia_bird_schoonhoven.gold: Claudia Bird Schoonhoven is Professor Emerita of Organization and Strategy at the University of California, Irvine’s Paul Merage School of Business. A Fellow of the Academy of Management, she served as Editor-in-Chief of *Organization Science*. Her research focuses on technology-based firms and innovation, and she co-authored *The Innovation Marathon* and co-edited *The Entrepreneurship Dynamic*.

univ_uc_irvine_glenn_healey.gold: Glenn Healey is a Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine, and director of the Computer Vision Laboratory. An IEEE and SPIE Fellow, he specializes in multispectral and hyperspectral image processing, color vision, and physics-based modeling. He earned his Ph.D. in computer science from Stanford University.

univ_uc_irvine_zeev_n_kain.gold: Zeev N. Kain is a Chancellor’s Professor at UC Irvine and Director of the UCI Center on Stress & Health. An anesthesiologist and pediatrician, he developed the Yale Preoperative Anxiety Scale. Formerly Yale’s Chief of Children’s Anesthesia and UCI Health’s Chief Medical Officer, his research focuses on perioperative pediatric pain management and value-based care.

univ_ucsd_ian_a_mcgregor.gold: Ian McGregor is a nanoengineering researcher at the University of California San Diego. Affiliated with the Joseph Wang group, he co-authored clinical studies on microneedle biosensors for continuous lactate monitoring in critically ill patients. He also serves as a lead guide and facilitator for the university’s Outback Adventures outdoor leadership program.

oa_author_xiaochuan_ni.gold: Xiaochuan Ni is a researcher at Microsoft Research Asia specializing in sentiment analysis and transfer learning. He co-authored the influential paper "Cross-domain sentiment classification via spectral feature alignment" (WWW 2010). An alumnus of Shanghai Jiao Tong University, his research spans opinion mining, query understanding, and the application of large language models to user intent taxonomies.

oa_author_per_b__brockhoff.gold: Per B. Brockhoff is the Rector of the IT University of Copenhagen and former Head of DTU Compute at the Technical University of Denmark. A Professor of Statistics, he co-authored Statistics for Sensory and Consumer Science and developed the lmerTest R package for linear mixed effects models. His research includes sensometrics and Bayesian inference.

oa_author_a__patrcia_bento.gold: A. Patrícia Bento is a computational researcher at the European Bioinformatics Institute (EMBL-EBI) and a core contributor to the ChEMBL database. She co-authored the 2017 study "A comprehensive map of molecular drug targets" and developed open-source chemical structure curation pipelines using RDKit. Her research focuses on drug-target mapping, drug safety, and bioactivity informatics.

oa_author_mohamed_hamdi.gold: Mohamed Hamdi is a Professor at the École Supérieure des Communications de Tunis (Sup'Com), University of Carthage. He is the founding director of the Elgazala Innovation Center. His research specializes in network security and VANETs, notably co-authoring the highly cited "Survey on VANET security challenges and possible cryptographic solutions" (2014) in the journal Vehicular Communications.

oa_author_noel_gorelick.gold: Noel Gorelick is a co-founder of Google Earth Engine and serves as Google’s Chief Extraterrestrial Observer. He lead-authored the foundational 2017 paper on the platform and contributed to NASA’s Mars Global Surveyor and Mars Odyssey missions. His research focuses on planetary-scale geospatial analysis for environmental monitoring, drought detection, and global agricultural mapping.

univ_uc_irvine_marcus_seldin.gold: Marcus Seldin is an Associate Professor of Biological Chemistry and Endocrinology at University of California, Irvine, leading the Systems Genetics Lab. He utilizes systems genetics to investigate organ crosstalk and metabolic homeostasis. His research identifies the folate pathway and B vitamins as key epigenomic regulators in metabolic diseases. He earned a Ph.D. from Johns Hopkins.

oa_author_michael_mller.gold: Michael Müller is a researcher at Forschungszentrum Jülich’s Institute of Energy and Climate Research (IEK-2). Specializing in thermochemistry and surface modification, he investigates high-temperature materials, biomass gasification, and slag behavior. His work includes developing superhydrophobic coatings for energy applications and studying surface tension in coal ash slags to optimize industrial gasification processes.

oa_author_yasunobu_nohara.gold: Yasunobu Nohara is an Associate Professor at Kumamoto University’s Faculty of Advanced Science and Technology and leads the Data Science Laboratory. Specializing in Explainable AI (XAI) and medical informatics, he develops interpretability methods like feature packing and applies Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP) to clinical data, including delirium prediction at Saiseikai Kumamoto Hospital.

oa_author_shu_lih_oh.gold: Shu Lih Oh is an AI Scientist and researcher at Ngee Ann Polytechnic specializing in EEG, Brain-Computer Interfaces, and machine learning. Her work includes automated detection of ADHD, conduct disorder, and sleep stages. She holds a Master of Engineering from Nanyang Technological University and is a highly cited author in biomedical signal processing.

oa_author_connor_shorten.gold: Connor Shorten is a Deep Learning researcher at Florida Atlantic University and a Research Scientist at Weaviate. He founded Henry AI Labs, a YouTube channel dedicated to artificial intelligence research. Shorten is recognized for his influential publications on data augmentation and consistency learning, and he hosts the Weaviate Podcast, exploring vector databases and large language models.

univ_ucsd_charles_parker.gold: Charles J. Parker is a Professor of Medicine at the University of Utah and a leading expert on the complement system. He specializes in paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria (PNH), having authored the textbook *Membrane Defenses Against Attack by Complement and Perforins*. His research focuses on complement-mediated hemolysis and the clinical management of bone marrow failure syndromes.

oa_author_simon_j__shaw.gold: Simon J. Shaw is a researcher at the Novo Nordisk Foundation Center for Biosustainability (DTU Biosustain). He is a lead developer of **antiSMASH**, a premier genome mining tool for identifying secondary metabolite biosynthetic gene clusters. His work includes the **antiSMASH database** and **ActinoMation**, facilitating the computational annotation of bioactive compounds in microbial metabolomics.

oa_author_leslie_prez_cceres.gold: Leslie Pérez Cáceres is an Assistant Professor at the Pontificia Universidad Católica de Valparaíso. She earned her Ph.D. from the IRIDIA laboratory at the Université Libre de Bruxelles in 2017. A specialist in metaheuristics, she is a primary developer of the irace package, an R tool for the automatic configuration of optimization algorithms.

oa_author_chengen_wang.gold: Chengen Wang is a professor at Northeastern University specializing in digital transformation in industry and smart manufacturing. Affiliated with the State Key Laboratory of Synthetical Automation for Process Industries, his research focuses on Industry 4.0. He co-authored the highly cited "Internet of Things for Enterprise Systems of Modern Manufacturing" and holds a Ph.D. from Nanjing University of Aeronautics and Astronautics.

oa_author_ziming_feng.gold: Zi-Ming Feng is a computer science researcher at Shanghai Jiao Tong University. He specializes in object detection and image retrieval, notably co-developing **R3Det**, a refined single-stage detector for rotating objects. His research also extends to recommender systems, where he co-authored a comprehensive survey on retrieval methods and explored multi-channel fusion for personalized recommendations.

oa_author_ying_cheng.gold: Ying Cheng is a researcher at Beihang University’s School of Automation Science and Electrical Engineering and the Digital Twin International Research Center. Specializing in smart manufacturing, Cheng co-authored the seminal paper "Cloud Manufacturing: A New Manufacturing Paradigm." Their research focuses on digital twins, cyber-physical integration, and industrial software to advance industrial digital transformation.

oa_author_wenchun_ge.gold: Wen-Chun Ge is a professor at Jilin University’s College of Earth Sciences. Specializing in geochronology and geochemistry, Ge researches the tectonic evolution of Northeast China and the Paleo-Asian Ocean. Notable work includes zircon U-Pb-Hf isotope analyses of the Tayuan Complex and Mesozoic volcanic rocks, focusing on mantle-crust interactions and the petrogenesis of I-type granites.

imo_stephen_sanjaya.gold: Stephen Sanjaya is a mathematician who won Indonesia’s first-ever gold medal at the International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO) in 2013, ranking 10th globally. An alumnus of SMAK 1 BPK Penabur and Universitas Pelita Harapan, he also secured First Prize at the 2017 International Mathematics Competition (IMC) for University Students and won the 2017 national ONMIPA-PT.

imo_zipei_nie.gold: Zipei Nie is a mathematician who achieved a perfect score and gold medal at the 2010 International Mathematical Olympiad. A three-time Putnam Fellow, he earned his B.S. from MIT and a Ph.D. from Princeton. He previously served as a senior researcher at Huawei and is currently a J.L. Doob Research Assistant Professor at UIUC.

imo_kyrylo_myliarchyk.gold: Kyrylo Myliarchyk is a Ukrainian mathematician who earned a gold medal at the 2011 International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO) in Amsterdam. Representing Ukraine, he scored 28 points and achieved an individual rank of 39th among 563 contestants. He is recognized in the IMO Hall of Fame for his performance in the competition.

oa_author_david_f__steiner.gold: David F. Steiner is a Senior Clinical Research Scientist at Google Health and an Adjunct Clinical Assistant Professor at Stanford. He specializes in deep learning for computational pathology and radiology, notably developing AI for breast and prostate cancer diagnosis. He also researches human-AI collaboration and onboarding for medical practitioners, holding an MD/PhD from UCSF.

imo_shutaro_inoue.gold: Shutaro Inoue represented Japan at the 2010 International Mathematical Olympiad, winning a gold medal. A 2011 graduate of Nada High School, he achieved the highest score on the University of Tokyo's science entrance examination. He is a physician and assistant professor in the Department of Pediatrics at the University of Tokyo.

oa_author_xiaobai_liu.gold: Xiaobai Liu is an Associate Professor of Computer Science at San Diego State University and a former postdoctoral researcher at UCLA. His research focuses on computer vision and machine learning, specifically 3D pose estimation and scene parsing. He co-authored "The Role of Context for Object Detection" and developed the "SAM-whistle" model for bioacoustic signal detection.

univ_ucsd_r_a_moyer.gold: Richard A. Moyer is a biologist and researcher specializing in the phytochemical properties of fruits. He lead-authored the seminal 2002 study "Anthocyanins, Phenolics, and Antioxidant Capacity in Diverse Small Fruits: Vaccinium, Rubus, and Ribes," which has received over 1,500 citations. He is a professor of biology at King University.

imo_jetze_zoethout.gold: Jetze Zoethout is a Dutch mathematician who won a gold medal at the 2012 International Mathematical Olympiad. He earned his PhD from Utrecht University in 2022, specializing in computability models and topos theory. Formerly a lecturer at Utrecht, he transitioned to a career as a software engineer at Sogyo.

imo_bogdan_stankov.gold: Bogdan Stankov is a Bulgarian mathematician who won a gold medal at the 2012 International Mathematical Olympiad and a silver medal in 2011. He earned his PhD from École Normale Supérieure, Paris, in 2021. Specializing in discrete group geometry and random walks, he has conducted research at the Bulgarian Academy of Sciences and Lyon 1.

univ_uc_irvine_robert_r_redfield.gold: Robert R. Redfield III is the Interim Chair of Surgery and Chief of Transplantation Surgery at the University of California, Irvine. His research focuses on kidney and pancreas transplantation, including immunologic desensitization and graft survival. He previously served as a transplant surgeon at the University of Wisconsin and is the son of former CDC director Robert R. Redfield.

univ_fac_mit_regina_barzilay.gold: Regina Barzilay is the School of Engineering Distinguished Professor at MIT and AI Faculty Lead for the Jameel Clinic. A 2017 MacArthur Fellow, she received the inaugural 2021 AAAI Squirrel AI Award and the 2025 IEEE Frances E. Allen Medal. She developed the Mirai and Sybil deep learning models for cancer risk prediction.

imo_egor_voronetckii.gold: Egor Voronetckii is a Russian mathematician who won a gold medal at the 2013 International Mathematical Olympiad. A graduate of St. Petersburg State University, he co-solved the half-century-old "centrality of K2" problem in algebraic K-theory. He is a lecturer at the SPbSU Faculty of Mathematics and Computer Science and a former Russian President’s Prize recipient.

imo_mark_sellke.gold: Mark Sellke is an Assistant Professor of Statistics at Harvard University and a 2025 Sloan Research Fellow. A two-time IMO gold medalist (2013, 2014) and 2014 Putnam Fellow, he earned his PhD from Stanford. In 2024, he co-authored an OpenAI-assisted proof overturning a long-standing Erdős conjecture regarding unit distances.

univ_fac_mit_rajeev_j_ram.gold: Rajeev J. Ram is the Clarence J. LeBel Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at MIT and a MacVicar Faculty Fellow. He leads the Physical Optics and Electronics Group, specializing in photonics for communications and energy. A former ARPA-E Program Director, he developed the first semiconductor laser without population inversion and record-efficiency light sources.

univ_fac_ucsd_russell_impagliazzo.gold: Russell Impagliazzo is a professor at UC San Diego specializing in computational complexity. He is renowned for proposing the "five worlds" of complexity and co-formulating the Exponential Time Hypothesis. A member of the National Academy of Sciences, he co-authored the seminal proof that pseudorandom generators can be constructed from any one-way function.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_lubomir_bic.gold: Lubomir Bic is a Professor Emeritus of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. His research focuses on parallel and distributed computing, mobile agents, and networks. He led Project MESSENGERS and authored the textbooks *Operating Systems Principles* and *Operating Systems: A Spiral Approach*. He earned his Ph.D. from UC Irvine.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_eric_mjolsness.gold: Eric Mjolsness is a Professor of Computer Science and Mathematics at the University of California, Irvine. He earned his PhD from Caltech under John Hopfield. Formerly a Yale faculty member and NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory researcher, he led the NSF-funded "Computable Plant" project. He is a Fellow of the AAAS specializing in computational biology.

univ_fac_ucsd_julian_john_mcauley.gold: Julian John McAuley is a Professor of Computer Science at UC San Diego specializing in recommender systems. He authored the textbook *Personalized Machine Learning* and earned his Ph.D. from Australian National University. His research models human behavior using massive datasets from Amazon and Yelp, and includes generating step-charts for the video game *Dance Dance Revolution*.

univ_fac_uc_berkeley_prabal_k_dutta.gold: Prabal K. Dutta is a Professor of EECS at UC Berkeley, where he earned his PhD in 2009. A Sloan Research Fellow and NSF CAREER Award recipient, he won the 2022 ACM SenSys Test of Time Award. His research focuses on energy harvesting and embedded systems; he co-founded startups including CubeWorks and Gridware.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_kai_zheng.gold: Kai Zheng is a Professor of Informatics and Emergency Medicine at the University of California, Irvine, directing the Center for Biomedical Informatics. He also serves as Chief Research Information Officer. A Carnegie Mellon PhD graduate, he is a Fellow of the American College of Medical Informatics and received the 2011 AMIA New Investigator Award.

univ_fac_uc_irvine_alex_h_ihler.gold: Alex H. Ihler is a Professor of Computer Science at the University of California, Irvine. He specializes in machine learning and graphical models, focusing on approximate inference. He earned his PhD from MIT in 2005. Ihler received an NSF CAREER award, co-chaired the UAI 2016 conference, and won the 2014 UAI Approximate Inference Challenge.

univ_uc_irvine_n_b_patel.gold: Nilesh B. Patel is a neuroscientist who earned his PhD in physiology and biophysics from the University of California, Irvine in 1984. Under the mentorship of Mu-ming Poo, he researched neuromuscular junction development. He later became a professor and chairman of medical physiology at the University of Nairobi, specializing in behavioral neuroscience and pain.

univ_uc_irvine_xiaopeng_xu.gold: Xiaopeng Xu is a researcher specializing in fatigue and fracture mechanics. He has collaborated with Alan Needleman on numerical simulations of fast and dynamic crack growth in brittle solids. His research includes hyperelastic phase-field fracture modeling and multiscale frameworks for predicting fracture toughness in polycrystalline metals, often in collaboration with University of California, Irvine faculty.
